clean_body: | | 🚨BREAKING ELECTION ALERT: Proposition 50 PASSED -- and California’s 22nd District just became the most important seat in the nation in the fight for the House majority. Experts say CA-22 -- currently held by Republican David Valadao -- will now shift four points toward Democrats, instantly making it one of the most competitive congressional districts in the country. = This is huge. CA-22 already has the largest Democratic registration edge of any Republican-held seat in America. With Prop 50’s passage, the numbers are now unmistakably on our side. In a district where Valadao has survived by margins of just 2 to 5 points, a 4-point shift is enough to completely rewrite the path to victory. If Democrats organize, we can flip this seat -- and if we flip this seat, we flip the House. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = | = Republicans know exactly what’s at stake. Their super PACs are already preparing to pour MILLIONS into defending Valadao -- because they know if they lose CA-22, they lose their majority. But we have something stronger: a people-powered campaign led by Randy Villegas -- a son of the Central Valley and strong public supporter of Prop 50 and the demand for fair representation for our communities. = Randy isn’t running to appease Washington insiders or special interests. He’s building a movement to mobilize the vast Democratic majority right here at home -- and he’s doing it without a dime of corporate PAC money. Now comes the real work. This is day one of a new race -- and what we do in these first 24 hours will set the tone for everything that follows. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = \-- California Election Alert | With deep ties to our community and a lifelong commitment to justice and equity, Randy is running for Congress in California’s 22nd District to bring bold, compassionate leadership to Washington. name: Reagan Dunn
subject: We did it!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Reagan Dunn
body:
I’m deeply grateful for your support and confidence. | | https://us5.forward-to-friend.com/forward?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id;=4bf9aae350&e;=77eef70c61 | Is this email not displaying correctly?
[View it in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/16882c618a94/we-did-it?e=77eef70c61).
---|---
| [](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=3b779c8704&e=77eef70c61)
---
| | Friends,

Tonight, thanks to your incredible support, **we did it!** I'm honored to have been re-elected in a landslide victory to continue representing District 9 on the King County Council.

This victory is proof that voters want accountability in government, affordability and safety in our communities, and effective leadership.

I want to thank each of you for your support and confidence in the work to hold King County government accountable and provide high quality local services to the public. Whether it was volunteering to help get out the vote, endorsing my candidacy, donating to the campaign, walking with us in a parade, or just sharing a good word with your neighbor, this victory would not have been possible without your help.

I'll keep working hard every day to deliver results and make sure King County government serves the people -- not the other way around.

I take your vote of confidence very seriously. It is truly the honor of my lifetime to represent you on the King County Council.

With deep gratitude,
Reagan Dunn
King County Councilmember
---
| | [Follow on Twitter](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=98692aebdd&e=77eef70c61) | [ on Facebook](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=6f8bb5c832&e=77eef70c61) |
---

name: Alexandra Rojas
subject: re: Zohran Mamdani
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Breaking news, Peter:

Democratic socialist Zohran Mamdani just won his race against a corporate,
out-of-touch political dynasty to become the next mayor of New York City.

This is huge.

Zohran’s victory is the turning point that our progressive movement has
been working toward for years within the Democratic Party: electing
leaders with the moral courage to unite voters to take on Republican
authoritarianism, Democratic corporatism, and billionaire greed all at
once.

It proves what Justice Democrats has been saying since our founding:
Democratic primaries are the battleground for the control of our party’s
direction.

We can send leaders like Zohran — who share the same vision of improving
everyday folks’ lives by making the wealthiest pay their fair share — to
fight for us in Congress, and it starts with next year’s Democratic
primaries.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251104.oHiE6Y&refcode=em20251104-37166&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1002&akid=37166%2E1203867%2EwB8xJo ]Will you help us build our progressive, working-class movement and
elect more Zohrans to office in 2026 by pitching in a grassroots
contribution of $5 or more now?

For too long, wealthy donors and billionaire lobbies have demanded
cowardice and moral vagueness from our politicians. As a result,
Democratic voters have had entrenched corporate politicians like Andrew
Cuomo stuffed down their throats as the only possible alternative to
Republican representation.

But working-class voters are waking up to realize they are represented by
a party unwilling to stand up to the biggest bullies and robber barons of
our time — and they’re walking away. This is the failure of Party
leadership, and it is in large part why Democrats lost so badly last
November.

What party pundits and strategists should take away from Zohran’s win is
that voters want leaders who have the moral clarity to stand up for their
most urgent needs and are brave enough to take on the powerful
billionaires, lobbies, and corporations who are standing in the way of
their prosperity.

It is because Zohran took on the real estate corporations, the Israel
lobby, and the billionaires robbing everyone blind that voters saw a
candidate they could actually believe would fight for them — not in spite
of it.

His victory builds on the wins from members of Congress like Summer Lee,
AOC, Rashida Tlaib, Delia Ramirez, Ilhan Omar, and others who finally
offered Democratic voters an alternative choice to the same corporate,
career politicians that have ruled the Party for decades.

Zohran’s win provides a clear mandate: our party can and must embrace
moral courage as a deliberate political strategy. If it does, we won’t
just move the conversation — we'll realign political power itself and put
it back in the hands of the people instead of the millionaires,
billionaires, corporations, and lobby groups.

We cannot let this victory and momentum end in NYC,
Peter. It must be a signal to political
candidates across the country that we CAN take on the wealthiest, most
well-connected, most entrenched corporate politicians and win, if they’re
willing to be a voice that unites the working class against the wealthy
elites.

Now is the moment to build on our work to transform the Democratic Party
through Congressional primary elections nationwide. Justice Democrats has
been the leading organization waging a war for the future of our party in
primaries since 2017, and this cycle, we’re planning on taking on as many
do-nothing, corporate Democrats as possible.

The next electoral fight is the 2026 midterms, and at a time when the
Party has some of its lowest approvals ever, the only way for us to take
power back from a Republican trifecta is to run candidates that people
actually can believe in, see themselves in, and be inspired by. Candidates
like Donavan McKinney, Angela Gonzales-Torres, Justin J. Pearson, Cori
Bush, and more to come soon.

The era of Cuomo and Schumer, who have controlled Democratic politics for
decades, is over. Voters want new leaders, with ideas bold enough to match
the scale of their crises, and the path to electing them is in Democratic
primaries across the country, where Justice Democrats is leading the
charge.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251104.oHiE6Y&refcode=em20251104-37166&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=37166%2E1203867%2EwB8xJo ]But just like it took a whole movement to defeat Andrew Cuomo, it's
going to take a groundswell of grassroots resources to defeat the rest of
the billionaire-backed politicians in the Democratic Party. Let’s multiply
this victory and keep building an unstoppable movement: chip in whatever
you can today to directly fuel our work this cycle.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251104.oHiE6Y&refcode=em20251104-37166&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1022&akid=37166%2E1203867%2EwB8xJo ]Contribute $5

Our only path to real change, in the face of a full corporate takeover of
our country, is to take on the billionaire class head-on and with our full
force. Thank you for anything you can do to stand alongside us in this
fight.

In solidarity,

Alexandra Rojas
Executive Director

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20251104-37166-footer&t=1023&refcode2=37166_1203867_wB8xJo&akid=37166%2E1203867%2EwB8xJo

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1024&akid=37166%2E1203867%2EwB8xJo

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

name: Troy Downing
subject: These Warriors Fought for America. Now They Need YOU.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
My fellow veterans need your support.

| | |

This is Congressman Troy Downing, Combat Veteran and proud conservative patriot. I’m reaching out with an urgent request – because the fight for America doesn’t end on the battlefield. It continues in the halls of Congress, and that’s where we need YOU.

Right now, six of my fellow warriors, also Freshman GOP Congressmen, are on the front lines of a different kind of battle: defending our Constitution, securing our borders, and standing up for American values against the radical Left.

These leaders don’t just talk the talk – they’ve served, sacrificed, and stood in the gap for this nation. But they can’t do it alone.
---
[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)
---
**[Will you chip in today and SPLIT a donation between these America First Veteran Congressmen to help them start their first term strong?](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)**
---
[ SPLIT A DONATION ](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)
---
The Left has deep pockets filled with special interest money. But we have something stronger: patriots like you who believe in faith, freedom, and the future of our country.

Your contribution isn't just a donation – it's an investment in leadership forged by service and tested under fire.

**[Let’s send a message that America’s warriors don’t back down – on the battlefield OR in Congress.](https://trk.emails.troydowning.-ul1euhoXYkTMw)**
---
[ SPLIT A DONATION ](https://trk.emails.troydowning.__susw1IeQ)
---
For America,

Congressman Troy Downing
Combat Veteran | America First Conservative

| | | [**President Trump, you suck at being president. You are just so terrible, in so many ways. And you need to leave NOW.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tecf48fa7-ec28-4b07-85c6-b7ad673f8105/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) If all the presidents who sucked from throughout history got together, and formed a club, they would elect you "president of Presidents Who Suck." You've tanked the economy, you've made us an International pariah, and you've taken stupidity to a whole new level Please resign today, for the good of America. Focus on retirement, where you can suck at things like golf, fishing, yelling at the kids on your lawn, and other things that won't put the country in peril. [**President Trump, you suck. Please resign NOW.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T1505c7a0-b4dc-49f3-aa24-fc22bdc8bc53/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | | |

| | |
# [Tell other Democratic states to follow California's lead, and redistrict to help win back the House](https://default.salsalabs.org/T8056e362-433c-4c9b-ad3e-3b63f86d0be6/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta70600e8-749c-4f7a-89b5-ced6bfa9f176/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | _([Take action](https://default.salsalabs.org/T63e186a8-d93c-4838-b282-ad74f686ef5c/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9), then share with friends to help build support!)_ Earlier this year, Republican states, led by Texas, pushed to redistrict early to help Republican House candidates win more races, and help Republicans keep the House. **And instead of whining or writing a sternly-worded letter, California decided to[fight fire with fire](https://default.salsalabs.org/T0bb4dda0-670b-4e6f-a27d-95848f3381ab/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9), and pass its own measure (proposition 50) to add as many as 5 new Democratic districts.** We salute California, and Governor Newsom for driving this effort, and we urge all other Democratic states that can help elect more Democrats to do the same. Yes, it would be great if we didn't have to do this, and could have simple, sensible rules that covered the nation. But that's not happening, Republicans are changing the rules to make their job easier, and if we don't fight back to counter this, there is no way we can win the House, and no way we can keep our democracy. [**So please add your name, and Tell other Democratic states to follow California's lead, and redistrict to help Democrats win back the House.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T56d738df-906c-43ef-aeca-92db186ad824/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

body:
Democratic Socialist Zohran Mamdani, 34, was [elected New York City mayor Tuesday night](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2u2zAQhE8jdRTI1Z9VsEijzmlTCityaTGmSIHcSHFOH9h4N3jFVAMM5vt-3Zc7-uDj4yfupNmlJdJVAjFTXnbcLUbP-KSyY9yQGWM0iAtI6IVSQvaLRR9eS1db7YiA2pq0GgdoOzlCV2967ad-ciuAQWvUdJtG7ImsUaNVtpO32uv3mlLyE4BmuHWDgVaadTWwGll1kqznlJvfyUfeyIVXOik3Ju110BvzUar2RwVzBfN1XY1Lf98U776C-UjBszelgrkk4zH4wqJsyTwvPEn8S1vGKL5YReE_sYj4MuJMTLmIDaMVR7ooC0t7Mhm5CIdZBHIsXMD4rLO2x-VD8GUYq04-dvTh846_pfTU8D8AAP__eG2Pag) as Democrats swept the governorships of Virginia and New Jersey as well.

| | |
---
| [](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu4yAQRb8Gd7bwgLFTUKwUpcu2W6IxDDG7gC2DE-XvV86u3g-8Ypo5VzNX59fd3DHEkB8_MZGufjWZXiVSrbSbhMlhDhX_UEmYF6wVc7aIBjgMbd-3fDAOQ3wb2TjtiYBEQ7ofFQjJR5DNogehLt6PAFZNHibluZ8vk5ykB3Jqwibo81rf888AdGqSyoLgdp4tzJYzycmFuu7d7zXkupCP7_VJe2fX1ES91LoVJn4wuDG4hfykUrvlvS0YcS5n6CTidtRkynrslpi4UsIQGahzmciFIzFx3TC4dsO9ZtrLErb_3GLaMDzyJ_EF-4m3YhDmn4Xj1GQA5KDGixz52Ozaba8QYyhqZJI_zoefxvVbmp8a_gYAAP__zPeRHg) | |
# Wednesday, November 5, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1872, suffragette Susan B. Anthony and 14 other women [defiantly voted in the presidential election](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuhDAQRL8GOiN7MZgrXKShu7Qp0WIvhxPbnPDmEH8fcb-QYrqn0cz7uk93DDHkxycmsrxsU6ajRGKmfUqYPObA-EMlYV6RGXN2iBNI6IRSQnaTxxDPSdfeLkRAbU1WmR5aLQ3oerWa_KKl9NpLNITdzRmj3U21g9JaqrkO9mpTSr4D0PSD7h200s2zg9nJSkvygbe9-d5C5pWWeG4v2hu3pTralflZqvajgrGC8TiOpqTAa9lywJzwcWEVjCXhzuJ6d1HrKcpvwSxmgZnXLZ_iwCJw36kweaEGA0IN8ma6bjAVjPVu_fMIMYbSm0rLR8IQ3wv4X9peFv4CAAD__w0kgKA) between Ulysses S. Grant and Horace Greeley at a time when women weren’t allowed to vote. She was arrested and fined, and pardoned nearly 150 years later by President Trump in 2020. 🍗Thanksgiving is just around the corner, and we’re feeling nostalgic. Do you have a favorite Thanksgiving memory—sweet, funny, or unexpected—that you’d like to share? Reply to this email with your story, plus your first name and city, and we may feature it in our holiday edition. If you missed the Ozempic boom, today's sponsor, [Hypha Labs](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu4yAQRb8Gd7bwgLFTUKwUpcu2W6IxDDG7gC2DE-XvV86u3g-8Ypo5VzNX59fd3DHEkB8_MZGufjWZXiVSrbSbhMlhDhX_UEmYF6wVc7aIBjgMbd-3fDAOQ3wb2TjtiYBEQ7ofFQjJR5DNogehLt6PAFZNHibluZ8vk5ykB3Jqwibo81rf888AdGqSyoLgdp4tzJYzycmFuu7d7zXkupCP7_VJe2fX1ES91LoVJn4wuDG4hfykUrvlvS0YcS5n6CTidtRkynrslpi4UsIQGahzmciFIzFx3TC4dsO9ZtrLErb_3GLaMDzyJ_EF-4m3YhDmn4Xj1GQA5KDGixz52Ozaba8QYyhqZJI_zoefxvVbmp8a_gYAAP__zPeRHg), could be your next big opportunity—using AI and functional mushrooms to revolutionize wellness and personalized medicine, with investors rushing to join before tonight’s share price increase.
|
---
## Democrats Sweep Key Races
Democratic Socialist Zohran Mamdani, 34, was [elected New York City mayor Tuesday night](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2u2zAQhE8jdRTI1Z9VsEijzmlTCityaTGmSIHcSHFOH9h4N3jFVAMM5vt-3Zc7-uDj4yfupNmlJdJVAjFTXnbcLUbP-KSyY9yQGWM0iAtI6IVSQvaLRR9eS1db7YiA2pq0GgdoOzlCV2967ad-ciuAQWvUdJtG7ImsUaNVtpO32uv3mlLyE4BmuHWDgVaadTWwGll1kqznlJvfyUfeyIVXOik3Ju110BvzUar2RwVzBfN1XY1Lf98U776C-UjBszelgrkk4zH4wqJsyTwvPEn8S1vGKL5YReE_sYj4MuJMTLmIDaMVR7ooC0t7Mhm5CIdZBHIsXMD4rLO2x-VD8GUYq04-dvTh846_pfTU8D8AAP__eG2Pag) as Democrats won the governorships of Virginia and New Jersey as well. Mamdani easily beat former N.Y. Gov. Andrew Cuomo, a fellow Democrat, and Republican Curtis Sliwa to take office in a race that drew a massive turnout. In Virginia, Democrat Abigail Spanberger [was declared the winner](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OpTAMhZ8GuqBgfrdIsQ3d3XZLZBIDngkJSjwX3bcfcaV5giksWfqkT-ec_4_5gew5bP_wICNrnANd2ZMIpfnAw2FgwU_KB4YdRTAEiziDhk7VtdLd7JD9a25LZ1YioKYkUw89NK0eoC1301HbwJ91cLUdqQek1Vndd9oODlccdMnmttW1fh9A1Y9tb6HRdlksLFYXrSbHElP1ETnITqt_xSelysaj9GYXOXPR_C1gKmC6rqsKi71b3LyA6YyehW0uYCJPVjiG-39y2jgwqu12hZjUD1WJ8peXrPKJYaG0UVKEyZPKhCmrhJbUxUElGxCasR-7Mhl3Xuw9534oWr0dyP6dT3416tPAdwAAAP__KreO_A) over GOP Lt. Gov. Winsome Earle-Sears, becoming the first woman to lead the state. In Virginia's attorney general race, Democrat Jay Jones defeated GOP incumbent Jason Miyares after resisting calls to drop out over text messages expressing violence toward Republicans. In New Jersey’s governor race, Democrat [Mikie Sherrill beat Republican Jack Ciattarelli](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L3uozAQBPCnMR2RvRjwFS6uocu1V6LFLPFe_BGZVaK8_Sl5hX8x1Ug_aebvdb0iJy63P5jJy1HXQq8zkQi1NWPesbDgnc6MJaIIlhIQV9Aw9sb0elx35PRebbf7gwho6MibeYLB6hlsF_1gNCIYJAc0OOu2YxpNmNwvMEcgN3bsP5ox-huAy-TsFGDQYdsCbEErq2lnqe3yr3KRSEd61ye1S6i5Sz6KPE41_FawKFgkUuSUPp2CJdZMn0EKloD5gXwrCpZxdDPooc98Z-rPSK1xSv1OuYaG0j85SG1vBUvX_P54cUp8TrOy-paRv3YnPzrr6eF_AAAA___3NH3M), keeping the state's governor’s mansion in Democratic Party hands. Also last night, California voters [approved Proposition 50](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQhZ_GdERmAMMtXGxDd7fdEg3jAWbXP5EZJcrbr5JXuMWpjnSOvu_P9_qNEiUfvzGx172smZ9XZFWua8IUMIviP74S5hNVMWdCXMHC2HZda8c1oMTXOjTB78zAfcO-mxz0g51gaE6_0VfnMMwbhX23lsI0ETp2PGGwo4NG_Hut6-wnADc3D46gt7RtBBtZM1gOoqXe_hbJevIeX-XB9UYlNdGfqvfL9L8MLAYWPfmUGN-dgeUsid9ABhbCdEc5soFlHOcZvqAljLKXmgXbwKlQRb3aykEurUIq-WgfQlrqy8DSVB_uT4lRLjeZwR4J5XPT6I-8PTz8DwAA___-qYOF), a ballot measure that allows Democrats to redraw the state’s congressional districts without using the independent redistricting commission.
## UPS Plane Crashes in Louisville
At least seven people were killed and 11 others injured after a UPS cargo plane was involved [in a "catastrophic crash"](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OpDAMx58mdKDEBNgpUlyTbq-9EpnELL4LySh4B_H2J-YVtrB-jfX_-vM5fyInzl-_cScna5kznUciEarzjnvEzIL_6NgxbyiCOQfEGTQMrTGtHuaInK7ZNtGtREB9Q85MI_RWT2CbzYE2hqKhuE6E8fGgaJcxGlxpgNFOsWF3qxmj3wfQjR92DNDrsCwBlqCV1RRZSu3-Fs6y0Zqu8qLahbI3yW0iz0P1vxR4Bf48z-77QCkRr_tBgT-k1EuBv5vdQOGSFfjbVoE3RoHXVoFP5ZuPF6dELXJ9lio3Q8VVWs6BI2VR4D8m_bBmGLSeFPimuvg8OSU-xklZ_bUjp3c0-dGeLwf_AwAA__-5B4gE) in Louisville that exploded in a huge fireball of flame and smoke. _"Anybody who has seen the images and the video knows how violent this crash is, and there are a lot of families that are gonna be waiting and wondering for a period of time,”_ [Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear said](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOszAQhJ_GdCB7bSB_4eJv6HLtlWixl-CLsRHeBPH2J_IKV4w00kgzmu_7Pt4xxJAeX7iS5TmPiY4SiZn2ccXVYwqMTyorpgWZMSWHOIKEtlaqlu3oMcRzNJW3MxGQrsiqvgNtZA-mWmyrSXul287L_p-_9U7TDEbdutlQq8hUwV5tSsmPAJruZjoHWrppcjA5KYwkHzjvzU8OiRea45nftDcur1W0C_NWhP4vYBAwHMfRuKlcL65cwHBZAcNrK_UWMVHtdixLHfMrlHeIkeonJX655ylgqHbrtyPEGErXCyMfK4b4GeI_0Xlb-A0AAP__RR15rw) at a news conference Tuesday night. Businesses in the area were heavily impacted by the crash, including Kentucky Petroleum Recycling and Grade A Auto Parts, the governor said. At least two employees of Grade A Auto Parts are unaccounted for. The plane crashed at around 5:15 p.m. local time as it was [departing for Honolulu](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHO3CAQhJ8Gd5xgjbk0FGno_rQprTWszyQYEOx_p3v7yPcKKaYa6dPM9_tr_cKUU3n8wpMc73Ut9BqZmKmvJ54RS2L8S-PEciAzlhIQV1CwSK2lWtaIKb9XM0W3EwHNEzl9tzAbdQczHc6QsfOymEXtSySaN2ujMjuYEPVGFqbkLprW6hOAm_1hbIBZhW0LsAUljKKYuPbbn5oKH7Tnd31Sv4V6TtkdzG2I-acAL8CXd6uDr0qAv8ACvNYCvDIC_PeQ1z8BvmUsJEPHcdCQyDLX7zSeKWeSmHqrnWXr9WycykOOgzJTl6nIljGQrD1SF-Cn7mJ7pZzTsHdh1OPElD_D-L9sPh38CwAA__-C84k3) from Louisville’s Muhammad Ali International Airport, the Federal Aviation Administration said, noting it would have been full of fuel for the long flight to Hawaii.
## Dick Cheney Dies at 84
Dick Cheney, [one of the most influential vice presidents](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL3uozAQxJ_GdI7MYsCNi2vocu2VaGMvwRd_IHsvEW9_Iq_wL6YazU8z8-e-3jHEkJ-_MZHlrayZPi0SM9U1YfKYA-OLWsK8IzPm7BBXUDDKvpdqXD2GeK6683YjAho6sv08waDVDLrbraHZgBqmwRmlcQQayWizzePozAjad8FetL5XXwHcJqMnB4Nyj4eDh1NCK_KBS739LSHzTls8y5vqzZXURbszH00MvwQsApZ8HqXxZQlYLrCApe8FLEoLWP41ee0TsGylJqryHRzJo1ILnjJLH9xLup0yndITeokszRXsqvXHJ8QY2jQLrZ4JQ_wW4B-99rbwPwAA__-ifn40) in U.S. history, died Tuesday at age 84 from complications of pneumonia and heart disease, his family announced. Serving under President George W. Bush, Cheney played a central role in [shaping post-9/11 foreign policy](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLuu3CAQhp8Gd6yG8XULijR0J21KC8N4PTkYLGDX8ttHPq-QYqQpfn3_5c_X_GU5cHz9tjvpuqY50lkC1Up53u3ubeRqv6nsNm62Vhujs3ZGwF4qJaGfveVwzV3j9UqE1Dak1Thg28GIXbPpST1XmHBqnwA4AS0L9h5o9ctAfa-GhvVNUwp-DvExTN3gsAW3LA4XB6ID8lxTfvxNHOtGa7jSh_LDpb0Jeqv1KKL9JdAINOd5Pt7F1uTtdQsEmlJTvgSau5lAc6TAld393sYCjVICDXQCjWf3Ld1GkS75YUfyyFTYU6zyHT1l-aKUXyRPubzLJj3TjZmGJw4j9gCjQNNk7Y-TQ-AyjKKD1245_GSt_zXwR-O_AAAA__-Vvoy2), including helping launch the Iraq War. A longtime fixture in Republican politics, Cheney also held key roles under Presidents Gerald Ford and George H.W. Bush and led Halliburton, a major oilfield services company, before returning to public office. His family remembered him as a devoted patriot, husband, and father. clean_body: Democratic Socialist Zohran Mamdani, 34, was as Democrats swept the governorships of Virginia and New Jersey as well. | | | | | | Wednesday, November 5, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1872, suffragette Susan B. Anthony and 14 other women between Ulysses S. Grant and Horace Greeley at a time when women weren’t allowed to vote. She was arrested and fined, and pardoned nearly 150 years later by President Trump in 2020. 🍗Thanksgiving is just around the corner, and we’re feeling nostalgic. Do you have a favorite Thanksgiving memory—sweet, funny, or unexpected—that you’d like to share? Reply to this email with your story, plus your first name and city, and we may feature it in our holiday edition. If you missed the Ozempic boom, today's sponsor, , could be your next big opportunity—using AI and functional mushrooms to revolutionize wellness and personalized medicine, with investors rushing to join before tonight’s share price increase. | Democrats Sweep Key Races Democratic Socialist Zohran Mamdani, 34, was as Democrats won the governorships of Virginia and New Jersey as well. Mamdani easily beat former N.Y. Gov. Andrew Cuomo, a fellow Democrat, and Republican Curtis Sliwa to take office in a race that drew a massive turnout. In Virginia, Democrat Abigail Spanberger over GOP Lt. Gov. Winsome Earle-Sears, becoming the first woman to lead the state. In Virginia's attorney general race, Democrat Jay Jones defeated GOP incumbent Jason Miyares after resisting calls to drop out over text messages expressing violence toward Republicans. In New Jersey’s governor race, Democrat , keeping the state's governor’s mansion in Democratic Party hands. Also last night, California voters , a ballot measure that allows Democrats to redraw the state’s congressional districts without using the independent redistricting commission. UPS Plane Crashes in Louisville At least seven people were killed and 11 others injured after a UPS cargo plane was involved in Louisville that exploded in a huge fireball of flame and smoke. "Anybody who has seen the images and the video knows how violent this crash is, and there are a lot of families that are gonna be waiting and wondering for a period of time,” at a news conference Tuesday night. Businesses in the area were heavily impacted by the crash, including Kentucky Petroleum Recycling and Grade A Auto Parts, the governor said. At least two employees of Grade A Auto Parts are unaccounted for. The plane crashed at around 5:15 p.m. local time as it was from Louisville’s Muhammad Ali International Airport, the Federal Aviation Administration said, noting it would have been full of fuel for the long flight to Hawaii. Dick Cheney Dies at 84 Dick Cheney, in U.S. history, died Tuesday at age 84 from complications of pneumonia and heart disease, his family announced. Serving under President George W. Bush, Cheney played a central role in , including helping launch the Iraq War. A longtime fixture in Republican politics, Cheney also held key roles under Presidents Gerald Ford and George H.W. Bush and led Halliburton, a major oilfield services company, before returning to public office. His family remembered him as a devoted patriot, husband, and father. . name: Amy Carnevale
subject: From Taunton to Worcester: CONSERVATIVES WIN!
body:
We notched wins up and down the ballot last night...
| | | | | [](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001V9Nb35zS1mGNS4yQkj1a5joD1F8OcWxHW2XQZdCfH-t19hXKXDKFe-xpjLEbD1KTjxT4gWvSbPtv-9fmIYijZKCX948BpTxEjpdYL5Cf-qYEMkUS8u0MUNpI3HJKljkiJXqlxGMW4rZjrKiZVZw3vQ==&c=Nuf9p9b2tsRChSbJTxrZLV8youJJYNSXu4D95W1ZkcToUD9fJDYg8A==&ch=sW6zqIMxL1A1PEN81a1ilPfSiSQvAOZEZqJ3-BVcM6D_ggEDk2sC8g==)
---
| Dear Peter,
Thanks to your support, we notched wins up and down the ballot last night! Across the Commonwealth, we proved that two party government is growing once again!
From city halls to school committees, Republican candidates delivered big wins that will shape the future of our communities for years to come.
🗳️ Because of you, we helped re-elect proven fighters like:
* Taunton Mayor Shaunna O'Connell
* Chelsea City Councilor Todd Taylor
* Leominster City Councilors Mark Bodanza & Thomas Ardinger
* Weymouth Town Councilors Rick Coughlin & Andrew Donovan
* Brockton City Councilor Jeffrey Thompson
* Attleboro City Councilors Jay Dilisio, Jonathan Tavares & Micheal Angelo
* Agawam City Councilor Cecelia Calabrese
* Westfield City Councilor Dan Allie
* Taunton City Councilors John McCaul & David Pottier
* Holyoke City Councilor Linda Vacon

✅ We propelled fresh voices into office including:
* Christopher Peckham, Fall River City Council
* Jaclyn Corriveau, Peabody City Council
* Will Crocker, Barnstable Town Council
* Sean Powers, Braintree Light Board

🎓 And we empowered parents and families across the state to stand up for their values, electing:
* Marc Kurzberg, Braintree School Committee
* Maura Ryan-Ciardiello, Haverhill School Committee
* James Poore & James Stors, Attleboro School Committee
* Stephen Pina, Brockton School Committee
* Ashley Dickerman, Weymouth School Committee
* Kathleen Roy, Re-elected to Worcester School Committee

....and the list goes on!
These victories didn't happen because lightning struck. They happened because of you: your donations, your time, your belief in what we're building, one victory at a time.
Today, we take a moment to celebrate our candidates. But as you know, the work doesn't stop here.
The Massachusetts Republican Party is more committed than ever to protecting these hard-fought gains, advancing our values, and winning Republican seats in every corner of the Commonwealth.
---
| With gratitude,
---
| |
---
| Amy Carnevale Chair, Massachusetts Republican Party
---
| | [SUPPORT THE MASSGOP TODAY](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001V9Nb35zS1mGNS4yQkj1a5joD1F8OcWxHW2XQZdCfH-t19hXKXDKFexyyGVnQS42nEdkIcJTUIz3mq2rTWyVAqXEM9i0ceQwB8xgznxugWY_gy7AGkYEMgoNzAQRBs7yJn6bCECHZXxiu1DvN2mXkj03ad_ZLKAfgd0e7WCElMamwX1aSK-6XlH6I2uzs1mJG5tpVpQpz_5w=&c=Nuf9p9b2tsRChSbJTxrZLV8youJJYNSXu4D95W1ZkcToUD9fJDYg8A==&ch=sW6zqIMxL1A1PEN81a1ilPfSiSQvAOZEZqJ3-BVcM6D_ggEDk2sC8g==)

name: Lena Moffitt, Evergreen
subject: HUGE wins for climate and affordability last night
disclaimer_text: paid for by Evergreen Action
body:
Now, it’s up to leaders across the country to take up the mantle
| | | | [](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4169680/87SdQ4wZ) |
---|---|---
---
| Hi—

When clean and affordable energy is on the ballot, we win.

That’s the first thing you should know about yesterday’s gubernatorial election results. By electing Mikie Sherrill in New Jersey and Abigail Spanberger in Virginia, **voters sent a clear, resounding message that they want leaders who are going to fight hard for them. They want leaders who are going to lower costs, challenge the corruption that’s driving skyrocketing bills, and expand clean energy in their states. The climate crisis is expensive, but clean energy is cheap.**

It’s a platform that offers a better path forward than Trump’s corrupt crusade against clean energy, and leaders across the country should take note of it.

[**Will you join us in spreading the word about Sherrill and Spanberger’s victories on social media so we can demonstrate that clean energy is cheap energy?**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4169681/87SdQ4wZ) |
[ ](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4169682/87SdQ4wZ)
---
---
[LIKE AND SHARE](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4169683/87SdQ4wZ)
---
---
| Ratepayers in both New Jersey and Virginia have faced brutal price hikes—and there was no shying away from that in this year’s elections. Tying their opponents’ support for failing anti-clean-energy policies in Washington to rising costs at home, Sherrill and Spanberger led with commonsense plans to lower costs through clean energy. **Plans that leaders across the country should take a page from _right now._**

Sherrill promised bold action to drive down utility costs with a massive build-out of cleaner, cheaper energy, and vowed to take on utilities and PJM—New Jersey’s grid operator—to get it done. Meanwhile, Spanberger made energy a top issue in her affordability platform, including expanding local clean energy generation and calling on data centers to bring their own clean generation.

The takeaway?

**Clean energy isn’t an abstract issue. It’s not only our best tool to continue tackling the climate crisis—****it’s also our best tool to drive down costs. These leaders ran and won on clean energy as an affordability solution.**

Governors-elect Mikie Sherrill and Abigail Spanberger owned this winning issue—now it’s up to them to deliver, and for leaders across the country to take up the mantle.

[**I hope you’ll share this post on social media today to help spread this message far and wide.**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4169684/87SdQ4wZ) |
---
| Onward, |
---
| | |
---
### Lena Moffitt
Executive Director
Evergreen Action
---
---
* * *
---
| _Evergreen is building the ambitious, actionable policy roadmap for an all-out mobilization to defeat climate change—and to create millions of jobs in a thriving, just, and inclusive clean energy future. None of this would be possible without your support._ |

name: The Texas Flyover
subject: Election Results, Lego Thefts, and Bass Pro Brawl
body:
Police in Watauga arrested a 28-year-old man accused of stealing $1,300 of Lego toys from a local Target on Friday. Authorities believe he's part of an organized crime ring responsible for more than 80 similar thefts in Texas and Oklahoma.

| | |
---
| [](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkc2unDAMRp8m7DJKbJKBBYsrUXa3aqVKXaKQGIgUyCjJwJ23r-iP1E1X9dKyj4_1fX8f340Pfl8-m4268jHHcaczByqF0kiBbPFxT5SfoeRASywrzSWPIEBxKblQozM-vMa6ct0kXCsr6uRdA9a1lFCtHU1O2JkcEqJsQDd4R8RGuEnPVGNb-e6CSSmUBCEV3MRkQYnWWt3odlYTqwU5X2K6lZUKfZg8h1c8KN1s3KrQraU8MsM3BgOD4TzP2yPFcpnvy_bK5vD7kjltxodrg8FQVuLWZOJzTDz7cFDiAqSG-mLgYHeGfaMU163idQuCgQ6ZYS-E5J8uEP-2Eh9-aTDQebbeMezvQn5dxVX0hl-AgX6WbbRmexi_7P8c2Mj558aw_8n-3czxmSwx7P86VaXOPU4fgs_6zmqx_HmqKv8X3tHBjwAAAP__2nSqJQ) | |
# Wednesday, November 5, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1912, politician [Morris Sheppard](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0Dty5CAQgOHToEwqaKFXQLCJstl0QxWPlmCNQAXt0cztXfYVfIH_r_r-PbaHDjGk468-UdFrz1vCu0YkwrJhREshp4L1M1KNeGTyuFPdgMPQCtHyYXM6xPcmG6cMd4toUIlphF5KIaDxiovZ8r6fJ661dEb3elrGZTTWSN4v_dwE9R0Tgg8CuBig48bCwBdrx3lc9sEwydEFyqUjj4QvXff4zk8snc1nE5Unuirr_zBYGaz3fXdUvc4phoRdLgeD1evkTM4fDFZMVAJWBmv1eF26uPZ_9qk9cymhNkW56w4xhjpOTPLj1CH-fOh3Ok8FXwEAAP__Jtd64Q) was elected to represent Texas in the U.S. Senate. He authored the Eighteenth Amendment to the Constitution, earning him the title "father of national Prohibition." Sheppard remained in office until his death in 1941, making him the state's longest-serving senator. It might not feel like it outside, but Thanksgiving is right around the corner. Do you have a favorite Thanksgiving memory you'd be willing to share? We plan to feature a few of your stories in an upcoming holiday edition. Reply to this email with a one-paragraph description. Be sure to include your first name and city! |
---
## Election Day Highlights
Unofficial results show that every one of the 17 proposed amendments to the Texas constitution was [approved by a majority of voters](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1uwyAUBeCnsbdYcGOgHhi6ZEunSh0RhouNdB0suPl7-yrpI3Q-Op-Ozs_ZnX2mfFm-_IaWH6m4C94bITNWh4SBc7lUbFfiRrgUXjFxcyBAHaQ8COWiz_R0Yx_tLOIke7TSaDiOo5TQr3YyQaHQAQJqn0xCI6KEIAIoNFKbPtsXJqVQEoRUMIg5gBJTCPpDT0nN3SgwZi514BUZH74lepYb1iGUrSe7Mu-tO352cOrgNHuiwjvG7IdSlw5O36_Ke7H7C92Gvl0rtr7auN8zUW7adKNYNp_prfL_vrhZ-A0AAP__HtJ0jQ) yesterday. The measures increase property tax exemptions for homeowners, deny bail for violent felonies, allocate $3 billion for dementia research and up to $1 billion a year for water projects, and limit participation in state and local elections to U.S. citizens. In Houston, Harris County Attorney Christian Menefee and former City Council member Amanda Edwards were the top two vote-getters in a special election to [fill a congressional seat](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL3O4yAURJ8Gd1hw_RO7oEjjLrvbbWlhuNhIGCy4iZO3XznSPsHXHWmko5n5-5gf2gcf1196R0Vvl-aIZwlIhHnGgIZ8ihnLM1AJuCba0FGZQUDHpeSim6324TO3lVWLsKOsUMlbD03bSgnVpox1AhrX4zD0zrTGwWJ0M_ZibDsN6CqvLpmUopMgZAe1WAx0YjSmH_rRdQtrBVpPKde0IeFbFxc-6YW5NmmvgtqIjsKaO4OJwXSeZ72XeGUMJoz8WRhMRwqevLnw_6iLvzYuB9q4SXHNWIpPUQdufaHsDXF9HKhz4Rtqi5ZT4prnZ0zOMZh05ve7_PP78ZmqrOxx-hB86W-sFeuuffgWpJ_d-lLwLwAA__9LlY8s) that has been vacant since March. The two Democrats will face each other in a runoff election early next year. ## Arrest Made in Lego Thefts
Police in Watauga arrested a 28-year-old man accused of [stealing $1,300 of Lego toys](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGuszAQhE8DndHuxoZQuPibdPnbVyIDC1ja4AhvArn9E7nC60aa0Yy--bl39xAlrvP_8GCvx5S6lfcsrMpbx8KDxrRunF-iWXhOuvCkuSMgZxANuG4MUT6dLUffw9hiyR6bmi7WIlK5eGvdtcEQyNm-cQEmAsAGaua-hpbbMvqzDBEcEqCjCvqBHLTDUF_rdnJ9YYHHqGmrdGHlI-RJPunNWzWkRyl-UX3m4vKvoFtBt33fqykd9sQ4AwXdTnk6QcNrDubEMF8Os8V1Nntcs6bVSHpzufnxuUeRmOumsDA_QpTvjv7tnben3wAAAP__3uh6mQ) from a local Target on Friday. Authorities believe he's part of an organized crime ring responsible for more than 80 similar thefts in Texas and Oklahoma. At the time of his arrest, Winston Love had eight outstanding felony warrants. He has since been [connected to 23 thefts](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFq5DAQxvGnkbtZpFnbey5cLAfimhzXpTRjaeQVyFKQZuPs2x_OK6QeGL7_7_1teaOYYt7-0s6zfIWyZD5aYhGuCyd2Ekuu3J5JWuKtyIODtAU1DmAM6GHxFNNr6Ts_r9pPpuPZ3Ea89r0x2D1mN61eh6vHQQcTAms3melmDNNKPKLv4nw-M0YPBrUZ8KJXh4OenBt_jVMYVtVr9lFKvciDhb-ohfQqn1wvruxdmh8iH01d7wqtQnscx2Vv-bwptJzh2RTaM0qhdTXurNDulIFq5SbsgZx7NvZQAjRhOjXgTAUprwahlh0OEnpuBEJ1Y4GY4dsBasybQksV7nfzz_55_93V2X8cMaXYxpvq9bZTTN9D5We8nzP-DwAA___DjJHg) of products worth $37,000 from Target stores throughout North Texas over the past two months. "_It’s incredibly brazen_ ," said Watauga Police Chief Jim Lewis. "_He either loads them in a cart or just loads them under his arms and literally walks right out the door_." Lewis [explained](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOrCAQRp8GOwyMolhQ3MZub3tLgzAqCcoG5q67b_-HfYW_nZyczPn-PZaHDTFc-197oqH3lpYL7xKRCPOCER2FdGUs_yOViHuiAzcqCwhQXEou1OJtiJ-lb7xZhZ9kg0aOA3R9LyU0h5kGL3U3aI-T3VYYUSqtx853HlfXT10TTJVJKZQEIRW0YnWgxOTcoIdpUyvrBfpAKbd0IOHbli1-0gtz69LZRHMQPQvr_jCYGcz3fVeOh8vjEy-PF1WOwVzDKpBy9Axme2IOztaTy-FEBvNXzmsm_3ZysnlH4oVSxsJX0Er3MLQHnbHJxj_vEGMow8h6sZ82xO9H9LsdXwZ-AgAA__9fAYkA) that Legos can be sold online for nearly retail value, "so the risk versus reward for them becomes worth it." Police could make additional arrests as they continue their investigation.
## Truck with Band Instruments Hit by Train
A semi-truck hauling instruments and equipment for Austin's Vandegrift High School marching band was [hit by a train](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2O2zAQBOCnoToaJE-_BQs3aoIzAqRIKazJpbQ4inTItXV--0B5hdQDDL6Z35_LJ1CktN5gR8vfIS8JjxqRGcuCER1TTgXrM3KNuGbeMHBdjDKd1FqqbvFA8b20jbd35SfdoNVDbz7aVmvTbBYApyH41oe2G5wfNWJw0zgOTvUjBN-QPcu0Vp02Snfmou7OdGpyrh_7KXR30Sr0xLlceEPGb6ghvvMLy8XlvYl2Y35U8XEVZhZmPo7jstd0ZsLMmOSzCjOfo4SZT34RZn5B8rgWCizvkLz8QnxUuUNxG6VVQmAskgtQkhtxlfjnSY8dE0suT_clKckKSULinCgLM0OR16v-efvx69YU6x8HxUi1H0Sr1h0o_qPy_x38suZvAAAA__-f1JNx) Monday afternoon in San Antonio. Authorities believe he's part of an organized crime ring responsible for more than 80 similar thefts in Texas and Oklahoma. | | | | | | Wednesday, November 5, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1912, politician was elected to represent Texas in the U.S. Senate. He authored the Eighteenth Amendment to the Constitution, earning him the title "father of national Prohibition." Sheppard remained in office until his death in 1941, making him the state's longest-serving senator. It might not feel like it outside, but Thanksgiving is right around the corner. Busy day? Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Election Day Highlights Unofficial results show that every one of the 17 proposed amendments to the Texas constitution was yesterday. The measures increase property tax exemptions for homeowners, deny bail for violent felonies, allocate $3 billion for dementia research and up to $1 billion a year for water projects, and limit participation in state and local elections to U.S. citizens. In Houston, Harris County Attorney Christian Menefee and former City Council member Amanda Edwards were the top two vote-getters in a special election to that has been vacant since March. The two Democrats will face each other in a runoff election early next year. In Tarrant County, Air Force veteran Taylor Rehmet and Patriot Mobile executive Leigh Wambsganss each received around 40% of the vote to vacated by Comptroller Kelly Hancock, while former Southlake mayor John Huffman finished a distant third. In San Antonio, voters approved a proposition to allocate up to $311 million in county hotel and rental car taxes toward the construction of a . In Austin, voters rejected a measure to to generate up to $110 million in funding for homelessness initiatives, public health services, and employee benefits. Arrest Made in Lego Thefts Police in Watauga arrested a 28-year-old man accused of from a local Target on Friday. Authorities believe he's part of an organized crime ring responsible for more than 80 similar thefts in Texas and Oklahoma. At the time of his arrest, Winston Love had eight outstanding felony warrants. He has since been of products worth $37,000 from Target stores throughout North Texas over the past two months. "It’s incredibly brazen ," said Watauga Police Chief Jim Lewis. "He either loads them in a cart or just loads them under his arms and literally walks right out the door." Lewis that Legos can be sold online for nearly retail value, "so the risk versus reward for them becomes worth it." Police could make additional arrests as they continue their investigation. Truck with Band Instruments Hit by Train A semi-truck hauling instruments and equipment for Austin's Vandegrift High School marching band was Monday afternoon in San Antonio. The band was scheduled to perform in the UIL State Marching Band Competition at the Alamodome that evening. After getting stuck at a railroad crossing, the driver and a passenger were able to before it was hit by a Union Pacific train, which dragged it 200 yards down the track and split the trailer in half. Bands at the competition to Vandegrift's band, enabling them to perform as planned. Vandegrift won the 6A state title in 2022 and 2023. "It is truly humbling to see this community of high school musicians pull together to support one another ," the district said in a statement. "The band community is a special one. Never forget that ," read a from TxBands.com. Most of the instruments in the collision, according to district officials. | Flying together with our sponsor Will The Silver Boom Charge Higher? Artificial Intelligence is creating a massive demand for silver. Electric Vehicles and EV Batteries are driving up demand for silver. Even Russia’s Central Bank has begun stockpiling it in record amounts. Silver has long been an essential building block to the green revolution, but now a powerful mix of new forces is quietly supercharging demand. Silver isn’t just a “green tech” metal anymore. It’s becoming the metal of the AI revolution. In a surprising twist, Russia has begun stockpiling silver for the first time ever through its State Reserve Fund. Since that move, silver has surged over 30% this year, even outperforming gold. With**demand outpacing supply and industrial use cases booming... Silver may be one of the most undervalued assets in the market today. () ➤ Hood County: Residents near a Bitcoin mining facility operated by MARA Holdings rejected a measure to incorporate as the city of Mitchell Bend to be able to regulate noise pollution from the data center. A federal judge denied the company’s request to block the election. () ➤ Dallas: Monday's city council meeting was packed with residents who spoke in opposition to the potential relocation of city hall, repairs for which could reach $500 million over the next decade. The property has been discussed as a potential site for a new Mavericks arena. () ➤ Odessa: A fight broke out Saturday at the new Bass Pro Shops store after two men in line for the restroom reportedly got into an altercation. None of those involved pressed charges, and the two men were banned from the store. () ➤ Canton: The city council unanimously voted Friday to suspend development of the proposed Grand Saline reservoir after other nearby cities withdrew their support. The reservoir was approved by representatives of the Texas Water Development Board's Region D in 2015 and 2020. () ➤ Austin: Five people were arrested last week after a woman was found handcuffed to exercise equipment outside a home. The woman told police she had been friends with one of the residents "until one day they decided they didn’t like her anymore." () ➤ Bexar County: Anselmo Barrera, a high-ranking member of the national Bandidos Outlaw Motorcycle Gang, was arrested Thursday at a home where a SWAT team found 33 firearms and more than $200,000 in cash. () ➤ The Dallas Cowboys made two deals before the NFL trade deadline yesterday, acquiring defensive tackle Quinnen Williams and linebacker Logan Wilson. () ➤ The Dallas Stars are hosting the 2027 NHL Stadium Series at AT&T Stadium in Arlington, the first hockey game to be played there. The February match is being billed as an outdoor contest, with the stadium's retractable roof open. () ➤ No. 16 Baylor women's basketball upset No. 7 Duke in their Paris season opener, defeating the Blue Devils 58-52. The Lady Bears are now 16-1 in program history when playing abroad. () ➤ San Antonio Spurs rookie Dylan Harper is set to miss multiple weeks after suffering a non-contact calf injury earlier this week. subject: Portland Voters Approve Minimum Wage Hike, Keep Pious Ali on City Council

------------------------------------------------------------
By Jon Fetherston on Nov 05, 2025 06:56 am
Voters in Maine’s largest city decisively approved a minimum wage increase on Tuesday, endorsing a city councilor’s proposal to raise the current $15.50 per hour to $19 per hour in increments over the next couple years. Nearly 63 percent voted in favor of the hike, versus only 35.5 percent who opposed it. Portland businesses had [...]

body:
\- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

Friend - I have just two things to share with you.

#1) Thank you! Just by opening my message, you've already proven that you're among the most active 20% of conservative voters.

_To put that in context, you 're more committed than 62 _MILLION_ Republican voters._

**[#2) And right now, you can put yourself in the _top 1% of committed conservatives by making even just a $1 contribution_ to help us stop Democrats from taking the Senate.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zmp2fjn2qjomj24pf4kibodr1dmn8cio621iv02g_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cphm4o36chh2bs)**

Thanks to donors giving $1, $5, $15, and so forth, we've been able to successfully fend off the Democrats' attacks and deliver on the agenda that matters to you and your family like border security, tax cuts, strong national defense, and so much more.

_The media often likes to call these "small-dollar donors."_

**[But I have to disagree. There's absolutely nothing small about it.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zgvesdceravma2o6b1fdlb47cc1esbtq4pevpem0_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cphm4o36chh2bs)**

Grassroots donors like you are the backbone of our Party and our secret weapon to beating the Left in the midterm elections - even as we get outraised by the Democrats' mega-donors.

**[That's why I'm asking you to make a donation that's the price of a single cup of coffee, a slice of pizza, or a jelly doughnut. Nothing more.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2znubm1t0ffn91qfhkdodt62alus4c4scqmo9jab0_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cphm4o36chh2bs)**

# [Mayor Mamdani](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=178052558&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MDUyNTU4LCJpYXQiOjE3NjIzNDc5MDMsImV4cCI6MTc2NDkzOTkwMywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.bx2-X0WaTseM68L2BvhaY8vfaKYAdv4XpRbo1F0Ph9Q)

Zohran Mamdani won the Mayor's race last night and is now the Mayor-Elect of New York City. Despite his historic win, the hard part in all likelihood lies ahead. I wrote this when he won the primary this summer:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/1d27cf9f-2404-4832-82f7-da2aa78adcb9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Last week, Zohran Mamdani posted a resounding victory in New York City's Democratic primary. It was a shocking outcome to many, as Zohran was young and unknown taking on Andrew Cuomo, who had money, the establishment and universal name ID, albeit with very mixed feelings among many voters from his time as Governor.
I think Zohran is likely to have a walkover in November unless the current field of Eric Adams, Jim Walden, Curtis Sliwa and Andrew Cuomo consolidates to just one or two candidates, and even then you'd have to project Zohran as the heavy favorite. He is very likely the city's next mayor.
Zohran triumphed despite the NYTimes and others clearly having it out for him. The institutions have less sway than ever, and voters are hungry for something new.
The reactions have been all over the map. People have been calling me with questions ranging from excitement to "Should I leave the city?"
I met with Zohran and Cuomo, as well as multiple other mayoral candidates before the primary. They met with me to seek advice but also an endorsement, as they thought I could move some voters their way. I declined to endorse for a number of reasons. I had former staffers and colleagues associated with 4 different campaigns, and didn't want to step on anyone out of loyalty to another. I see my role as an Independent reformer of the system. Last, I had complicated feelings and thoughts.
I had met Zohran prior to this campaign and sat down with him a number of weeks ago. I found him to be a good person who wants good things for people. He's a sincere and talented messenger. He is positive and not the least bit hateful or corrupt. He is newly married and comes across as dedicated and energetic.
I also think Zohran is smart and adaptable. Some folks who are ideological come across as stuck in their thinking - I don't think that's an issue with Zohran. I believe he will figure out a lot of things in real time. He certainly figured out a lot about how to connect with people as a candidate.
Big picture, I agree with his goal of making New York City more affordable. The rent in New York has gotten higher and higher - I hear it from young people I work with all of the time and can scarcely believe what they're paying. One person I know who is working in hospitality is paying close to $4,000 for an apartment that would have cost me $1,500 25 years ago. I like the idea of free child care - it is indeed very difficult and expensive to manage it on your own. Trying to make New York more affordable for the vast majority of its residents is a worthy vision.
Having a young, smart, dynamic mayor with good intentions who will show up for events and neighborhoods big or small and put his best foot forward is a major upgrade among several dimensions. That's a pretty good summary of why he won. Who would choose the scandal-tarred 67-year old over the next generation?
But I have some concerns, some of which are within Zohran's control, some of which aren't.
First, it's unclear how much of Zohran's economic agenda he'll be able to implement and how it would work. Raising taxes on the wealthy for example requires the state legislature to approve, and Governor Hochul has already said that would be a non-starter for her. Would she change her tune next year after Zohran is sworn in? I'm dubious. Free buses sound great, but homeless or near-homeless people would be more likely to use the buses when they're free and air-conditioned, and you could see the quality of experience go down. Even working-class New Yorkers might miss paying for the bus, which is definitely the cheapest way to get around. City-run grocery stores are a bad idea and would compete with mom-and-pop shops; if you're not trying to fill food deserts you're much better off simply giving people vouchers for groceries at existing stores that have razor-thin margins to begin with.
I love the idea of a mayor who wants to make the city work better for working families. That's a win. I studied this when I ran for mayor, and the mayor's ability to actually make things more affordable is somewhat limited in scope. I think a lot of people, including Zohran himself, are going to be frustrated at how little the average New Yorker's cost structure will change.
Meanwhile, business people are very nervous with a self-described socialist heading to City Hall. Imagining that all of these CEO-types will stick around New York under all circumstances is incorrect. You can't think that people who run businesses are profiteering assholes on one hand, but then also that they're not up to making a ruthless decision based on different tax rates or relationship with government on the other. If I were Zohran, I'd get in front of as many major business leaders as possible and say, look, you may not always get what you want but you'll have a line to my administration, if only to complain. A lot of them want the city's help clearing red tape and whatnot. Giving them face time would get some of them to give you a chance before relocating to Florida.
They're nervous too because Zohran is 34 years old without a ton of managerial experience. A lot of it will be based on who he gets to run the agencies. Zohran for example should ask Police Commissioner Jessica Tisch to stay on. That would inspire a ton of confidence and continuity. Maria Torres-Springer is another administrator with a great reputation for competence. Build a team that signals professionalism and pragmatism as quickly as possible and it would soothe some nerves.
Zohran has said that law enforcement needs help. He has taken the progressive approach of saying we need more mental health professionals and resources, which sounds good. But among police officers themselves, morale is low and more officers are taking early retirement or moving to find work in Long Island or the suburbs. In my view, you need more officers and should go into recruitment mode. I was endorsed by the police captains' union and believed that the police force was key to the city's success. I think culturally that will be a stretch for Zohran.
Zohran has also pledged more resources to combat hate crimes. According to police data, 345 hate crimes were committed against Jews in New York last year, almost one per day. That's compared to 78 based on sexual orientation and 43 against Muslims in the same period.
I was troubled by Zohran's academic defense of the phrase "Globalize the Intifada" because it's a term used by some to justify violence against Jews. My Jewish friends in New York fear that they're more likely to be attacked on the street now than they were a couple years ago, and that this will get much worse under a Mamdani administration.
Unfortunately, I think they're right. Antisemitism is real. There are hateful people who will feel emboldened by Zohran's victory and will use it as an excuse to take out their anger and bigotry on another New Yorker.
This speaks to the fundamental fear I have for Zohran. I believe he is in public service to do good and improve people's lives, which itself might be unusual in this day and age. But in practice the job may become more about trying to keep very bad things from happening in a big, fractious city.
For example, if I had won the mayor's race in 2021, I would have faced some very tough situations. I would have had to curtail services to migrants, saying "Look, we are a good and welcoming city, but we also have limited resources. We cannot expend them on the world's migrants who have come to this country." I would have been attacked as being anti-black and anti-brown. Someone would have beaten up some Asians on the street, because they didn't have a security detail. There are constant trade-offs and no right answer. You just make a call knowing that you might look like shit.
Let's stipulate that Zohran is a good person. This is an era that manipulates us and turns politics into a clash of personalities. Doing what you think is the right thing could feed into the polarization and lead to chaos.
Let's imagine a scenario; ICE raids pick up under Trump in New York City. Videos of cruelty and masked goons grabbing Latinos and Muslims surface. Mayor Mamdani says, "We are a sanctuary city, we do not give our neighbors up!" President Trump relishes a clash and sends in the National Guard to back up ICE, something as happened in Los Angeles but on a bigger scale. The young Mayor refuses to let go of a detainee and is arrested. He is held for days. His followers erupt in protest. Jumaane Williams takes over as acting mayor. While Zohran is held, violence breaks out. Someone's house is burned down. Trump uses the disorder to send in more troops.
During this time, images of riots fill the screens. The police are reluctant to put themselves in harm's way. Attacks on Jews spike. Mayor Mamdani, finally free of ICE, gives an address, saying that "We are better than this. New York is a city of peace and goodwill." But a sense of disorder fills the air. A major firm decides to leave New York as well as hundreds of families. Conditions deteriorate and passions rise.
Being mayor of New York has not been kind to the futures of Eric Adams, Bill DeBlasio, Mike Bloomberg, or Rudy Giuliani. It's the kind of job that eats people up.
One reason some held their nose and voted for Andrew Cuomo was the sense that New York City is a diverse land of 8.2 million packed into a compressed area and a bit of a powderkeg. New York City Democrats, including a ton of new voters, chose a young, idealistic leader. I like Zohran. I hope it works out, for him and the city. But I think Zohran is going to have to adopt some new attributes very quickly - what got him here may not be what he now needs to succeed. It may sound crazy, but winning the race might have been the easy part compared with what's to come.
[Forward](https://substack.com/redirect/c713d45b-7d53-419e-9328-77a354e6ffd6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is trying to reduce polarization around the country. [Offline](https://substack.com/redirect/61d75a6c-31fd-47e5-8323-019fe43226d8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is on the way to Brooklyn, Chicago, SF and LA in November. If you want a break from your wireless bill email [matt@noblemobile.com](https://substack.com/redirect/d36a6b01-4789-4df5-8e40-a11d682780eb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Noble Mobile](https://substack.com/redirect/8b6099d4-3423-4664-befb-d0c5c8ecb07b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) will help you look up
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4845d537-98c0-4db5-9f4c-d9880b04a89d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

While Zohran is held, violence breaks out. Someone's house is burned down. Trump uses the disorder to send in more troops. During this time, images of riots fill the screens. The police are reluctant to put themselves in harm's way. Attacks on Jews spike. Mayor Mamdani, finally free of ICE, gives an address, saying that "We are better than this. New York is a city of peace and goodwill." But a sense of disorder fills the air. A major firm decides to leave New York as well as hundreds of families. Conditions deteriorate and passions rise. Being mayor of New York has not been kind to the futures of Eric Adams, Bill DeBlasio, Mike Bloomberg, or Rudy Giuliani. It's the kind of job that eats people up. One reason some held their nose and voted for Andrew Cuomo was the sense that New York City is a diverse land of 8.2 million packed into a compressed area and a bit of a powderkeg. New York City Democrats, including a ton of new voters, chose a young, idealistic leader. I like Zohran. I hope it works out, for him and the city. But I think Zohran is going to have to adopt some new attributes very quickly - what got him here may not be what he now needs to succeed. It may sound crazy, but winning the race might have been the easy part compared with what's to come. is trying to reduce polarization around the country. is on the way to Brooklyn, Chicago, SF and LA in November. If you want a break from your wireless bill email and will help you look up | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Andrew Yang 111 Sutter Street, 7th Floor, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

name: Chuck Rocha
subject: Can we take a moment to celebrate? 🥳
body:
With so much at stake - we're giving it our all and WINNING. | | | |
---
| | | Friends,

After months of digital advertising, over 100K+ doors knocked in New Jersey, various community events in Virginia and more — all that hard work paid off.

A HUGE thank you and CONGRATULATIONS to Governor-elect Abigail Spanberger, Governor-elect Mikie Sherrill, the New Jersey Democratic State Committee, NextGen America and UnidosUS Action PAC!

We're proud to stand with these leaders and will continue to fight alongside them to deliver the relief and support our communities deserve.

name: DemsGive
subject: While Trump builds his throne, families go hungry
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Protect Our Democracy Fund_
body:
$300 million for a ballroom—during a shutdown.
| | | | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01K9A4N9Z4CQRW5CMQ6G1EZ0TK_0)
---
| | | | Hey Peter, Donald Trump just bulldozed the White House East Wing to build a $300 million ballroom—during a government shutdown. While families struggle to make rent, he’s spending donor cash on marble floors and gold trim. This isn’t leadership. It’s greed and ego. The East Wing once honored FDR’s recovery from polio—now it’s rubble for Trump's playground. We are demanding answers on who paid for it and why. Because the People’s House belongs to all of us—not one man’s vanity project. [**Read more**](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01K9A4N9Z4CQRW5CMQ6G1EZ0TK_1)

– Jesse, DemsGive
---

While families struggle to make rent, he’s spending donor cash on marble floors and gold trim. This isn’t leadership. It’s greed and ego. The East Wing once honored FDR’s recovery from polio—now it’s rubble for Trump's playground. We are demanding answers on who paid for it and why. name: Gavin Newsom
subject: Historic victory
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CAMPAIGN FOR DEMOCRACY PAC
body:
Last night was a great night ... for everyone ... not just the Democratic Party.

**Last night was a great night ... for everyone ... not just the Democratic Party.**
But what a night for the Democratic Party! We are a party on its toes, no longer on its heels -- from coast to coast -- from sea to shining sea.
Here in California, we passed Proposition 50 by an overwhelming margin with a historic turnout. And I want to thank ALL OF YOU who worked so hard to make this victory possible.
If you donated your time or your money, if you lent your voice online and certainly if you mailed-in your ballot or stood in line to vote YES yesterday, what we accomplished was nothing short of extraordinary.
This was not just about drawing lines on a map, it was about holding the line on what makes us who we are.
But none of us can be naive. Donald Trump understands his position and unpopularity, so he will continue to work to rig the midterm elections before one single vote is cast.
We are seeing him take action all across this country: Redrawing maps in Missouri, North Carolina, Indiana and inevitably in Florida. They are not screwing around.
But that is not all.
In June of this year, we saw 4,000 National Guard troops federalized in the state of California. We saw active duty Marines not sent overseas, but sent to the second largest city in the country.
We said it was a preview of things to come. And it is happening now.
When we kicked off this campaign at the Democracy Center, Donald Trump sent his private police force that increasingly appears to have taken an oath to Donald Trump, not the Constitution. He sent them to intimidate.
Just yesterday, in Los Angeles, Donald Trump called up the border patrol and sent them to Dodger Stadium to suppress the vote in America's second largest city. People in tactical gear sent out to intimidate.
But it didn't work.
Yesterday California sent a message to Donald Trump: no crowns, no thrones, No Kings.
So today, I am proud. But I am very mindful about the state of things in this country. Donald Trump does not believe in free and fair elections: period, full stop.
It is not complicated. It is self-evident to anyone paying attention. And it won't stop. This is just a preview of things to come.
My call today is that we all have something to contribute going forward.
We need Virginia ... we need Maryland ... we need our friends in New York and Illinois and Colorado -- we need to see other states meet this moment head-on as well.
To recognize what we're up against in 2026.
Because if we do, we will de facto end Donald Trump's presidency as we know it the moment Speaker Jeffries is sworn in as the next Speaker of the House.
It is all on the line in 2026.
Again, thank you to everyone who stood up not just for our democracy, but for those who are feeling intimidated, for those who are struggling to get by, and for those who believe in the notion of checks and balances.
We won last night.
But the fight goes on.
Gavin Newsom
* * *
i️ To my fellow Democrats — don't scroll past this. We cannot rest after last night's victories. Donald Trump certainly is not. 🇺🇸
We need Virginia ... we need Maryland ... we need our friends in New York and Illinois and Colorado -- we need to see other states meet this moment head-on as well.
God help us if we're not successful. We may have enjoyed our last free and fair election. They're redrawing districts to hold the House; going after ActBlue and Democrats' ability to fundraise; going after colleges and the free press... Donald Trump is not screwing around.
[**Please make a contribution to Gavin Newsom's Campaign for Democracy to help us continue to go on the offense and take the fight to Trump. The amount isn't as important as the donation itself. It all adds up fast and we'll put it right to work to help Democrats defend our democracy everywhere. Thank you.**](https://act.gavinnewsom.com/go/5316?source=em251105-1&t=1&akid=2751%2E4767699%2ElzrrUg)
| | **[CONTRIBUTE $35](https://act.gavinnewsom.com/go/5317?source=em251105-1&t=2&akid=2751%2E4767699%2ElzrrUg)**
---
| **[CONTRIBUTE OTHER](https://act.gavinnewsom.com/go/5316?source=em251105-1&t=3&akid=2751%2E4767699%2ElzrrUg)**
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com . Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.gavinnewsom.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=4&akid=2751%2E4767699%2ElzrrUg).
PAID FOR BY CAMPAIGN FOR DEMOCRACY PAC
1787 Tribute Road, Suite K
Sacramento, CA 95815

This is Devarjaye “DJ” Daniel – one of President Trump’s most trusted bodyguards. ** ****Will you help get my dad Theodis elected to U.S. Congress?**
---
[ SUPPORT MY DAD'S CAMPAIGN ](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/cEslLC7Ubw-rFO87KAHD_T6cKgHwmg_QCr8mXu-8sZdHJet3de6nfOhxxy7LfDSXziVnoaDzSw6P4kB5AIhGTycqgz9w15RBXNjjre8-XGgUn1QQ3J9SpQtfaLo99u2XSSc2UFeWM8lLYtsLhdocKzRJC2bDz1Rfy0UH0UmjtB13ffGbNChBEd-cNuvEtmo6mE4ORf1oYte1z5G6w6cNqASwOkUOMO82-nvrKug1bKnpuCXr6noVDesT4-6IzQNHdLeT4gXC_svJtgYBp0YAduOB51NRIOWcjarLPwUSFGQ5TwnJaDUDS1b_t8pslD1MHdXQE2SRZkFRysi7z2NoD6syg9JcgrEXov9TNzITm2QtRRM1wIHhQsU3IZS_RwHZl6hhGR6htoYjHZefVvimCSVtEy1PcEp13HFanE_3MnDz1nlJFyWGteus9v3RTCy7y5d1YWClrGWSkhFHAhGV2fxos9SSxRHF8SB6Z--qJvSMNJkJwj7VyFs8DN9slPlztQWwbwRgqVLbehNGS--snOh0nrefEPVdWEfyu_iIxRgs0GX2DN4Ujebg57pP-P_BMR2lwDv0LVgNNCPeLM5EUtTaEYL6B7mpy5dxlX-VTzh4voXZ1rx2nxY6JEC3EHbPb-dzqFkK_EgPWWPQuZlES_CoyxGb-GCxK454F6E4PEV8MEZnepE1ttHtSGC-c-TbkSHo2nhLI9TtX6TUXBshE_US66Blk1Kpwqo8Jr9ZEj9z8Gmbnc67dNkgS9Gt4WUlhlQdPRh8vVTYmQD_75D3XngKFvklp5TeK7ECMac/Wr9hfqhvlDpRO4Z0_s2yNrEs9CYYFOEa/14)
---
You may have heard our story before. _When I was 6 years old I started seizing up._ **Brain cancer.** My dad rushed me to the hospital and they told me they needed to do surgery that night. **They even said I had 5 months left to live.** **I’m 13 years old now.** And as God is my witness, _I’m going to keep on going until my gas tank runs out._ You never know when God is going to call you home. That’s something you don’t usually hear from a terminally ill child. But my dad told me to always lean on Jesus when times get rough... **And Jesus tells me to always honor and support my father.**
---
[](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/mydy7VhgAjxG2K3XQbxsx1I8me8n8-mjoWHe7L_3_toph4-c4sYFCoFpS0trFyIUhqj8aZlH3Z7y5DZNKQu5hhegbfYS8fK8PeCXFN379p3sRc4OeceTrT3k8_ednL4A3KOtTbLU6wLXi3bMGCvx1gnzEQlYTrYDT9uE2iaPnk78aIdISMl-Kqni2bS4o0_Et0eWXOHlm9lnre_m0pch44F7tokOBjJJCh50xs1-3WWAYq8FMk5jmFWju3yO8WMyoNXapON9JuxUcgehZtkIJzXzLKHHJQyrCxP_3LPu8iEyd4GS8dS-JwPf9H5WC_qJCDlZYZMKZWpVcB4s9i0yhYeTv9jOQCAOucVHusJXQu6EpKLaZkKB27UTtuH-8qVF2WfGfI0alJqQUDrtRlbGW-6Xh-CVDLTp2eb_EFP73WzKM4QYlzpkxXJJNEFZ96XUgYGiSl0FXP7ve2Nddnypmsa5Gxx0nOzd8cF83H31NDPZ4FwmqkoEVR-pQyWB0cL8Hyb0EpDbWUGF_g97E98-CAVASAgNfry_gH3cVJTSHIiG8DM7zk8ZCth5jEhOK46nh0c242ZUFcAW1xTZmyvhBayKpS3txKbAlyHjBH8QGKNONpa4bJeFpZWytp6sQJOvtob4p-O4mbXw_dpusO6UXgp2KETAeV9Y_0k3s_Ca7EIfcHpON0AdiyHsBKqxQmqJRz-z3-CFUb4Hijo3ek9mGVM_Of19FGLhvAODjr0n88TXh8uz44xWCxpyBqpqTARN_9lO2gxFh-D1cAv0hswAtct63kGnqnkj6HfZBnw/OIhwBzhsZIOyM5Jtbsa3IVGH35X89TjW/14)
---
**So that’s exactly what I’m doing today.** **My dad Theodis is running for****Congress as a Republican.** I don’t understand all of it, but he tells me that the politicians we elect are _corrupted by money and almost never good listeners._ _I can assure you my dad isn’t like that._ He never gave up on me – even after 13 brain surgeries, expensive hospital bills, and tons of stress. And the worst part is, now that he’s running for Congress, I see these _**crazy people ripping up his yard signs and saying all of these nasty things about him.**_ I really don’t appreciate that. My dad is a good man. An honest man. And I wouldn’t be alive today if it weren’t for him. **The same goes for President Trump. **

clean_body: | | | This is Devarjaye “DJ” Daniel – one of President Trump’s most trusted bodyguards. Will you help get my dad Theodis elected to U.S. Congress? You may have heard our story before. When I was 6 years old I started seizing up. Brain cancer. My dad rushed me to the hospital and they told me they needed to do surgery that night. They even said I had 5 months left to live. I’m 13 years old now. And as God is my witness, I’m going to keep on going until my gas tank runs out. You never know when God is going to call you home. That’s something you don’t usually hear from a terminally ill child. But my dad told me to always lean on Jesus when times get rough... And Jesus tells me to always honor and support my father. So that’s exactly what I’m doing today. My dad Theodis is running forCongress as a Republican. I don’t understand all of it, but he tells me that the politicians we elect are corrupted by money and almost never good listeners. I can assure you my dad isn’t like that. He never gave up on me – even after 13 brain surgeries, expensive hospital bills, and tons of stress. And the worst part is, now that he’s running for Congress, I see these crazy people ripping up his yard signs and saying all of these nasty things about him. I really don’t appreciate that. My dad is a good man. An honest man. And I wouldn’t be alive today if it weren’t for him. The same goes for President Trump. Now that my dad is running for Congress,I’m asking you to be there for him the same way he has always been there for me. We don’t come from a lot of money or anything like that. But we do love this country, and we want to serve the people well. I’m honored to be sworn in as an honorary member of over 900 law enforcement agencies. I couldn’t believe it when President Trump let me join****theSecret Service too. That’s over 901 agencies now. I hope I get to 1,000 soon. But more than that, I really hope to see my dad go to Washington D.C. and help President Trump turn this country around. That would make me a very proud son. After all, I’ve seen the way some of these people treat our police officers. I’ve seen how violence has taken over our streets. I’ve seen how mean and rude many of these Democrat politicians are to folks like you. They refused to stand for me, Laken Riley, and Jocelyn Nungaray when President Trump told our stories in front of Congress. That idiot woman Jasmine Crockett even bullied me for my brain surgery scars! I’m just a 13 year old kid for crying out loud! That said, it would mean the world to me if you joined “Team Daniel for Congress” today. My dad is a fighter. I think I inherited those genes from him. He will NEVER give up on you... The same way he NEVER gave up on me. Will you please support my dad’s campaign for Congress by rushing over $5 or more? God Bless America, Devarjaye “DJ” Daniel Honorary U.S. Secret Service Agent Son of Theodis Daniel for Congress. Good morning, my friend. It’s Jane Fonda with the first wave of election results from last night... and it’s good news! 🎉 As of 8:00AM ET, here’s where we stand: ✅ **In Virginia, we FLIPPED nine highly competitive Republican-held districts for the House of Delegates** — expanding our climate majority and electing **Ghazala Hashimi** , the first Muslim woman to hold statewide office, as the next Lieutenant Governor. ✅ **In Georgia, we OVERPERFORMED in one of the toughest battlegrounds in the country** , where JanePAC-endorsed candidate **Alicia Johnson** flipped the Georgia Public Service Commission, District 2, marking Democrats’ _first statewide victory in a non-federal race since 2006_. ✅ **In Pennsylvania** , we said we were coming to flip the **Luzerne County Council** , and we did. What began as a 10-1 Republican supermajority in 2023 is now a solidly pro-environment majority in 2025. ✅ **In Mississippi, Democrats broke the Republican supermajority in the State Senate**. JanePAC-endorsed candidate **Johnny DuPree** flipped his seat from red to blue and played a fundamental role in shifting the balance of power in a deep-red state. That energy is undeniable. You can sure as hell see it, and I can feel it in my bones. From Virginia to Pennsylvania, voters are demanding a new kind of leadership. **Leadership that’s accountable to the _people_ , not corporations.** **Leadership that _inspires_ , instead of dividing.** **Leadership that _dreams_ big, and delivers.** And we hear them loud and clear. I couldn’t be prouder of the candidates we’ve endorsed and of the grassroots movement we’ve built to make days like these possible. Because this work was never mine, or yours, alone. It’s the work of millions of ordinary people doing something extraordinary: turning pain into purpose, despair into determination, and fear into collective power. Wherever you call home, these victories are _your_ victories. But our story doesn’t end when the polls close. Ballots are still being counted in dozens of races, and our team is already preparing for recounts, runoffs, and yes, the 2026 midterm elections. **So before we catch our breath, I have to ask:[Will you help us keep the momentum going by making your very own donation to my Climate PAC today? ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118698645/580603448/2105508926?refcode=ea.aba1317c-53ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&ms=ea.aba1317c-53ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImFiYTEzMTdjLTUzYmEtZjAxMS04ZTYxLTAwMjI0ODI0ZTA4ZCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=NQGlzREts_ala8IbIcw8MikviM5qDmV-4NSpxxAc6Gw=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [**DONATE $5**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118698646/580603449/2105508926?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=ea.aba1317c-53ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&ms=ea.aba1317c-53ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImFiYTEzMTdjLTUzYmEtZjAxMS04ZTYxLTAwMjI0ODI0ZTA4ZCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=NQGlzREts_ala8IbIcw8MikviM5qDmV-4NSpxxAc6Gw=) | [**DONATE $25**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118698647/580603450/2105508926?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=ea.aba1317c-53ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&ms=ea.aba1317c-53ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImFiYTEzMTdjLTUzYmEtZjAxMS04ZTYxLTAwMjI0ODI0ZTA4ZCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=NQGlzREts_ala8IbIcw8MikviM5qDmV-4NSpxxAc6Gw=)
