name: Ilhan Omar
subject: Zohran Mamdani
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
Zohran charted a new path forward for the Democratic party. Let me explain.
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=1944%2E791427%2E2y7FHy)
[](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-nov25.AgDe-P&refcode=em251105-1-180&t=2&akid=1944%2E791427%2E2y7FHy)
Friend, last night Zohran Mamdani officially became Mayor-elect of New York City.
It’s an incredible achievement — Zohran started at 1% in the polls. He was told he had no shot. He was told a progressive, Muslim, immigrant man could not win.
**He proved them all wrong.**
And in doing so Zohran charted a new path forward for the Democratic party. Let me explain.
Since our devastating losses to the far-right in 2024, Democrats have tried to figure out **why.** Polls show that approval ratings for the Democratic Party are at all time lows, even as Trump moves ahead with his extremely unpopular agenda.
One poll of working-class voters showed that one of the first words that they associated with our party was “weak”. We play it too safe. We don’t show fire. We don’t reflect the anger that regular people feel when the system is stacked against them. In many ways, our party is lost in the wilderness.
The politics that propelled Zohran to victory are a true antidote to this situation: Positive, bold, ideas-driven, and focused on the needs of the working class instead of the billionaire class. He’s generated so much excitement among regular folks at a time when Democrats need it most.
But the fact that party leaders refused to endorse Zohran, or waited until the last minute to show their half-hearted support for his campaign, really shows where the problem lies.
**If Democrats continue to be too afraid to get behind bold ideas and working-class politics, we will continue to lose.** If we sacrifice a truly progressive agenda to appease billionaire donors and mythical “moderate Republicans”, we will leave behind millions of young people, working people, and folks who simply don’t see a reason to vote and decide to stay home — the exact groups that Democrats lost tons of support from in 2024.
Friend — When I first ran for Congress in 2018, I faced many of the same challenges as Zohran. So did AOC, Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib. So did Bernie Sanders when he ran for president in two historic campaigns. Our entire movement has faced pushback from powerful voices in the Democratic party who are invested in the status quo.
But now, as Trump’s fascist agenda is exposing the ineffectiveness of the establishment wing of the Democratic party, more and more folks are realizing the truth: **Progressive, working class politics are the only chance that Democrats have at winning over Americans and defeating Trump.**
That’s why I’m fighting every day to not just put forward a progressive agenda that speaks directly to the skyrocketing cost of living, but to push our party in that direction too.
So if you want to see more Zohrans, more Bernies, and more Democratic victories in the years to come, please:
**[Make a contribution to our movement today and help us continue to build a progressive, working-class campaign that can go head to head with Trump and WIN. We have a responsibility to do away with the tired, old approaches that have given us Trump 2.0 and Project 2025. We can’t afford more of the same when our futures are on the line.**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-nov25.AgDe-P&refcode=em251105-1-180&t=3&akid=1944%2E791427%2E2y7FHy)
_If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your contribution will go through immediately:_
| | **[Donate $10](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/771?c0=io-em-nov25.7QWUq1&refcode=em251105-1-180&t=22&akid=1944%2E791427%2E2y7FHy)**
---
****
| **[Donate $25](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/15?c0=io-em-nov25.BW1jq7&refcode=em251105-1-180&t=23&akid=1944%2E791427%2E2y7FHy)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $50](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/8?c0=io-em-nov25.r_en0_&refcode=em251105-1-180&t=24&akid=1944%2E791427%2E2y7FHy)**
---
****
| **[Donate $100](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/9?c0=io-em-nov25.-j9utF&refcode=em251105-1-180&t=25&akid=1944%2E791427%2E2y7FHy)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $250](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/10?c0=io-em-nov25.OxS4pY&refcode=em251105-1-180&t=26&akid=1944%2E791427%2E2y7FHy)**
---
****
| **[Other Amount](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/13?c0=io-em-nov25.pfG4Bl&refcode=em251105-1-180&t=27&akid=1944%2E791427%2E2y7FHy)**
---
****
In solidarity,
Ilhan

You can also **[sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/fewer-emails/?source=em-footer&t=29&akid=1944%2E791427%2E2y7FHy)**.
Submit this form to [**update your contact information**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/update-contact-info/source=em-footer?t=30&akid=1944%2E791427%2E2y7FHy)
Ilhan is counting on our people-powered grassroots movement to fund this campaign. If you can, stand with us by **[making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/io-em-footer/?refcode=em-footer&t=31&refcode2=1944_791427_2y7FHy&akid=1944%2E791427%2E2y7FHy)**.
Ilhan for Congress

Washington D.C., 20033

Parler
https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=baa222512c0202f685fab8f8fc508439c1aac6cf0974af6a943a663688c4b2cfbd4e5306f06db9def12269ded45f884dd6981898a0ae5c1d178747fc5e4419c8

https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=baa222512c0202f685fab8f8fc508439c1aac6cf0974af6a943a663688c4b2cfbd4e5306f06db9def12269ded45f884dd6981898a0ae5c1d178747fc5e4419c8

You helped build Parler. Now you can own a piece of it.

We believe that Parler isn’t just a company, we’re a movement empowering the next generation of creators with the world’s first fully owned stack of cloud, commerce, payments, and media.

Today, we’re launching

https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=baa222512c0202f685fab8f8fc508439c1aac6cf0974af6a943a663688c4b2cfbd4e5306f06db9def12269ded45f884dd6981898a0ae5c1d178747fc5e4419c8

a unique investment opportunity to own a piece of the creator economy.

Our “Private Placement Offering” is your chance to invest in helping build the infrastructure for a new creator driven economy and consists of shares of the Company’s Series A 7% Convertible Preferred Stock for a maximum Offering amount of up to $50 million.

Based on investment amount, Preferred Round investors also receive up to a 20% Parler Ads Network credit, potentially creating some value through marketing reach and ecosystem impact.

The creator economy is exploding, and your investment will help fuel expansion of the Pulse ecosystem, a creator-centered, next-generation digital platform that unites social engagement, streaming video, e-commerce, and decentralized financial tools. ### Voters turned out in big numbers to rebuke Trump.
| | [Patrick J. Murphy](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---
| Nov 5
---
| [](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=178080670&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MDgwNjcwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2MjM1NDY0OCwiZXhwIjoxNzY0OTQ2NjQ4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDM5OTEzMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Crw2PV-M951ulE3MPgpM7_nQsOEerkDlRJhV5sAE1jU&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=178080670&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MDgwNjcwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjIzNTQ2NDgsImV4cCI6MTc2NDk0NjY0OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.xHI3u0s9GVN9wITpFz-S4QnNkiyAuYFTCws_mkldZjQ&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/another-change-election?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7f9f168a-461a-4e94-8f9c-ce717680feb9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Yesterday, millions of Americans made their voices heard at the polls.
It was a big night for Democrats, and a likely preview of what we could see in the 2026 midterms.
That these elections happened against the backdrop of a government shutdown makes it all the more significant. The Senate failed for the 14th time to get the votes to pass a short-term funding deal, making this the longest shutdown in U.S. history, [surpassing the previous 35-day record](https://substack.com/redirect/a5b639c6-1367-4165-9f3f-3231a7e320c7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) set during Trump's first term in 2019. Each passing week means more federal workers go without paychecks, and more than 40 million people who rely on SNAP benefits struggle to put food on the table.
These are just some of the things that were on voters' minds.
It's important to remember that politics is cyclical. Exhibit A:
When I won reelection to Congress in 2008 by 15 points, I thought we were riding a wave of hope boosted by Obama's historic election. It felt like a new era for Democrats.
That high didn't last long, though.
A year later, there were some early warning signs that Republicans were mounting backlash. In 2009, Republican David Heckler won Bucks County's District Attorney's race by double digits despite facing a younger, better-funded Democratic former prosecutor, Chris Asplen. That same cycle, the GOP reclaimed two governorships (Virginia and New Jersey), foreshadowing what became a red tsunami in 2010 with the rise of the Tea Party. Democrats lost 63 seats in the midterms and the Republicans regained control of the House. I was one of the Democrats who got unseated.
All of this is to say, in politics, the tide can turn fast.
After the Republicans took back the White House in 2024 and kept the majority in the House and Senate, the tide shifted last night toward Democrats, driven by growing dissatisfaction with Trump 2.0. The momentum had been building for weeks; nearly seven million Americans joined the recent "No Kings" rallies protesting Trump's authoritarianism, which was two million more than the first demonstrations held in June.
To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..paWOYjsS-IRvmtk7wiIiOq0doZEQCQxX3uzNY73LNsg?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=178080670)
# History Made
In New Jersey, Democratic Rep. **Mikie Sherrill** defeated Trump-backed Republican Jack Ciattarelli, becoming the state's first woman Democratic governor. A former Navy helicopter pilot, Sherrill was first elected to the House as part of the 2018 blue wave midterms.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c64a5f6d-4bab-439a-b800-dc4336b2bb76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In Virginia, Democratic Rep. **Abigail Spanberger** defeated Republican Winsome Earle-Sears, the sitting lieutenant governor, becoming the **first woman ever elected governor of Virginia**. Like Sherrill, Spanberger first won her congressional seat in the 2018 midterms. Both would become roommates on Capitol Hill.
# NYC
Turnout shattered expectations across the country. In New York City alone, more than 735,000 people voted early--over four times the total from the 2021 mayoral race. By the end of the night, two million New Yorkers had cast ballots, marking the city's [highest turnout for a local election since 1969.](https://substack.com/redirect/9f79c21b-7977-4714-bab2-dec9e56c7540?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The New York City mayor's race especially energized Gen Z voters who turned out in large numbers for **Zohran Mamdani** , the 34-year-old democratic socialist whose successful campaign has captivated attention far beyond the five boroughs. His meteoric rise from an obscure assemblyman, bolstered by a polished social media strategy, grassroots energy, and focus on affordability, will likely become a case study for future Democrats looking to channel that same passion.
# PA
In Pennsylvania, all three Democratic Supreme Court justices who sought reelection--Christine Donohue, Kevin Dougherty, and David Wecht--were retained by voters, preserving the court's **5 -2 Democratic majority** through the 2028 election cycle.
I couldn't be prouder that Army veterans Danny Ceisler and Siddiq Kamara--two candidates endorsed by my PAC, Kennedy Dems PA--won their races for county sheriff in Bucks County and Delaware County, respectively. We also got a clean sweep for DA, row officers, and judges, including Tiffany Thomas-Smith, a military mom who made history as the first Black woman ever elected judge in Bucks County.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b9994864-4894-418e-abf5-03031ba2559e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2b70d90d-4172-4459-9da8-2bdd35fb490e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
At Danny Ceisler's victory party for Bucks County Sheriff with him and his father, Larry.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/bb955c49-e6ce-4182-8989-4162b384eb71?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
These elections reaffirmed my faith that voters care about people who show what courage and decency in public service look like. And that integrity still matters.
As we look towards the midterms in 2026, it's important to remember that change doesn't start in Washington. It starts at the ballot box and with who we send to represent us.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4a6b5617-d231-4739-a9be-4253b81609a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
# Change Elections Will Continue
When you don't trust the government, you throw them out. Neither political party is popular with the American people and has been voted out 11 of the 13 U.S. midterm or presidential elections this century, including the last six in a row.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/68ff4f54-ef01-4388-a095-5daf02380712?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6c54684f-2fe1-4fb4-92ab-d88c753c8e07?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Now is the time for our Democrats and Republicans to govern together, end the shutdown, get a hold of the spike in prices (especially energy & groceries), and restore trust with the millions of Americans in public service working without paychecks-- including our federal prison guards, TSA agents, and air traffic controllers. If the leaders in Washington don't wake up, put their political party aside, and start working for all of us, they'll be swept out soon enough.
# Moving Forward - Focus on the Economy, Healthcare, & Affordability
Energy prices have risen 11% nationally under Trump since last year, and in New Jersey and Virginia, soaring energy prices led to Democratic wins with candidates who promised to make it more affordable:
New Jersey residents saw a [22.21%](https://substack.com/redirect/16a1aaea-72cd-4845-9c9f-2cc318b2eca4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) increase between July 2024 and 2025.
Virginians saw an average [11.25%](https://substack.com/redirect/16a1aaea-72cd-4845-9c9f-2cc318b2eca4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) increase in their utility bills since last year.
Our two national security governors-elect in NJ & VA successfully positioned energy prices as a central pillar of their affordability-focused campaign. Sherrill and Spanberger both outlined a clear path to lower costs while holding Republicans accountable for their harmful agenda that cut alternative energy sources like wind and solar.
[Exit polls in New Jersey](https://substack.com/redirect/674b74a5-b307-4133-86f8-32566d08ae5c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) found that 59% of voters viewed electricity costs as a major problem and only 12% said no problem at all.
Whether you call it affordability or _prices are just too damn high_ , there's a lot we should be doing to drive down costs for the American people. That starts with reopening the government and extending the ACA subsidies before healthcare premiums spike in 2026 to levels many Americans simply can't afford.
# Young Voters
In 2024, Republicans made gains with [young voters ](https://substack.com/redirect/f3140232-5006-4f38-acb2-5abc2b33a9ad?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)(18-29) based on their economic concerns. You've heard me speak about how young men are especially feeling the pain - [nearly 60% of men 18-24 still live at home](https://substack.com/redirect/438a5cc1-38f9-4f2a-b4b2-b2bed39c705c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and far too many don't believe in the American Dream.

Last night, young voters swung back in support of Democrats.
In Virginia, Abigail Spanberger won young voters (18-29) 2 to 1 compared to her Republican opponent and generated higher turnout among this group compared to the last gubernatorial race. In New Jersey, Mikie Sherrill also dominated among young voters -- winning 66% to 32%.
But don't forget Independents - 90% of Democrats and Republicans vote party line, making Independents the key demographic in purple counties (like Bucks) and states (like PA). 53% of veterans identify as independents: In both races for governor, Democrats won a majority of independent voters.
In Virginia, Abigail Spanberger won independents by 15 points -- a 24-point swing compared to 4 years ago. In New Jersey, Mikie Sherrill won the independent vote by 7 points.
While I often say it's always darkest before dawn, it's time to work on answering the call in our US Constitution and form a more perfect union. We can do that by working together and ending this tragic shutdown.

Annual paid subscribers in the U.S. will receive a signed copy of my 2008 memoir, (shipping not available to Alaska, Hawaii, and U.S. territories). Voters turned out in big numbers to rebuke Trump. | | | Nov 5 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- Yesterday, millions of Americans made their voices heard at the polls. It was a big night for Democrats, and a likely preview of what we could see in the 2026 midterms. That these elections happened against the backdrop of a government shutdown makes it all the more significant. The Senate failed for the 14th time to get the votes to pass a short-term funding deal, making this the longest shutdown in U.S. history, set during Trump's first term in 2019. Each passing week means more federal workers go without paychecks, and more than 40 million people who rely on SNAP benefits struggle to put food on the table. These are just some of the things that were on voters' minds. It's important to remember that politics is cyclical. Exhibit A: When I won reelection to Congress in 2008 by 15 points, I thought we were riding a wave of hope boosted by Obama's historic election. It felt like a new era for Democrats. That high didn't last long, though. A year later, there were some early warning signs that Republicans were mounting backlash. In 2009, Republican David Heckler won Bucks County's District Attorney's race by double digits despite facing a younger, better-funded Democratic former prosecutor, Chris Asplen. That same cycle, the GOP reclaimed two governorships (Virginia and New Jersey), foreshadowing what became a red tsunami in 2010 with the rise of the Tea Party. Democrats lost 63 seats in the midterms and the Republicans regained control of the House. I was one of the Democrats who got unseated. All of this is to say, in politics, the tide can turn fast. After the Republicans took back the White House in 2024 and kept the majority in the House and Senate, the tide shifted last night toward Democrats, driven by growing dissatisfaction with Trump 2.0. The momentum had been building for weeks; nearly seven million Americans joined the recent "No Kings" rallies protesting Trump's authoritarianism, which was two million more than the first demonstrations held in June. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. History Made In New Jersey, Democratic Rep. Mikie Sherrill defeated Trump-backed Republican Jack Ciattarelli, becoming the state's first woman Democratic governor. A former Navy helicopter pilot, Sherrill was first elected to the House as part of the 2018 blue wave midterms. | | ---|---|--- In Virginia, Democratic Rep. Abigail Spanberger defeated Republican Winsome Earle-Sears, the sitting lieutenant governor, becoming the first woman ever elected governor of Virginia. Like Sherrill, Spanberger first won her congressional seat in the 2018 midterms. Both would become roommates on Capitol Hill. NYC Turnout shattered expectations across the country. In New York City alone, more than 735,000 people voted early--over four times the total from the 2021 mayoral race. By the end of the night, two million New Yorkers had cast ballots, marking the city's The New York City mayor's race especially energized Gen Z voters who turned out in large numbers for Zohran Mamdani , the 34-year-old democratic socialist whose successful campaign has captivated attention far beyond the five boroughs. His meteoric rise from an obscure assemblyman, bolstered by a polished social media strategy, grassroots energy, and focus on affordability, will likely become a case study for future Democrats looking to channel that same passion. PA In Pennsylvania, all three Democratic Supreme Court justices who sought reelection--Christine Donohue, Kevin Dougherty, and David Wecht--were retained by voters, preserving the court's 5 -2 Democratic majority** through the 2028 election cycle. I couldn't be prouder that Army veterans Danny Ceisler and Siddiq Kamara--two candidates endorsed by my PAC, Kennedy Dems PA--won their races for county sheriff in Bucks County and Delaware County, respectively. We also got a clean sweep for DA, row officers, and judges, including Tiffany Thomas-Smith, a military mom who made history as the first Black woman ever elected judge in Bucks County. | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- At Danny Ceisler's victory party for Bucks County Sheriff with him and his father, Larry. | | ---|---|--- These elections reaffirmed my faith that voters care about people who show what courage and decency in public service look like. And that integrity still matters. As we look towards the midterms in 2026, it's important to remember that change doesn't start in Washington. It starts at the ballot box and with who we send to represent us. | | ---|---|--- Change Elections Will Continue When you don't trust the government, you throw them out. Neither political party is popular with the American people and has been voted out 11 of the 13 U.S. midterm or presidential elections this century, including the last six in a row. | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- Now is the time for our Democrats and Republicans to govern together, end the shutdown, get a hold of the spike in prices (especially energy & groceries), and restore trust with the millions of Americans in public service working without paychecks-- including our federal prison guards, TSA agents, and air traffic controllers. If the leaders in Washington don't wake up, put their political party aside, and start working for all of us, they'll be swept out soon enough. name: Mark Ronchetti
subject: Last night was a disaster. Except Albuquerque.
body:
Dems swept the country - but Darren White has a real shot at Keller

| | | | | |
---
[|
---](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=cd453da4b0&e=567664f14c)
| | | | | |
Election night just gave us the clearest signal yet: Americans are desperate for change. Some places got it right. Others elected a socialist who wants government-run grocery stores. [We break down what happened](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=5fa194984c&e=567664f14c), what it means, and why Albuquerque has a real shot at firing Tim Keller.
---
[|
---](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=bd9e232c22&e=567664f14c)
| |
🔥 **ALBUQUERQUE GETS A SECOND CHANCE
** The numbers tell a story Tim Keller doesn't want you to hear:

✅ Keller finished at 36% after spending 2-3X what Darren White spent

✅ 65% of voters rejected him after 8 years in office

✅ Darren White hit 31% - outperforming partisan registration by at least 6 points

✅ December 9th runoff is now a one-on-one fight

Spoiler alert: We reveal why Albuquerque business owners were terrified to donate to Darren's campaign, what Keller does to people who oppose him, and the exact dollar amount they need to raise in the next 3 weeks to have a fighting chance.

Plus: Jay McCluskey (Darren's consultant) explains why incumbents HATE runoffs and what the path to victory looks like. Jessica Perez (who's raised millions for campaigns) breaks down the brutal reality of fundraising when the mayor retaliates against donors.

This is winnable. But only if you understand what comes next.
---
| [Stream Episode Now](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=e50e4ab4a3&e=567664f14c)
---
| |
🗽 **NYC JUST ELECTED A SOCIALIST MAYOR**
Zohran Mamdani won. A guy who wants government-run grocery stores. Who has praised anti-Israel positions. Who promises "affordable housing" with math that doesn't work.
We expose why this happened - and why it's a warning sign about how desperate voters are becoming when they feel the system has failed them. Mark makes the case that this isn't about socialism winning. It's about hopelessness spreading.
You need to hear this conversation.

🚨 **DEMOCRATS CRUSHED REPUBLICANS NATIONWIDE**
Virginia, New Jersey, Georgia, California - Democrats won everywhere. We break down each race and reveal the one thing Republicans refuse to acknowledge: the economy is destroying them.
Spoiler: Trump's approval is underwater. Republicans control the White House, House, and Senate. They own the results now. We explain what candidates running in 2026 need to learn from last night - and why localizing campaigns matters more than ever.
Plus: The Virginia attorney general race where words apparently don't matter anymore. Jay Jones threatened violence against a political opponent's family and still won. We discuss what that means for the future of political discourse.
---
| [Stream Episode Now](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=7d763bb85e&e=567664f14c)
---
| |
**👉 Stream the full election night special now**[**here**](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=5d64b5978c&e=567664f14c)
We recorded this at midnight with special guests who are living this fight. You'll get the inside story on what really happened, what the data shows, and what has to happen in the next 34 days.
---
|
---
| | ✅ **LIKE** the episode on [YouTube](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=90fb0570ab&e=567664f14c)[
](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=9feeb5790c&e=567664f14c)
✅ **SUBSCRIBE** to [our channel](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=12241a4183&e=567664f14c) and hit the 🔔 for updates

✅ **RATE & REVIEW** us on [your favorite podcast platform](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=3d6a721a9e&e=567664f14c)

✅ **DONATE** to support independent, fearless commentary at: 🌐 [NoDoubtAboutItPodcast.com](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=9b2187ae3b&e=567664f14c)

Thank you for being part of this community — real talk, real issues, and no spin.

**Mark & Krysty Ronchetti **
_No Doubt About It Podcast 🎙️_

name: Speaker Mike Johnson
subject: I just walked out of a meeting…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mike Johnson for Louisiana
body:
| | | [](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/MgzejmR4xDIUQp9548NFW4KgYkvBieuR0i9RJ69dJV2jn_CRETJ0ryxE1_VOnn0gBVRyeMsb3JMCJwF8wvCfUDIxBlsdynWOXt9sU0Ko1TwGd772IhpFAxDO9cB5NRaZcnV0SXho4Ag1S9vJcDJPWMMEOtlV_uvHpi0dZDdI2wLP4bZSJKA_KGSvVAhxar5mzZP2BXjyDG1cLjy_eKvRGMPu7xmzdfyEQNH98jq12_6rpM1odb0C_46850q2RzoXKkV0UsbO8QAQwCc42ksZbbGXMlp9keJoLPlqgz2PhuArCkBRmqhAxtO7VDp2FWrhAzJfGF28gP_mlcpr8xUtnnyL39p-VE0svI-mpjqdBMPoOphN_I8H44CcQscPWCjD6M34Wng-Bxi0fEWSAbN2x74NmLDO-aLEVoLfpwAgYWJaLyx3eyiAQ3YvQpNCPsBDUheogXKaCnyfC5aVf9HgMPK3SYZhKabXg1Do8P3yJW7GqxhQsEujfgQI8vgGLZAPVchBjaFgC9zL-9XP57Fisi-BXf0apyMZkY_NwzCfHJBxB2g01tHzbXDSIOEWyTCVMzMIlXjY5SOuwDzoP5GoDCislMXJmk1NvN0KGQePJf9Xf3-hL5i7-oZZttKbVMHA90kSpsXSLuPoQXUdrQoa-FD6TRiwT0B35GBKM-hrvSKWMmcovuMiB_uoJzPsywILemnbRhSFenSz2uG1MIxspAWL40vRTCh2TBwt7NpPd0YIm9XKn9xc9NjWWGS9ZC44uU2fTqDK4kQ2LkUDUWA5JlVtUF0q8-hSAmLbLV5K0ZwXcLq6dtbkTeg4h3rg7jU1mAjCp4WpGh0Ci_2V4q1z_S3kBOxtFa463avPz0x3R9k9Eu-ggA_ur8AhRFHXRMcwF1V9vyQTG2r-pILRL8i7fhG4uRaXgYapKX4jhMhErlh8LOu7d7YDWK2VJU9fKixE17tLEEVJXa9LYzHvUhTvhe5QchVOrqu9SnxF9x9y5R25wNdB1qKRnthAJG94B5lyeHZ-fm0YkdQRgwqYnAAjP0HddEWBRecSSHxWrALW8EIAMswZc7eJyESRpBRoktblogh1xjlyhHLoQvPFFu1yXVDhOTqosQNCL6mXdFk/Xl70JelgvuUkbwXn5vlOhzyzeNVjAmjC/14)
---
Peter, This is Mike Johnson. I just walked out of a meeting with my senior staff, and I’ll be honest with you – it was concerning. The numbers they showed me have me worried. I knew I couldn’t sit on this, so I’ve written a note just for you. name: =?utf-8?Q?Cry_for_help_=F0=9F=92=94?=
subject: Lauren Boebert is pleading for your help
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
Boebert’s recent behavior suggests she is not at all interested in being reelected, she just doesn’t have the courage to admit it.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001xHBVgad4LwiPrepWbmRBLSFZeSSI4VTve-AGg4R9YpCFZryumC40d_zqJqhl0eIJMPC7_WMSzUMqbqMX1U1RecgkJ4kvvPoFBTTl0fTzhSyEgygCMCwUB-M1CF0zGrY2RGtheFE3pYbTAqCtWwtiFBNYLraF4mjmLikA61CuhLbazoFUZaOXtnoAFuIw_llFXii2wn2UAel2bQ70FlkX13nGoT9-F2wZ&c=bLeh0F6LQGNz-piokHWl9ZihGie1OEq9JHUs6xIqKYhWtA-xo_eF2w==&ch=35sWulebvS9uZbYA0nss-zvBWpK9UBIb7BflP_Gtd82SEewM5lhSGA==)
---
|

Peter,
Here on Team Trisha, our boss has made it clear that we NEVER turn our backs on someone who's suffering. And the recent behaviour of our opponent has us seriously concerned.
Just in the last few weeks, Boebert:

* Only raised $100,000 dollars last quarter (5 times less than us).

* Launched her reelection bid with UFOs as the centerpiece of her platform.

* Forgot what district she represented in a public statement.

* Purposefully mocked thousands of her Latino and Chicano constituents by wearing a Mexican blackface costume.

It's almost as if she's trying to lose. 🧐
That's why we've come to the conclusion that Boebert's recent behavior suggests she is not at all interested in being reelected, she just doesn't have the courage to admit it.
We get it, from her perspective making laws in Congress is probably sooooo boring--that's why she misses so many votes and is focusing her energy on "fun" stuff like UFOs and reptilians.
And if she quit today, she could easily get a cushy job as a Fox News pundit, or start a podcast with her BFF George Santos, all sorts of things!
But we also know how hard it is to manifest the courage to quit your job, and we really really do empathize with that.
That's why, as Democrats, we think it would be cruel to let Boebert suffer in silence. So we are going to answer her cry for help, and we asking you to join us in showing Boebert the ultimate act of kindness so that she can pursue what her heart truly desires:

body:
Boebert’s recent behavior suggests she is not at all interested in being reelected, she just doesn’t have the courage to admit it.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001FPNuK2Z_sEz2XcVvpe9lb2u8z-o5S0oz7HBHKcBqqoQEPlckWERXt-WXPVTe2wnHoWNu83XtFQdSHMs5xOjitQscQPgr0KPnZ9u_HZXdmODuOyuq6wA7EifqTkOj6Jtj14leLl4zwA-YUiRCeZ0ciOkycthp4V7lzul9koAmY9T_oSaOi29PG6my4t-J4rMZNG21kKVHDi1VHADDiWSHIcah9ZALAUCE&c=Lwn0VtdGxtBknhREpoyYNzTEN6Lpz5AmX4scZUkswK4OrCYxeZvBoQ==&ch=9oGW3qty4texyw6c7c0V0oAH3sPHANXbUtwCgWVMwoWuxSdealquFg==)

Peter,
Here on Team Trisha, our boss has made it clear that we NEVER turn our backs on someone who's suffering. And the recent behaviour of our opponent has us seriously concerned.
Just in the last few weeks, Boebert:

* Only raised $100,000 dollars last quarter (5 times less than us).

* Launched her reelection bid with UFOs as the centerpiece of her platform.

* Forgot what district she represented in a public statement.

* Purposefully mocked thousands of her Latino and Chicano constituents by wearing a Mexican blackface costume.

It's almost as if she's trying to lose. 🧐
That's why we've come to the conclusion that Boebert's recent behavior suggests she is not at all interested in being reelected, she just doesn't have the courage to admit it.
We get it, from her perspective making laws in Congress is probably sooooo boring--that's why she misses so many votes and is focusing her energy on "fun" stuff like UFOs and reptilians.
And if she quit today, she could easily get a cushy job as a Fox News pundit, or start a podcast with her BFF George Santos, all sorts of things!
But we also know how hard it is to manifest the courage to quit your job, and we really really do empathize with that.
That's why, as Democrats, we think it would be cruel to let Boebert suffer in silence. So we are going to answer her cry for help, and we asking you to join us in showing Boebert the ultimate act of kindness so that she can pursue what her heart truly desires:
[Will you rush a donation of $50, $25, or whatever you can spare to help Democrat Trisha Calvarese defeat Boebert so she doesn't have to admit she wants to quit her job?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001FPNuK2Z_sEz2XcVvpe9lb2u8z-o5S0oz7HBHKcBqqoQEPlckWERXt-WXPVTe2wnHxs8PVXpmcsMX-mNrAs_fiv6DOwmNlwQHIqakMnYcV3MLr0kappIX0N000OcsOQD-uPv9Jnv7zumozysYLRyKbSWx-D97Z2YZjwdrXCEBUjpFbR6MBXuCZJ_0dYMlrCw0bJ0DhwAesxn-xk9TVNHAMr_EuAhNJgyRlFBbrNGdu4k=&c=Lwn0VtdGxtBknhREpoyYNzTEN6Lpz5AmX4scZUkswK4OrCYxeZvBoQ==&ch=9oGW3qty4texyw6c7c0V0oAH3sPHANXbUtwCgWVMwoWuxSdealquFg==)

clean_body: Boebert’s recent behavior suggests she is not at all interested in being reelected, she just doesn’t have the courage to admit it. | | | | | == | Peter, Here on Team Trisha, our boss has made it clear that we NEVER turn our backs on someone who's suffering. And the recent behaviour of our opponent has us seriously concerned. Just in the last few weeks, Boebert: Only raised $100,000 dollars last quarter (5 times less than us). Launched her reelection bid with UFOs as the centerpiece of her platform. Forgot what district she represented in a public statement. Purposefully mocked thousands of her Latino and Chicano constituents by wearing a Mexican blackface costume. It's almost as if she's trying to lose. 🧐 That's why we've come to the conclusion that Boebert's recent behavior suggests she is not at all interested in being reelected, she just doesn't have the courage to admit it. We get it, from her perspective making laws in Congress is probably sooooo boring--that's why she misses so many votes and is focusing her energy on "fun" stuff like UFOs and reptilians. And if she quit today, she could easily get a cushy job as a Fox News pundit, or start a podcast with her BFF George Santos, all sorts of things! But we also know how hard it is to manifest the courage to quit your job, and we really really do empathize with that. That's why, as Democrats, we think it would be cruel to let Boebert suffer in silence. So we are going to answer her cry for help, and we asking you to join us in showing Boebert the ultimate act of kindness so that she can pursue what her heart truly desires: == If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | Let's show Boebert that we really care by making sure she never has to show up for a real job again! ❤️ Team Trisha | | | | == | Mail checks to: Trisha 4 CO 9609 S University Blvd., PO Box 631322 Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 | Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado Trisha Calvarese for Congress | 9609 S University Blvd. PO Box 631322 | Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 US | | | | | | |

name: Major General Paul Eaton, U.S. Army, retired
subject: The culmination of a decades-long effort
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
#
Peter, good morning.
20 years ago, VoteVets did not exist.
There was no political operation of scale and strength that could recruit and support Veterans and National Security Professionals in races from coast-to-coast, in every state of our nation.
Over the last 20 years, we built that operation. We supported candidates like Ruben Gallego, an original VoteVets State Captain in Arizona. Tammy Duckworth, Mark Kelly, Tim Walz, and more. We were there at the beginning of their campaigns because we believed in each of them.
Yesterday’s results in New Jersey and Virginia were decades in the making. From Abigail and Mikie’s first campaigns for Congress in districts nobody expected to win, across re-election campaigns in the years after, and now with two victorious governors-elect.
But yesterday has as much to do with other candidates as it does with Abigail and Mikie. VoteVets members won their races in down-ballot campaigns in Pennsylvania, Texas, and Virginia as well. Their willingness to continue serving their country out of uniform — especially now — gives me immense hope. VoteVets looks forward to supporting them in the years to come.
Make no mistake: VoteVets was on the ballot yesterday. VoteVets members consistently win in races no other candidate could. And when you donate to VoteVets, your donation goes directly towards campaigns like Abigail’s, Mikie’s, and all of the others who won last night. It has an impact bigger than you might expect.
In the Army, we would call that a force multiplier. In politics, it’s just good tactics.
So, with that in mind, [**I am asking you to chip in $5 to VoteVets. There are thousands of candidates who need your support ahead of their campaigns in 2026. Our democracy is on the line, and the work to save it starts now.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-nov25.xqPykj&refcode=em251105-1-120&t=1&akid=34748%2E3013755%2EUTYw2V)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[CHIP IN $5](https://act.votevets.org/go/2933?c0=vv-pac-em-nov25.pScLIv&amount=5&refcode=em251105-1-120&t=2&akid=34748%2E3013755%2EUTYw2V)**
---
****
| **[CHIP IN MORE](https://act.votevets.org/go/2933?c0=vv-pac-em-nov25.pScLIv&refcode=em251105-1-120&t=3&akid=34748%2E3013755%2EUTYw2V)**
---
****
Thank you,
Paul Eaton
Major General, U.S. Army, Retired
Senior Advisor, VoteVets

PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS.ORG, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE’S COMMITTEE

name: Ossoff for Georgia
subject: Tough road ahead
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action
body:
We’re pretty sure this didn’t happen
| | | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Td2579efc-5071-40d5-9ef5-71c7639b8c21/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
| Peter - We're pretty sure you didn't wake up this morning, jump out of bed, and say, "Today is the day I am going to split a donation between Jon Ossoff's U.S. Senate campaign and Blue Amp Action!" (If you did, great! Here’s the link, and thank you in advance: [**blueaa.org/ossoff-home**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T4f1a967b-54fa-4f1d-8ece-215f38389cf6/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)) Otherwise, please give us a second to explain why we’re asking you to chip in and why, particularly right now. Jon won his last election by building the largest on-the-ground movement that Georgia had ever seen. Doing it again — and going even bigger — is not going to be easy. It is going to take sustained effort from now through Election Day. And we have to get to work right away. Unfortunately, a lot of people think maybe it's too early to give. Or they'll wait until later. We get that. But the truth is, early money to a campaign is more important than a donation at any other time. Your donation now will help us build the strong foundation we need to win, and ensure we’re ready for anything that comes our way on this campaign, from Donald Trump, our MAGA opponents, or anyone else. So what do you say? [Can Jon count on you to chip in $5, or any amount, to his campaign and Blue Amp Action today? We’ve got a tough road ahead of us, and every dollar makes a difference.](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tdf41d1d4-37be-4b92-8d20-09a1678e7d3b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade, it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in a democracy, not a dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T6a9ad08a-d389-4b15-a813-b94061a2aa8d/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: Team Lindstrom
subject: Join us this Saturday!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brett Lindstrom for Congress
body:
Honor our brave men and women who have served our country.
| | | | [](https://link.win.lindstromforne.)
---
| | Peter,
We’ve got a big weekend ahead to honor our veterans!
This **Saturday, November 8th,** Brett and our campaign team will be walking in the Nebraska Veteran’s Day Parade in Bellevue to honor the brave men and women who have served our country. It’s one of our favorite community events every year.
Let’s pack the route with energy, support, and Nebraska spirit! ****
---
[](https://link.win.lindstromforne.)
**Event Details:**
We are meeting at 903 W Mission Ave, Bellevue, NE 68005
Line-up at 9:00 AM
Parade begins at 10:00 AM
We are Float #36!
**If you can join us, just send a quick RSVP to:**
**Andy@votelindstrom.com**
Bring your family. Bring a friend. Bring that great Nebraskan energy.
Let’s get out there and show our support for our veterans and for Nebraska!
See you Saturday, Team Lindstrom
| | |
---

Peter,

I'm going to give it to you straight.

_We 're on the verge of wasting a historic opportunity._

Right now, the Democrats are about as unpopular as it gets. The American people are done, to put it mildly, with their insane liberal agenda.

name: Delegate-Elect Elizabeth Guzman
subject: THANK YOU!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Elizabeth Guzman
body:
| | | **_
_** **_¡Sí, se pudo!_**
I’m so honored to return to the House of Delegates to keep fighting for working families, our schools, and women’s rights.
**Gracias for believing in this movement — the work continues!**
The voters of Prince William made it clear — they are ready for change and for leadership that actually delivers results.
I’m humbled and grateful to once again earn the trust of my community and to bring this seat back home to the people it belongs to.
This victory is about more than politics — it’s about progress.
Voters chose effective leadership over excuses, and a vision rooted in opportunity, safety, and respect for every family.
**Starting day one, I’ll get to work lowering costs, protecting our public schools, and strengthening our local economy so that every Virginian has a fair shot.**
As our nation faces growing division and chaos, Virginia must continue to be a place that leads with compassion, common sense, and courage.
That’s the kind of leadership I’ll bring to Richmond — and I’m ready to get to work.
Thank you for your support,
Elizabeth Guzman

clean_body: | | | ¡Sí, se pudo! I’m so honored to return to the House of Delegates to keep fighting for working families, our schools, and women’s rights. Gracias for believing in this movement — the work continues! The voters of Prince William made it clear — they are ready for change and for leadership that actually delivers results. I’m humbled and grateful to once again earn the trust of my community and to bring this seat back home to the people it belongs to. This victory is about more than politics — it’s about progress. Voters chose effective leadership over excuses, and a vision rooted in opportunity, safety, and respect for every family. Starting day one, I’ll get to work lowering costs, protecting our public schools, and strengthening our local economy so that every Virginian has a fair shot. As our nation faces growing division and chaos, Virginia must continue to be a place that leads with compassion, common sense, and courage. That’s the kind of leadership I’ll bring to Richmond — and I’m ready to get to work. Thank you for your support, Elizabeth Guzman | Democrat is running for Delegate in Virginia's 22nd District. She came to America as a single mother, becoming a union leader and one of the first Latinas in the Virginia General Assembly. She passed stronger gun safety laws, expanded collective bargaining rights and paid sick leave, and protected reproductive rights. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.
---

Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. Dear Peter,

Last week, we asked what you wanted to know about the fight for reproductive rights—and you delivered. This week, we're answering one of the most common questions we received: What are crisis pregnancy centers (CPCs)? [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/118707506/580630748/646894325?sourceid=1150939&utm_campaign=26emkge-1101aax-on&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=askusanything&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NTQ5NzA0MC01ZGJhLWYwMTEtOGU2MS0wMDIyNDgyNGUwOGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NhWif4nK0dq3mTHLMYdwOKANx1z6T9uRwYFwx46SEOM=&emci=8934b941-cbb9-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=95497040-5dba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&ceid=954237) **Here's what you need to know:**
* **Crisis Pregnancy Centers (CPCs) often _look_ like full-service health clinics.** They advertise to pregnant people and may offer free pregnancy tests or non-diagnostic ultrasounds, but they don't provide real medical care. By delaying care and steering people away from abortion and contraception, CPCs can have a serious impact on physical and mental health.
* **CPCs use deception to stigmatize abortion and to stop people from getting abortions.** Patients come seeking care—and instead are misled. CPCs frequently spread misinformation, like the claim that abortion causes infertility or breast cancer. These statements are completely false.
* **CPCs target low-income communities** where people don't have access to other resources, luring pregnant people in with promises of free goods like diapers, which are conditioned upon engaging in religious counseling.
* **Many CPCs have no licensed medical staff, provide no prenatal care, and are not bound by patient privacy laws like HIPAA.** That means no diagnostic ultrasounds, no contraception, and no confidentiality.

CPCs can delay essential care and increase confusion, fear, and shame for people seeking reproductive health care or information. By posing as legitimate medical providers and spreading misinformation, CPCs jeopardize patients' safety and autonomy.

[**At the Center for Reproductive Rights, we believe everyone deserves access to accurate information and real health care.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/118707507/580630749/646894325?sourceid=1150939&utm_campaign=26emkge-1101aax-on&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=askusanything&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NTQ5NzA0MC01ZGJhLWYwMTEtOGU2MS0wMDIyNDgyNGUwOGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NhWif4nK0dq3mTHLMYdwOKANx1z6T9uRwYFwx46SEOM=&emci=8934b941-cbb9-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=95497040-5dba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&ceid=954237)

clean_body: No doctors, no privacy, no reliable information | | | ---|--- Dear Peter, Last week, we asked what you wanted to know about the fight for reproductive rights—and you delivered. This week, we're answering one of the most common questions we received: What are crisis pregnancy centers (CPCs)? Here's what you need to know: Crisis Pregnancy Centers (CPCs) often look like full-service health clinics. They advertise to pregnant people and may offer free pregnancy tests or non-diagnostic ultrasounds, but they don't provide real medical care. By delaying care and steering people away from abortion and contraception, CPCs can have a serious impact on physical and mental health. CPCs use deception to stigmatize abortion and to stop people from getting abortions. Patients come seeking care—and instead are misled. CPCs frequently spread misinformation, like the claim that abortion causes infertility or breast cancer. These statements are completely false. CPCs target low-income communities where people don't have access to other resources, luring pregnant people in with promises of free goods like diapers, which are conditioned upon engaging in religious counseling. Many CPCs have no licensed medical staff, provide no prenatal care, and are not bound by patient privacy laws like HIPAA. That means no diagnostic ultrasounds, no contraception, and no confidentiality. CPCs can delay essential care and increase confusion, fear, and shame for people seeking reproductive health care or information. By posing as legitimate medical providers and spreading misinformation, CPCs jeopardize patients' safety and autonomy. Join us on for our upcoming Town Hall, where my colleagues and I will break down the truth about CPCs and answer more of your questions about reproductive rights. | "REGISTER FOR THE TOWN HALL" Sincerely, Alice Wang Senior Staff Attorney "DONATE NOW" | | | | ---|---|--- The Center for Reproductive Rights uses the power of law to advance reproductive rights as fundamental human rights around the world. (C) Center for Reproductive Rights Center for Reproductive Rights 199 Water St. New York, NY 10038 United States

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Governor
body:
It is the honor of my lifetime to be elected the 75th Governor of the Commonwealth of Virginia!

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118707878/580632011/-1898250673?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2025-11-05_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmVhYzcyMWItNWRiYS1mMDExLThlNjEtMDAyMjQ4MjRlMDhkIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=lt5bJmYwVCoY1ruyHedqIG3rBgP21va3LV_j3ISwScQ=)
Peter,
Last night, Virginians sent a message to every corner of the Commonwealth, to our neighbors and our fellow Americans across the country, to the whole world.
They chose leadership that will put pragmatism over partisanship.
They chose leadership that will focus on problem solving, not stoking division.
They chose leadership that will focus relentlessly on what matters most: lowering costs, keeping our communities safe, and strengthening our economy for every Virginian.
They chose a Governor who will always put Virginia — not a President, not a political party — first.
**It is the honor of my lifetime to be elected the 75th Governor of the Commonwealth of Virginia!**
To everyone who helped us achieve this win, thank you for the trust you have placed in me. To our tireless volunteers and grassroots donors, thank you for devoting your time, energy, and hard-earned dollars to his campaign.
_We won this race because of you._
Now, the list of challenges we are facing is long, but I know we can solve these problems by tackling them together — Democrats, Republicans, Independents, and everyone in-between. Whether you voted for me or not, my intent is to serve all Virginians.
That means I will listen to you. I will work for you and with you. And I cannot wait to get started.
Let's show the world what we're made of,

clean_body: It is the honor of my lifetime to be elected the 75th Governor of the Commonwealth of Virginia! = Peter, Last night, Virginians sent a message to every corner of the Commonwealth, to our neighbors and our fellow Americans across the country, to the whole world. They chose leadership that will put pragmatism over partisanship. They chose leadership that will focus on problem solving, not stoking division. They chose leadership that will focus relentlessly on what matters most: lowering costs, keeping our communities safe, and strengthening our economy for every Virginian. They chose a Governor who will always put Virginia — not a President, not a political party — first. It is the honor of my lifetime to be elected the 75th Governor of the Commonwealth of Virginia! To everyone who helped us achieve this win, thank you for the trust you have placed in me. To our tireless volunteers and grassroots donors, thank you for devoting your time, energy, and hard-earned dollars to his campaign. We won this race because of you. Now, the list of challenges we are facing is long, but I know we can solve these problems by tackling them together — Democrats, Republicans, Independents, and everyone in-between. Whether you voted for me or not, my intent is to serve all Virginians. That means I will listen to you. I will work for you and with you. And I cannot wait to get started. Let's show the world what we're made of, Abigail Spanberger Governor-Elect, Virginia = By submitting your phone number you're agreeing to receive periodic text messages from the Spanberger for Governor campaign. Republicans lost more than elections--they lost the story they've been selling America.

| |

# [Yesterday Proved It: Democracy Is a Verb](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=178085055&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MDg1MDU1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjIzNTg0NzYsImV4cCI6MTc2NDk1MDQ3NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQyNTgxMTUiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.TrDTbtaFRS3hn8yL0XEDGdHVC1XU9C6Rkmqhpz7e36k)
### You dragged these wins across the finish line.
| | [Mike Nellis](https://substack.com/@mikenellis)
---
| Nov 5
---
| [](https://substack.com/@mikenellis)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=178085055&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MDg1MDU1LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2MjM1ODQ3NiwiZXhwIjoxNzY0OTUwNDc2LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDI1ODExNSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4HC8Uc5eIQtZJHs0rZlqNlO0KBF79nVqc0iTjzBYtkY&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=178085055&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MDg1MDU1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjIzNTg0NzYsImV4cCI6MTc2NDk1MDQ3NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQyNTgxMTUiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.TrDTbtaFRS3hn8yL0XEDGdHVC1XU9C6Rkmqhpz7e36k&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

---

_**[Endless Urgency is free to read, but we're hustling to launch our new content studio and expand our team with a video editor. If you've been enjoying the work, now's the time to back it. Paid subscribers make it possible--just $8/month makes a huge difference: ](https://substack.com/redirect/e989a268-6eac-418e-baea-b5c78ce51ec5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/e989a268-6eac-418e-baea-b5c78ce51ec5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
I couldn't stop thinking about it last night as I watched Democrats rack up win after win. And it wasn't about exit polls or some consultant's postmortem memo. It was about the people--the ones who refused to let this country fully slip into the hands of the worst, loudest, most corrupt assholes in America.
Republicans didn't just lose elections last night. For the first time in a year, they lost full control of the narrative they've been force-feeding the American public: that Donald Trump is beloved, and that resistance is futile.
The American people are pissed. They were promised lower prices--now everything costs more. Promised a booming economy--and we've got the weakest job market in five years. Promised a functioning government--and instead, it's shut down. Health care is bring ripped from people while tens of millions are staring down hunger. And meanwhile? Republicans in the House aren't even showing up to work.
But people didn't give up. Over the last year, while the GOP tried to gaslight us into believing Democrats could never win again, we proved them wrong. You said: _screw that._
And then you acted on it.
Every No Kings rally. Every special election. Every time communities backed each other when ICE came knocking. Every story of someone stepping up--like when Manny's Deli in Chicago started handing out sandwiches to folks losing SNAP benefits this week. The retired teacher with aching knees still knocking doors. The union worker running his first door-to-door shift instead of doomscrolling. The neighbor who ran for school board because some MAGA lunatic was screwing up her kid's education. The priest holding vigil outside ICE detention--not for himself, but because his faith wouldn't let him sit still.
These aren't just small acts of resistance. They're bricks--the foundation of a democracy that can function again. And now, it's showing up at the ballot box.
Mikie Sherrill stomped Jack Ciattarelli in New Jersey--the guy couldn't have hugged Trump any tighter. Abigail Spanberger dominated in Virginia, and Virgina Democrats flipped more than a dozen GOP-held State Senate seats. Zohran Mamdani broke 50% in New York City, even after all the Trump and Cuomo coordination.
But this isn't about any one candidate. Yeah, the media will spin it as _The Zohran Mamdani Story_ because to them everything revolves around NYC. And sure--there are lessons to take from that campaign (honestly, one of the most joyful and disciplined races I've seen since Bernie 2016).
The bigger story is this: regular people--not institutions, not pundits, not influencers or party hacks--dragged these wins across the finish line. They kept democracy on life support long enough for it to breathe again.
The pundit class will say Republicans lost because of bad messaging or low turnout or Trump fatigue. The White House will act like nothing has changed. Fine. But the truth is: they lost because they betrayed the American people--and because we refused to be bullied into silence.
Because you remembered that democracy only works when people decide it's worth the fight.
Because we're done being told this economy is fine.
Done watching federal agents kidnap people off the street.
Done watching the president and his cronies rob taxpayers while millions can't afford groceries.
But this fight isn't over.
* * *
_**[Endless Urgency is free to read, but we're hustling to launch our new content studio and expand our team with a video editor. If you've been enjoying the work, now's the time to back it. Paid subscribers make it possible--just $8/month makes a huge difference:](https://substack.com/redirect/e989a268-6eac-418e-baea-b5c78ce51ec5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/e989a268-6eac-418e-baea-b5c78ce51ec5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
The people trying to break this country aren't disappearing. Trump is still president. Republicans still hold Congress. They'll keep mistaking cruelty for strength. While Mamdani's numbers were still coming in, Stephen Miller was already online threatening more ICE raids in NYC. It's going to get uglier.
But last night was a win. In big races and tiny ones.
And we're going to do it again. In upcoming special elections. In 2026. In 2028. We're taking this country back--and we're doing it by building a movement we can actually be proud of. That means pushing the Democratic Party to grow the hell up and get tougher, too.
Don't take these wins for granted. Let them fuel you. Let them remind you what's possible. Let them prove--again--that we don't just hope the arc of the universe bends.
We grab it. We pull.
So whatever you did, however you showed up--breathe it in. You helped build this.
Be proud. You earned that pride.
Whether you worked on a campaign, talked to a neighbor, had the hard conversation, knocked the doors, made the calls--this win is yours.
Celebrate. Then get ready to do it again.
Because the story of this country has never belonged to the people trying to control it through power and greed.
It belongs to the people like YOU who show the hell up.
**And last night, we proved it again: democracy isn 't a noun. It's a verb.**
With urgency,
--Mike

You dragged these wins across the finish line. | | | Nov 5 | | | | | | | | | | | | I couldn't stop thinking about it last night as I watched Democrats rack up win after win. And it wasn't about exit polls or some consultant's postmortem memo. It was about the people--the ones who refused to let this country fully slip into the hands of the worst, loudest, most corrupt assholes in America. Republicans didn't just lose elections last night. For the first time in a year, they lost full control of the narrative they've been force-feeding the American public: that Donald Trump is beloved, and that resistance is futile. The American people are pissed. They were promised lower prices--now everything costs more. Promised a booming economy--and we've got the weakest job market in five years. Promised a functioning government--and instead, it's shut down. Health care is bring ripped from people while tens of millions are staring down hunger. And meanwhile? Republicans in the House aren't even showing up to work. But people didn't give up. Over the last year, while the GOP tried to gaslight us into believing Democrats could never win again, we proved them wrong. You said: screw that. And then you acted on it. Every No Kings rally. Every special election. Every time communities backed each other when ICE came knocking. Every story of someone stepping up--like when Manny's Deli in Chicago started handing out sandwiches to folks losing SNAP benefits this week. The retired teacher with aching knees still knocking doors. The union worker running his first door-to-door shift instead of doomscrolling. The neighbor who ran for school board because some MAGA lunatic was screwing up her kid's education. The priest holding vigil outside ICE detention--not for himself, but because his faith wouldn't let him sit still. These aren't just small acts of resistance. They're bricks--the foundation of a democracy that can function again. And now, it's showing up at the ballot box. Mikie Sherrill stomped Jack Ciattarelli in New Jersey--the guy couldn't have hugged Trump any tighter. Abigail Spanberger dominated in Virginia, and Virgina Democrats flipped more than a dozen GOP-held State Senate seats. Zohran Mamdani broke 50% in New York City, even after all the Trump and Cuomo coordination. But this isn't about any one candidate. Yeah, the media will spin it as The Zohran Mamdani Story because to them everything revolves around NYC. And sure--there are lessons to take from that campaign (honestly, one of the most joyful and disciplined races I've seen since Bernie 2016). The bigger story is this: regular people--not institutions, not pundits, not influencers or party hacks--dragged these wins across the finish line. They kept democracy on life support long enough for it to breathe again. The pundit class will say Republicans lost because of bad messaging or low turnout or Trump fatigue. The White House will act like nothing has changed. Fine. But the truth is: they lost because they betrayed the American people--and because we refused to be bullied into silence. Because you remembered that democracy only works when people decide it's worth the fight. Because we're done being told this economy is fine. Done watching federal agents kidnap people off the street. Done watching the president and his cronies rob taxpayers while millions can't afford groceries. But this fight isn't over. The people trying to break this country aren't disappearing. Trump is still president. Republicans still hold Congress. They'll keep mistaking cruelty for strength. While Mamdani's numbers were still coming in, Stephen Miller was already online threatening more ICE raids in NYC. It's going to get uglier. But last night was a win. In big races and tiny ones. And we're going to do it again. In upcoming special elections. In 2026. In 2028. We're taking this country back--and we're doing it by building a movement we can actually be proud of. That means pushing the Democratic Party to grow the hell up and get tougher, too. Don't take these wins for granted. Let them fuel you. Let them remind you what's possible. Let them prove--again--that we don't just hope the arc of the universe bends. We grab it. We pull. So whatever you did, however you showed up--breathe it in. You helped build this. Be proud. You earned that pride. Whether you worked on a campaign, talked to a neighbor, had the hard conversation, knocked the doors, made the calls--this win is yours. Celebrate. Then get ready to do it again. Because the story of this country has never belonged to the people trying to control it through power and greed. It belongs to the people like YOU who show the hell up. And last night, we proved it again: democracy isn 't a noun. It's a verb.** With urgency, --Mike Mike Nellis is a Democratic strategist and entrepreneur who has raised over $1 billion for Democratic campaigns and causes. He 's the founder of Authentic.org, an award-winning fundraising and advertising agency, and a former Senior Advisor to Vice President Kamala Harris. This Substack is where he writes with endless urgency about the issues of the day--and how we can save the Democratic Party and our democracy. You 're currently a free subscriber to Endless Urgency with Mike Nellis. For the full experience or to support my work so I can keep it free for as many people as possible, upgrade your subscription. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Mike Nellis 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

We wish we could say these are the exceptions, but it's becoming increasingly common on the left. And make no mistake, _no prominent Democratic leader has denounced these lunatics_.

However, we want to stress something to you. We _cannot_ overreact to the violent left's provocations.

We _will_ defeat them... At the ballot box, just like Charlie Kirk wanted us to do.

We will win again in 2026, 2028, and beyond. We will _take back our country_ from these violent thugs who want to impose socialism on the rest of us.
