---
| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

This isn't a rumor. Not speculation. These are facts the authorities are desperate to hide.

They mocked him for saying the pandemic started in a lab.
They sneered when he warned about microplastics in our bodies.
They dismissed his claims of FDA corruption.

But he was right. Always right.

Now his latest warning is darker than anything before.

Leaked documents reveal: America’s seniors are quietly being pushed into danger—mysterious illnesses, sudden collapses written off as “normal aging,” and millions funneling into risky prescriptions while the truth remains hidden.

If you were born before **January 1, 1965** , this is your personal risk.

---
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. | | | RFK Jr just dropped a bomb—and Washington is in panic. This isn't a rumor. Not speculation. These are facts the authorities are desperate to hide. They mocked him for saying the pandemic started in a lab. They sneered when he warned about microplastics in our bodies. They dismissed his claims of FDA corruption. But he was right. Always right. Now his latest warning is darker than anything before. Leaked documents reveal: America’s seniors are quietly being pushed into danger—mysterious illnesses, sudden collapses written off as “normal aging,” and millions funneling into risky prescriptions while the truth remains hidden. If you were born before January 1, 1965 , this is your personal risk. | | | 🟡 News From "American Alternative Assets" | (Sponsored) ---|--- Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from YourCapitalMinds.com, an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: Wes Moore
subject: 🙅🏽 I won’t back down
body:
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=881%2E1086043%2EvmlsSu)
Team, it’s Gov. Wes Moore – **and I won’t back down.**
Not when Trump’s government shutdown is threatening Maryland families, and not when Republicans try to defeat us in 2026.
We’ve launched relief efforts for impacted workers, kept key programs afloat, and made sure Marylanders know their government won’t abandon them.
**But the truth is, the longer this shutdown drags on, the more strain it puts on our schools, small businesses, and essential workers.**
That’s why we’re mobilizing every resource to protect families and keep our economy moving – and why grassroots support is critical right now.
**[I need your help to stay in this fight. Will you chip in $5 or whatever you can to help us keep standing strong for Maryland?](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251105-1.8EcrzG&refcode=em-20251105-1&t=4&akid=881%2E1086043%2EvmlsSu)**

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: The lesson from last night
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
Voters are saying — shouting — that they want Democrats to fight for working people. To fight HARD.

| | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/216?t=3&akid=2694%2E4032634%2EocrXQF)
---
The people triumphed over billionaires yesterday, proving beyond a shadow of a doubt that when we fight for bold policies that help the working class, we can win big. Zohran Mamdani rocketed to victory last night, overcoming millions of dollars spent by billionaires to defeat his agenda to make New York City a city that working people can afford. The pundits may want to dismiss this win as New York being New York, but here’s the reality: The bold, progressive candidate who campaigned on a real affordability agenda won. That can happen anywhere. And let’s talk about who lost. A disgraced former governor endorsed by Trump, who can’t take no for an answer, who sold out working families to get donations from billionaires, and who only spoke about what we can’t do and what’s not possible. Voters proved that all the MAGA-billionaire money in the world still could not buy New York City. They rejected the old politics of fear and ushered in an era of new leadership. Mamdani’s win is a beacon of hope that we CAN make our government work for working people. It’s not radical to fight for universal childcare. It’s not radical to think transportation should be fast and free. It’s not radical to make housing affordable. In fact, these things are the reason why we have government in the first place — to make life better for the people we serve. And voters overwhelmingly agree, not just in New York. Affordability — not the billionaire agenda — wins all across America. Here in Boston, Josh Kraft — the son of a billionaire with infinite money — wasn’t even on the ballot last night against Mayor Michelle Wu because he dropped out after an embarrassing 49-point loss in the September primary. In Virginia and New Jersey, Democratic champions Abigail Spanberger and Mikie Sherrill defeated Trump-wannabes, catapulting to victory with a clear message to make life more affordable for working people. They championed ideas like more affordable housing, freezing utility rates, lowering health care costs. So don’t tell me that Democrats need to suck up to billionaires in order to win elections. Nope. Voters are saying — shouting — that they want Democrats to fight for working people. To fight HARD. No longer can we stay in a defensive crouch. No longer can we nibble around the edges and hope we can settle for safe, incremental change. We’ve got to stiffen our spines, stand together, and fight. That’s exactly what Governor Newsom and California voters did by overwhelmingly passing Prop 50 yesterday. It’s a critical stopgap that prevents the GOP from aggressively tipping the scales with their unprecedented mid-year gerrymandering campaign. And unlike what the GOP forced through in Texas, this temporary measure was passed by voters — not by a phone call from the President. Californians showed that we will not just stand by and accept that Trump is destroying our democracy. Today I’m waking up feeling hopeful and energized. The path forward for the Democratic party is clear: We win when we fight like hell for working people. When we champion bold ideas even if billionaires don’t like them. When we aspire to govern in ways that may not have been tried before, rather than settle with “that’s never going to happen.” When we stand up to the extremism of Donald Trump’s administration. Voters sent us a clear message. And I’m going to do everything in my power to get our government to listen. Thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth

---

name: Nellie Pou HQ
subject: New Jersey is a blue state
body:
New Jersey just got bluer. Let’s keep the momentum going.

| |
---
We did it, friend. Yesterday, we proved what we’ve always known: that New Jersey is a blue state. Thanks to your hard work and dedication, we helped elect Mikie Sherrill to the governor’s mansion, a major victory and a confirmation that New Jersey voters don’t want extremists and far-right Republicans representing them—in their home state and in Washington. In 2024, Donald Trump won Passaic County by **5,798 votes**. But last night, Mikie Sherrill won by a margin of **21,503 votes** in Passaic. _**The tides are turning**_ , and that’s a direct result of our hard work—spreading our message throughout the district and mobilizing people who are ready for change. We’ve shown what can happen when people come together with focus and determination. But our work isn’t over. The House Majority is on the line, and the challenges ahead will require the same energy, organization, and belief in what we can accomplish together. We can’t slow down. So our work starts today, friend. clean_body: New Jersey just got bluer. Let’s keep the momentum going. | | We did it, friend. Yesterday, we proved what we’ve always known: that New Jersey is a blue state. Thanks to your hard work and dedication, we helped elect Mikie Sherrill to the governor’s mansion, a major victory and a confirmation that New Jersey voters don’t want extremists and far-right Republicans representing them—in their home state and in Washington. In 2024, Donald Trump won Passaic County by 5,798 votes. But last night, Mikie Sherrill won by a margin of 21,503 votes in Passaic. The tides are turning , and that’s a direct result of our hard work—spreading our message throughout the district and mobilizing people who are ready for change. We’ve shown what can happen when people come together with focus and determination. But our work isn’t over. The House Majority is on the line, and the challenges ahead will require the same energy, organization, and belief in what we can accomplish together. We can’t slow down. So our work starts today, friend. Every contribution, no matter the size, helps us reach more voters, fight back against right-wing attacks, and protect one of the most competitive seats in the country. = | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately. Let’s show up for the next elections just like we did for this one — because every vote, every dollar, and every volunteer hour makes a difference. With gratitude, Nellie Pou HQ | | | ---|---|--- About Nellie Pou Nellie Pou is the first Latina to represent New Jersey in Congress, and will be a target for the NRCC in 2026. As a mother and grandmother, Nellie knows what New Jersey families are facing and will continue to bring down costs while creating good-paying jobs. Nellie has dedicated her entire life to the community that raised her, and in Congress she will do the same. | = | = ---|--- Follow Nellie | | | ---|---|--- Checks may also be mailed to: Nellie Pou for Congress - PO BOX 2696, Paterson, NJ, 07509 Paid for by Nellie Pou for Congress Email is a critical way to stay in touch with our supporters, but if you wish to unsubscribe, you may do so . Nellie Pou for Congress PO Box 2696 Paterson, NJ 07509 United States

name: John Bisognano, NDRC
subject: How democracy won last night:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
“When people stand up, speak out, and get involved — we move our country forward.”
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118725054/580687475/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjk0YjllNWFmLTc4YmEtZjAxMS04ZTYxLTAwMjI0ODI0ZTA4ZCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=KljK1pwd6nABkx1MZqp_2Md9ARFXmY_7b49ynvTCSF4=&emci=283926f5-61ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&emdi=94b9e5af-78ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&ceid=8431045)
---

Last night was a resounding victory for our democracy, Peter. Voters across the country — including in Virginia, Pennsylvania, and California — headed to the polls amid unprecedented attacks on our democracy and overwhelmingly rejected the cynical politics of those in power. Americans delivered a sharp rebuke to Republican attempts to silence the will of the people. We sent a clear message that democracy can win if we fight for it. Thanks to the persistence and grit of this movement, pro-democracy candidates and causes prevailed from coast to coast:
* In **Virginia** , voters elected pro-democracy Abigail Spanberger to be its next governor, and significantly expanded Democrats’ majority in the House of Delegates by flipping several seats in the legislature. The Democratic majority, which was just two seats, has grown by double digits, ensuring that the legislature is in an even stronger position to protect basic freedoms in the state — and allow Virginia to keep fighting back against Trump’s mid-decade gerrymandering crisis.
* Voters in **California** overwhelmingly approved Prop 50 to respond to the extreme, mid-decade redraws Republicans are perpetrating in states like Texas, Missouri, and North Carolina. And unlike those Republican-led states, California put power in the hands of the people to pass this temporary countermeasure. These results send a clear message to Republicans that voters won’t let their voices be silenced.
* The pro-democracy movement in **Pennsylvania** overcame an influx of billionaire and special-interest spending and retained Justices Christine Donohue, Kevin Dougherty, and David Wecht. The state’s highest court will continue to be a reliable backstop against anti-democracy efforts, including mounting pressure from the White House to impose GOP gerrymanders.

It was a really good night for democracy. But make no mistake: _The work gets harder from here — the year ahead will be one of the most consequential in the history of our republic._ **Our mission now is to ensure the 2026 midterm elections are free and fair — and truly representative of the will of the American people.** That’s why NDRC is prepared for any further escalation of Republican attempts to manipulate maps in their favor. We are ready to fight back fiercely. At the same time, we need to elect Democracy Defenders in dozens of critical state-level races next year — and need your help to build on this incredible momentum. [**The scale of this work is unprecedented — and so it requires unprecedented resources. Will you power our strategy so we can fight hard on multiple fronts to ensure democracy prevails in the 2026 midterm elections?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118725055/580687478/-1263916904?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_ElectionResults_email_20251105-NDRC-Tier1-ElectionResults_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251105-NDRC-Tier1-ElectionResults_&utm_campaign=ElectionResults&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjk0YjllNWFmLTc4YmEtZjAxMS04ZTYxLTAwMjI0ODI0ZTA4ZCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=KljK1pwd6nABkx1MZqp_2Md9ARFXmY_7b49ynvTCSF4=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

Thank you for being one of the millions of concerned Americans who are standing up and fighting for our democracy. We’re honored to fight alongside you.
— Biz

And unlike those Republican-led states, California put power in the hands of the people to pass this temporary countermeasure. These results send a clear message to Republicans that voters won’t let their voices be silenced. The pro-democracy movement in Pennsylvania overcame an influx of billionaire and special-interest spending and retained Justices Christine Donohue, Kevin Dougherty, and David Wecht. The state’s highest court will continue to be a reliable backstop against anti-democracy efforts, including mounting pressure from the White House to impose GOP gerrymanders. It was a really good night for democracy. But make no mistake: The work gets harder from here — the year ahead will be one of the most consequential in the history of our republic. Our mission now is to ensure the 2026 midterm elections are free and fair — and truly representative of the will of the American people. That’s why NDRC is prepared for any further escalation of Republican attempts to manipulate maps in their favor. We are ready to fight back fiercely. At the same time, we need to elect Democracy Defenders in dozens of critical state-level races next year — and need your help to build on this incredible momentum. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Last night’s results send a strong message to everyone watching: As Attorney General Holder put it, “When people stand up, speak out, and get involved — we move our country forward.” Thank you for being one of the millions of concerned Americans who are standing up and fighting for our democracy. We’re honored to fight alongside you. — Biz John Bisognano President, National Democratic Redistricting Committee = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. Our victories have been made possible by our strong community of grassroots supporters. = If you want to receive periodic updates from the NDRC on our fight for fair maps, text MAPS to 36787. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. We know we send a lot of emails, but we think it's important to keep you updated with the latest information on Republican gerrymandering and voter suppression and how we're fighting back -- regardless of whether you choose to donate. = to make a donation. Every dollar you give will go toward our fight to end gerrymandering and voter suppression. | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NE, #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States ** if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC.

Peter, Last night was a great night for American democracy.

Congratulations to Senator-elect Hemmingsen-Jaeger for her victory last evening. With that win, the DFL now holds a razor thin one-seat majority in the State Senate. Now, we know what we have to do to win big in 2026. In just 363 days, every seat in both the House and Senate will be up for grabs, along with each of Minnesota’s executive offices.

**The stakes couldn’t be higher. The progress we’ve made is on the line. Free school lunches, paid family and medical leave, earned sick and safe time — all of the DFL’s accomplishments — will be on the ballot.**

subject: Our anniversary is today & you still haven't unlocked your MAGA membership?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Never Surrender, Inc
body:

| | | | This is a message from Donald Trump:

# **THIS IS MY MAGA CRY!**

---
[](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
Trump: _Friend, our anniversary is today & you still haven't unlocked your MAGA membership?_
I’ve come to the conclusion that the deep state must’ve been blocking my emails or something wicked like that because I added INCREDIBLE new perks to celebrate our one year together.
I know you would cherish them - it’s actually depressing that you haven’t gotten to see them.
**TRUMP’S MAGA CRY:** _I’m praying that this email is passed over to you soon because we’re down to our final few spots and I need you on our team_

[TAKE A LOOK](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
__Sincerely,
Donald Trump

subject: It’s official: Prop 50 passed

In case you missed it last night, Peter:

**California voters officially voted to pass Proposition 50.**

This sends a resounding message to Republicans in Texas and nationwide who are engaging in extreme partisan gerrymandering: we will _not_ stay silent.

California has now democratically leveled the playing field for next year’s midterm elections and defended millions of voters from being erased by the GOP’s petty partisan games.

The passage of Prop 50 also marks an unprecedented change in the fight to keep CA-27 blue: Thousands of new voters will be folded into this district, and with multiple Republican challengers already in the race, we need to act fast to reach them.

With just about a year to defend this seat, we’re rapidly increasing our voter outreach efforts. Only a few races will make or break the House majority, and we need to start now.

name: Massachusetts Democratic Party
subject: It Was A Very Good Night!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/Logo.png]
Hello Peter,
Yesterday, voters across the nation made their voices heard, and the results are starting to show the impact of civic engagement. Here’s a snapshot of some key races and initiatives:
This cycle, our community showed up in powerful ways. Together, we made over 100,000 phone calls here in Massachusetts and nationwide , knocked north of 5,000 doors , and dedicated hundreds of hours to connecting with voters, sharing information, and inspiring civic participation. Every conversation, every doorstep, and every hour volunteered made a real difference.
Massachusetts Local Races
Across Massachusetts, voters decided on mayors, city councils, school boards, and other local offices that shape day-to-day community life.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/Screenshot%202025-11-05%20at%2012.13.00%E2%80%AFPM.png]
These races are critical because they influence local policies, school budgets, public services and more.
New Jersey Governor Race
Voters in New Jersey chose to continue strong, pragmatic leadership by electing Mikie Sherrill as governor. Sherrill’s victory represents a commitment to expanding access to affordable healthcare, strengthening public education, and investing in an economy that works for working families. Her win underscores that voters want leaders who put people over politics and focus on delivering real results for their communities.
Virginia Governor Race
In Virginia, Abigail Spanberger ’s win marks a decisive moment for the Commonwealth. Spanberger’s leadership promises to center public safety, education, and opportunity—reflecting a forward-looking vision that unites rather than divides. Her victory sends a clear message: voters are ready to move past partisan gridlock and toward common-sense solutions that strengthen Virginia’s economy and protect fundamental rights.
California Proposition 50
Californians voted Yes on Proposition 50 , reaffirming their commitment to fair representation and transparent government. The measure reforms congressional redistricting rules to ensure that district boundaries are drawn based on fairness—not partisan interests. This is a win for democracy and a model for states across the country working to uphold equitable elections and voter trust.
Maine Question 1
Maine voters decisively rejected Question 1 , a proposal that would have restricted absentee voting and limited access to the ballot box. By voting No, Mainers reaffirmed their long tradition of civic participation and defended the right to vote freely and fairly. This outcome demonstrates a strong statewide commitment to protecting democracy and expanding, not constraining, voter access.
These results remind us that every vote counts and that participation in democracy drives real change. While we celebrate these wins, the work doesn’t stop here. Staying informed, engaged, and involved ensures that momentum continues into the 2026 midterm elections.
These victories are only possible because people choose to serve, whether as candidates, campaign staff, organizers, or volunteers, and because supporters like you put in the time and heart to make it happen. From the Organizing/GOTV phone banks to weekend canvasses and community outreach, our collective effort powered progress up and down the ballot.
As we celebrate these accomplishments, we also look ahead. The road to 2026 begins now. By continuing to engage voters, strengthen our grassroots networks, and invest in the next generation of leaders, we can keep building on this momentum and ensure Democratic victories for years to come.
Thank you for showing up, speaking out, and helping turn yesterday’s votes into meaningful progress. Together, we’re proving that democracy works best when everyone participates.
With gratitude,
Massachusetts Democratic Party
https://www.facebook.com/massdems [https://www.facebook.com/massdems] https://twitter.com/massdems [https://twitter.com/massdems] https://instagram.com/massdems [https://instagram.com/massdems] https://www.tiktok.com/@massdems [https://www.tiktok.com/@massdems]
Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee
Massachusetts Democratic Party
11 Beacon St
Suite 410
Boston, MA 02108
United States

These results remind us that every vote counts and that participation in democracy drives real change. While we celebrate these wins, the work doesn’t stop here. Staying informed, engaged, and involved ensures that momentum continues into the 2026 midterm elections. These victories are only possible because people choose to serve, whether as candidates, campaign staff, organizers, or volunteers, and because supporters like you put in the time and heart to make it happen. From the Organizing/GOTV phone banks to weekend canvasses and community outreach, our collective effort powered progress up and down the ballot. As we celebrate these accomplishments, we also look ahead. The road to 2026 begins now. By continuing to engage voters, strengthen our grassroots networks, and invest in the next generation of leaders, we can keep building on this momentum and ensure Democratic victories for years to come. Thank you for showing up, speaking out, and helping turn yesterday’s votes into meaningful progress. Together, we’re proving that democracy works best when everyone participates. With gratitude, Massachusetts Democratic Party [] [] [] [] Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee Massachusetts Democratic Party 11 Beacon St Suite 410 Boston, MA 02108 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

name: Elise Stefanik
subject: New Yorkers are DONE with Kathy Hochul
body:
Help me FIRE Kathy Hochul in 2026!

| | | | [](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/bs5i/?__vbtrk=MzMxNjM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMxNjM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

Friend, did you catch this interview?
I went on Sean Hannity’s show to expose the truth to the nation.

**WATCH THE CLIP:**

[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/bs5i/?__vbtrk=MzMxNjM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMxNjM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
**_Elise Stefanik on "The Sean Hannity Show"_**

Sean and I discussed how New Yorkers are FED UP with failed Democrat single party leadership!
They know that Kathy Hochul,**the WORST governor in America** , is destroying the Empire State from within by pushing policies that put violent criminals and illegals first while putting the needs of New Yorkers last.
Under her regime of sky-high taxes, open borders, and failed bail reform New York has become increasingly unaffordable and unsafe.
And just when we thought it couldn’t get any worse from New York Democrats,**Governor Hochul along with Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries have bent the knee** to the Far-Left of their party by**endorsing Zohran Mamdani** for Mayor of New York City.
Zohran Mamdani is a**radical communist** who unapologetically**promotes jihadists** and has called to both**DEFUND and DISMANTLE the police.**
This is who Kathy Hochul wants to run our nation’s largest city!
If we stand by and do nothing the Defund-The-Police Democrats led by Kathy Hochul and Zohran Mamdani will complete a full socialist takeover of New York from Albany to NYC.

[SAVE NEW YORK](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/bs5i/?__vbtrk=MzMxNjM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMxNjM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
Fellow patriot, I am not giving up on New York, this is my home and I know that we cannot Make America Great Again until we save our nation’s states and cities from the grip of the Radical Left.
And I am not alone. The polls are continuing to shift in our favor with recent independent polls indicating that I am LEADING Kathy Hochul. **This race is now a TOSS-UP!**

[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/bs5i/?__vbtrk=MzMxNjM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMxNjM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
**_Independent Poll Shows Elise Stefanik LEADING Kathy Hochul in the NY Governor Race, Giving Hope to millions of New Yorkers Terrified of a Mamdani Communist Takeover_**

We are gaining momentum and poised to flip NY in 2026!
This state is now in play because New York Democrats stand with Mamdani while I stand with New Yorkers who want safe streets, affordable communities, and real leadership.
I’ve been the loudest, strongest, and most consistent fighter to **SAVE NEW YORK** and **_[with your help I will continue to FIGHT BACK against Mamdani and FIRE HOCHUL once and for all](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/bs5i/?__vbtrk=MzMxNjM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMxNjM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_.**

[STAND WITH ELISE](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/bs5i/?__vbtrk=MzMxNjM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMxNjM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
There’s no hiding from this fight. Kathy Hochul has decided to make Zohran Mamdani the new face of the Democrat Party.
If we don’t hold the line here their radical platform will become the model for the next wave of Democrats across the country.
But with your continued support we can deliver a victory in New York next year that will replace Hochul with America First leadership and secure several battleground House seats that are crucial to preserving the GOP House Majority.
I am counting on you to help us seize this moment.

_Your fellow patriot,_
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/bs5i/?__vbtrk=MzMxNjM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMxNjM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
**Elise Stefanik**
Proud New Yorker
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/bs5i/?__vbtrk=MzMxNjM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMxNjM0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

name: The Turning Point USA Family
subject: Charlie always believed this day would come
body:
Even now... it still doesn't feel real that we lost Charlie.

| | | [](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkbGO3CAQhp8Gd17BwMK6cBHFspTirk1pDTCx0WHsAPZp3z5yskXa60bzfzOfRvPzbXrDEEOa33Gl3p1hymVyC-YYCOMnPoulGOgkX5dQJuBwb4Vo-f3iRON757WEhnphNEgDD-iape9QWGMcaWU7YUlJpaziVhtrrIcHNKG_NgnB78A5B33T0oDspLcPJZxDwRQ_do-Vys1tqVA-sYaTQkrbVW3p5ra1if1S616Y_MZgZDAWckemGybyW70IBmM9cgppbvctpNoeBRmMHSdPxuj2l9KuVQq6FknwVlrOyYFEfNyZHMt2ZEeT2zwxOXz_8W6UAsNAH3WdVvLhWJkcaMUQX81_E0wOC2aP5WNC9_sVOVx3DHNicnAfU6QZ3fMVVcrrf4Im937_DDGGog1TfL4Ef--tX__Q2cOfAAAA__9NRp3L)
---
Friend, Even now... it still doesn't feel real that we lost Charlie. His voice, his courage, his conviction -- it shaped all of us. And his vision -- **to build the most faithful and the most patriotic generation in American history** -- is why Turning Point USA exists today. Friend, that's why we have an inspiring update for you... In just the first seven days since Charlie was taken from all of us, Turning Point USA has received more than 54,000 requests from students who want to start or join a chapter:
---
[](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkbGO3CAQhp8Gd17BwMK6cBHFspTirk1pDTCx0WHsAPZp3z5yskXa60bzfzOfRvPzbXrDEEOa33Gl3p1hymVyC-YYCOMnPoulGOgkX5dQJuBwb4Vo-f3iRON757WEhnphNEgDD-iape9QWGMcaWU7YUlJpaziVhtrrIcHNKG_NgnB78A5B33T0oDspLcPJZxDwRQ_do-Vys1tqVA-sYaTQkrbVW3p5ra1if1S616Y_MZgZDAWckemGybyW70IBmM9cgppbvctpNoeBRmMHSdPxuj2l9KuVQq6FknwVlrOyYFEfNyZHMt2ZEeT2zwxOXz_8W6UAsNAH3WdVvLhWJkcaMUQX81_E0wOC2aP5WNC9_sVOVx3DHNicnAfU6QZ3fMVVcrrf4Im937_DDGGog1TfL4Ef--tX__Q2cOfAAAA__9NRp3L)
---
**God is so good.** ✝️🙏🕊️ To give you perspective, Friend... Coming into this school year, Turning Point USA had roughly 900+ college chapters and 1,200+ high school chapters. It took us years to build those chapters... over a decade in fact... _But now?_ These students who signed up? They weren't asked to... They weren't recruited... **They felt it was their time to stand up for this country and honor Charlie.** Because of the life he lived. The faith he had. The love he showed God, his family, and others. The courage he had to speak truth. And his vision of a better America for us all.
---
[](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkbGO3CAQhp8Gd17BwMK6cBHFspTirk1pDTCx0WHsAPZp3z5yskXa60bzfzOfRvPzbXrDEEOa33Gl3p1hymVyC-YYCOMnPoulGOgkX5dQJuBwb4Vo-f3iRON757WEhnphNEgDD-iape9QWGMcaWU7YUlJpaziVhtrrIcHNKG_NgnB78A5B33T0oDspLcPJZxDwRQ_do-Vys1tqVA-sYaTQkrbVW3p5ra1if1S616Y_MZgZDAWckemGybyW70IBmM9cgppbvctpNoeBRmMHSdPxuj2l9KuVQq6FknwVlrOyYFEfNyZHMt2ZEeT2zwxOXz_8W6UAsNAH3WdVvLhWJkcaMUQX81_E0wOC2aP5WNC9_sVOVx3DHNicnAfU6QZ3fMVVcrrf4Im937_DDGGog1TfL4Ef--tX__Q2cOfAAAA__9NRp3L)
---
He always believed this day would come. He used to talk about it all the time -- how the momentum would shift, how young people would rise up with courage, and how a Turning Point USA chapter would be in every college and high school in America. And now... _It 's happening._ Faster than he could have imagined. Bigger than we thought possible. And more beautiful than we can even describe. **Again, Friend, God is so good.** Since we lost Charlie, we've also felt SO MUCH love... Prayers. Emails. Messages. Stories about how Charlie's voice changed lives... or your children's or grandchildren's lives. And so many supporters have asked, _" What can I do?"_ As you read this, our team is working around the clock to follow up with every chapter request. And we're going to need help equipping them with our activism kits. **Every new chapter receives these kits from Turning Point USA with everything they need to get started -- including: ** ✅ Leadership materials
✅ Pocket Constitutions
✅ Chapter training tools
✅ American flags & patriotic gear Friend, I'm sure you'd believe it when we say it... _this won 't be a small task. _ But we're going to do it. Whatever it takes. **_For Charlie._** _And here 's how YOU can help with this..._ **Every $25 you give today helps stock another activism kit -- ready to deploy into a new Turning Point USA student chapter...** How many of these activism kits can you help provide to the wave of new student chapters that are just getting started?

clean_body: Even now... it still doesn't feel real that we lost Charlie. | | | Friend, Even now... it still doesn't feel real that we lost Charlie. His voice, his courage, his conviction -- it shaped all of us. And his vision -- to build the most faithful and the most patriotic generation in American history -- is why Turning Point USA exists today. Friend, that's why we have an inspiring update for you... In just the first seven days since Charlie was taken from all of us, Turning Point USA has received more than 54,000 requests from students who want to start or join a chapter: God is so good. ✝️🙏🕊️ To give you perspective, Friend... Coming into this school year, Turning Point USA had roughly 900+ college chapters and 1,200+ high school chapters. It took us years to build those chapters... over a decade in fact... But now? These students who signed up? They weren't asked to... They weren't recruited... They felt it was their time to stand up for this country and honor Charlie. Because of the life he lived. The faith he had. The love he showed God, his family, and others. The courage he had to speak truth. And his vision of a better America for us all. He always believed this day would come. He used to talk about it all the time -- how the momentum would shift, how young people would rise up with courage, and how a Turning Point USA chapter would be in every college and high school in America. And now... It 's happening. Faster than he could have imagined. Bigger than we thought possible. And more beautiful than we can even describe. Again, Friend, God is so good. Since we lost Charlie, we've also felt SO MUCH love... Prayers. Emails. Messages. Stories about how Charlie's voice changed lives... or your children's or grandchildren's lives. And so many supporters have asked, " What can I do?" As you read this, our team is working around the clock to follow up with every chapter request. And we're going to need help equipping them with our activism kits. Every new chapter receives these kits from Turning Point USA with everything they need to get started -- including: ✅ Leadership materials ✅ Pocket Constitutions ✅ Chapter training tools ✅ American flags & patriotic gear Friend, I'm sure you'd believe it when we say it... this won 't be a small task. But we're going to do it. Whatever it takes. For Charlie. And here 's how YOU can help with this... Every $25 you give today helps stock another activism kit -- ready to deploy into a new Turning Point USA student chapter... How many of these activism kits can you help provide to the wave of new student chapters that are just getting started? Friend, if there was ever a more immediate way you could help make Charlie's vision a reality... This is it. Also... Here 's one more powerful thing you can do: 📩 If you have a son, daughter, grandchild, niece, nephew -- or any young person in your life -- who wants to get involved... who wants to join or start a chapter... Please send them this link: Friend, if you've already given... If you've already donated... If you've already been a voice of encouragement and strength in this painful week... If you've prayed, been praying, and continue to pray... Thank you. We can never say it enough. If you've read this far, we want you to know this... You 're the reason Charlie's mission and vision will grow for generations to come. God is so good. For Charlie, -The Turning Point USA Family P.S. Please know, Friend, if you can't afford to help right now, all we ask for are your prayers. Charlie would have wanted you to pray first -- and if that's all you can give today, we 're grateful. But if you're able to help... if you're overjoyed to hear that students are flocking to Charlie's message and mission... Your gift today helps sustain this wave of student inspiration and gets new student chapters off the ground floor... And ensures Charlie's vision is realized faster than we all thought possible. Every $25 you give today helps us stock another activism kit ready to deploy to a new student chapter. Thank you for your help and for your prayers, Friend. God is so good. 🙏 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044 Pop Acta Media | Conservative Innovation PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522 |

My mother was suffering from Alzheimer’s, and my father was living with the effects of a debilitating stroke. Like so many other women, my life was a constant balancing act. But with a lot of help, I did my best to make sure I was doing everything I could in Congress to help working families — and making sure my family had everything they needed to succeed, too. One thing I’ve learned is that in order to set the right priorities and agenda, you’ve got to collaborate. **So I’m writing today to ask if you will help me in my role to keep House Democrats united and focused on the concerns of our constituents by sharing your priorities with me.** [**Peter, we are committed to making progress for Americans despite GOP attempts to advance their right-wing MAGA agenda. Let me know: Which issues are you most concerned about? name: Gretchen Whitmer
subject: Progress needs people like you to step up.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight Like Hell PAC
body:
We’ll help you take that first step toward running for office. |
#
---
Team, Last night, Democrats across the country turned out in huge numbers — _and we won big._ In Virginia, voters elected Abigail Spanberger, who put pragmatism over partisanship and became the first woman elected Governor of Virginia. In New Jersey, voters elected Mikie Sherrill, who relentlessly focused on lowering costs and helping working families get ahead. **I was proud to support both of these bold leaders on the campaign trail, and I’m even more excited to cheer them on as they fight like hell for their states.** It felt good. It _should_ feel good. Because every win is proof that when people step up, organize, and care deeply about their communities, we move forward. Now, we need to keep that energy going by growing the next generation of leaders who will fight like hell for progress. **So I’m asking you — _yes, you_ — to think about running for office.** I know that might sound a little wild. I felt the same way once. But the truth is, the best leaders aren’t always the ones who always planned to run; they’re the ones who care enough to step up when it matters. Whether it’s for your local school board, city council, or state legislature, I want to hear from you. And if you’re even a little curious about what running could look like, now’s the time to find out. **[Add your name right now to say you’re interested in running for office. We’ll connect you with the people, the tools, and the resources to get started.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118729464/580702489/439554760?contactdata=NDictJcwxzlV2UG8JHVVtayfqMpUJySq1R8OhIiNMCNaD7aIKNzpWG1JK4q0VQl9L6s0F/2I/z5T0474siRVkq9yLBclRgFHcfn3ou3f4YORxQLROh0Avp1WWPPXczq14OPJRN2kUs+H3UzsSHIh1Q==&refcode=AU_FLHP_EG_GEN_RES_AC_20251105_PM1_V1_S3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY0NjIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzA0ODNlNGItODJiYS1mMDExLThlNjEtMDAyMjQ4MjRlMDhkIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ipxHRJRxdy1UVZOnGwfD05Zk9n8Q1ImTbwyzEdyb_xU=&emci=a6483e4b-82ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&emdi=c0483e4b-82ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&ceid=1028753)** | | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118729465/580702491/439554760?contactdata=NDictJcwxzlV2UG8JHVVtayfqMpUJySq1R8OhIiNMCNaD7aIKNzpWG1JK4q0VQl9L6s0F/2I/z5T0474siRVkq9yLBclRgFHcfn3ou3f4YORxQLROh0Avp1WWPPXczq14OPJRN2kUs+H3UzsSHIh1Q==&refcode=AU_FLHP_EG_GEN_RES_AC_20251105_PM1_V1_S3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY0NjIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzA0ODNlNGItODJiYS1mMDExLThlNjEtMDAyMjQ4MjRlMDhkIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ipxHRJRxdy1UVZOnGwfD05Zk9n8Q1ImTbwyzEdyb_xU=&emci=a6483e4b-82ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&emdi=c0483e4b-82ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&ceid=1028753)

You don’t need to have it all figured out. You also don’t need a fancy title or a big network. **You just need to care.**
Yesterday showed that the future depends on us working together. With your help, we can keep that momentum going.
— Gretchen
Fight Like Hell PAC

Lansing, MI 48901

clean_body: We’ll help you take that first step toward running for office. | Team, Last night, Democrats across the country turned out in huge numbers — and we won big. In Virginia, voters elected Abigail Spanberger, who put pragmatism over partisanship and became the first woman elected Governor of Virginia. In New Jersey, voters elected Mikie Sherrill, who relentlessly focused on lowering costs and helping working families get ahead. I was proud to support both of these bold leaders on the campaign trail, and I’m even more excited to cheer them on as they fight like hell for their states. It felt good. It should feel good. Because every win is proof that when people step up, organize, and care deeply about their communities, we move forward. Now, we need to keep that energy going by growing the next generation of leaders who will fight like hell for progress. So I’m asking you — yes, you — to think about running for office. I know that might sound a little wild. I felt the same way once. But the truth is, the best leaders aren’t always the ones who always planned to run; they’re the ones who care enough to step up when it matters. Whether it’s for your local school board, city council, or state legislature, I want to hear from you. And if you’re even a little curious about what running could look like, now’s the time to find out. | | You don’t need to have it all figured out. You also don’t need a fancy title or a big network. You just need to care. Yesterday showed that the future depends on us working together. With your help, we can keep that momentum going. — Gretchen Fight Like Hell PAC PO Box 15093 Lansing, MI 48901 United States ** | mailto:info@fightlikehellpac.org| | Paid for by Fight Like Hell PAC. FightLikeHellPAC.org. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee

body:

name: Americans for Responsible Innovation
subject: Stacking the Chips in America’s favor
body:
The latest on ARI's work on AI governance

| | | | | |
---
|
---
[|
---](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=d08c36e642&e=a07d75591f)
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
Between trade negotiations, the NDAA, and new research on biosecurity risks, October has been a packed month. ARI weighed in on risky measures like preemption and a potential rollback of export controls, and we’ve had the opportunity to move the ball forward on measures like the GAIN AI Act and gene synthesis screening policy.Here’s a look at what we’ve been up to:
---
| |
### NOT JUST A BARGAINING CHIP
Last week, President Trump met with China’s President Xi Jinping to negotiate a new trade deal. On the table were the world’s most advanced AI chips, versions of Nvidia’s Blackwell GPU, potentially up for sale.The stakes on export controls for US sales of Nvidia’s Blackwell chips are incredibly high. Right now, the US maintains a measurable lead in AI innovation, partly due to greater access to compute. But [new research](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=7c9ae86349&e=a07d75591f) from the Institute For Progress finds that America’s compute lead could be erased - or even flip to China - should the US end export controls on Nvidia’s B30A chip, a Blackwell-based GPU.
Prior to Trump’s meeting with Xi, ARI [circulated a memo](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=814764b0f0&e=a07d75591f) with the Administration detailing these concerns and highlighting IFP’s new research. Following the meeting, we were pleased to see the President shut down speculation that the US would allow Nvidia to export its most advanced chips following his meeting with President Xi. On Friday of last week, ARI sent a letter to the Admin thanking the President and negotiating team for keeping the most advanced AI chips in the US.

---
[|
---](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=ca3f3adf56&e=a07d75591f)
| | While the Administration was negotiating its trade deal last week, Congress was hard at work finalizing a conferenced version of the National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA), including groundbreaking legislation on chip exports. Included in the Senate’s version of the must-pass annual defense package is a bill called the GAIN AI Act, which requires chipmakers like Nvidia to give US customers a right of first refusal before selling advanced AI chips to countries of concern. This past couple weeks saw some major developments for that legislation:
* **Industry Support:** On Oct. 24, a leading Microsoft exec [spoke in favor](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=41b352b44e&e=a07d75591f) of the GAIN AI Act.
* **House Version:** Last Thursday, a bipartisan team [introduced](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=33fb287aa6&e=a07d75591f) a House version of the bill.
* **NatSec Leaders Speak Up:** Later the same day, [ARI led a coalition](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=4019eb522d&e=a07d75591f) of national security experts, including former defense officials, in urging congressional leadership to include the GAIN AI Act in the NDAA. _(_[_Politico Morning Tech_](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=c7068bc154&e=a07d75591f) _)_

---
[|
---](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=4422ff2ef4&e=a07d75591f)
| |
### AI & BIOSECURITY: A DANGEROUS LOOPHOLE
At the beginning of October, a group of researchers, including a leading researcher at Microsoft, revealed that AI models are capable of helping users design dangerous synthetic proteins that sneak past safety screening protocols. The findings were alarming, exposing a gap in how synthetic biology tools are governed in the AI era. In response, ARI convened a virtual panel featuring Microsoft researcher and research author Bruce Wittman, Dean Ball from the Foundation for American Innovation (formerly Senior Advisor at OSTP), and industry leaders to discuss how policy must adapt to these emerging threats. The conversation centered on safeguards, accountability, and the risks of open distribution of dangerous capabilities.
---
| [Watch the Full Panel Discussion](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=d50dbb2e3b&e=a07d75591f)
---
[|
---](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=cb51cfaa3a&e=a07d75591f)
| |
### FOUR AI POLICY MISTAKES WE CAN’T AFFORD
This month, ARI Senior Policy Director Morgan Plummer launched a new op-ed series in War on the Rocks, a leading defense policy publication, outlining four critical AI policy choices that US lawmakers must get right. The first piece warns against overreliance on voluntary commitments and highlights the stakes of explicitly determining how AI is deployed in the US military. [**Read the first op-ed in War on the Rocks.**](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=d01314f2ee&e=a07d75591f) This week, War on the Rocks published the second piece in the series, [Warfighters, Not Engineers, Decide What AI Can Be Trusted](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=ae3e1e841b&e=a07d75591f), which dives into the first of four policy choices Morgan urges policymakers to decide: who defines “trustworthy AI” in the Pentagon? In this sharp op-ed, Morgan argues that unless the military reorganizes AI acquisition around operators’ trust and battlefield reality, the Department of Defense risks making AI systems into yet another over-engineered, under-deployed product. More essays are forthcoming in the series, which aims to bring national security and governance perspectives into sharper focus.
Step by step, these efforts are strengthening the foundation for responsible AI governance.
Thanks for being part of the growing ARI community. Whether you're organizing with us, attending panels, or sharing our research—your support helps drive this work forward and to keep pushing for responsible innovation.

---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | [](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=9c78d6d450&e=a07d75591f)
---
[](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=c86735afc6&e=a07d75591f)
---
[](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=98e73303c0&e=a07d75591f)
---
| | | | |

---
|

[_why did I get this?_](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/about?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=78defcc9fe&e=a07d75591f&c=6603013fd2) [unsubscribe from this list](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=78defcc9fe&t=b&e=a07d75591f&c=6603013fd2) [update subscription preferences](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/profile?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=78defcc9fe&e=a07d75591f&c=6603013fd2)

name: Keith Ellison
subject: We did it, Peter
disclaimer_text: paid for by the Keith Ellison for Attorney General committee, PO Box 17224, Minneapolis, MN 55417
body:
Peter,

I can’t thank you enough for everything you’ve done to support my campaign for reelection. Because of you, we didn’t just reach our Founding Donor goal — we exceeded it.

When the stakes are high, I know I can count on this grassroots team. And right now, the stakes couldn’t be higher.

Donald Trump and his MAGA allies are already working to turn Minnesota red. If we want our state to be a place where the rule of law prevails over power and privilege, we can’t let that happen. Building a strong, people-powered movement is the only way to stop them.

If we work together and give it our all, there’s nothing we can’t overcome. I look forward to fighting side-by-side with you.

Thank you,

Keith Ellison

Attorney General Keith Ellison is the People’s Lawyer, fighting to help people afford their lives and live with safety, dignity, and respect.

CONTRIBUTE: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em251105ke?refcode=other

Prepared and paid for by the Keith Ellison for Attorney General committee, PO Box 17224, Minneapolis, MN 55417

Minneapolis, MN 55417
United States

name: Team Kelly Ayotte Press
subject: Governor Ayotte Invites NYC Businesses to Relocate to New Hampshire
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kelly for New Hampshire, Chris Connelly Treasurer, PO Box 4723 Manchester, NH 03108
body:
The Governor’s campaign is running ads in Manhattan today to share her invitation.

| | | | | [|
---](https://kellyfornh.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b706db8cb07a975cbe2ecb8b7&id=a312d356a4&e=38cb9f3df0)
| | | | | | **Safe, Prosperous, and Free: What New York Used to Be** _Ayotte: Big Apple Businesses Welcome in the Granite State_
---
| | **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE:** November 5, 2025
**CONTACT:**[ press@kellyfornh.com](mailto:press@kellyfornh.com?subject=&body=)
**NASHUA, N.H. —** In response to the election of Zohran Mamdani as Mayor of New York City, Governor Kelly Ayotte has invited businesses seeking lower taxes and opportunity to grow without government interference to relocate to New Hampshire: a state that’s safe, prosperous, and free.
“New Hampshire has a lot to offer anyone who is looking to leave NYC ahead of the Mamdani regime. We are the safest state in the country, the best for economic opportunity, and we have some of the lowest taxes anywhere. Whether you own a business or just want to keep more of your hard earned money - come on up to the Granite State. We’d love to have you,” **said Governor Ayotte.**
Today, the Governor’s campaign ran ads in Manhattan inviting Big Apple businesses to make the switch to the Granite State.
---
[|
---](https://kellyfornh.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b706db8cb07a975cbe2ecb8b7&id=77304098d0&e=38cb9f3df0)
| | New Hampshire has been ranked #1 in America for economic opportunity, taxpayer return on investment, child wellbeing, and public safety.
Video of the billboard truck can be downloaded [here](https://kellyfornh.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b706db8cb07a975cbe2ecb8b7&id=62183560d9&e=38cb9f3df0).
_###_
---
| | Paid for by Kelly for New Hampshire, Chris Connelly Treasurer, PO Box 4723 Manchester, NH 03108
---

name: Cori Bush
subject: Zohran’s campaign was proof that people-power works.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cori For Us
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251105_zohran_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251105_zohran_act]
Yesterday was historic. Across the country, voters showed up in incredible numbers. In New York City, Zohran Mamdani’s win as mayor reminded us why this movement matters.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251105_zohran_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251105_zohran_act]
Zohran ran an honest campaign rooted in solutions, care, and community. He led a relentlessly hopeful fight for basic dignity, even when political and media elites tried to cast it as something dangerous or extreme. His campaign is proof that people-power works.
To Zohran, his team, volunteers, and everyone who showed up to vote: you did the thing! You showed those in power what happens when everyday people link arms and refuse to back down.
These are the same principles that have fueled our movement here in St. Louis from day one. Early grassroots and organizational support from endorsers like Justice Democrats, Our Revolution, St. Louis DSA, and so many more show that our message is resonating.
And Peter, this movement is alive, growing, and needed now more than ever. Every volunteer, every supporter, every act of courage yesterday made a difference, but our fight for justice and equity doesn’t end there.
If you’re able, please consider chipping in today to keep the momentum going. The only way forward is one powered by people who support bold, unbought, and unbossed candidates. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251105_zohran_act]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=x_251105_zohran_act] DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=x_251105_zohran_act]
DONATE $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=x_251105_zohran_act] DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=x_251105_zohran_act]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=x_251105_zohran_act] OTHER [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane_other=true&refcode=x_251105_zohran_act]
In solidarity,
Cori
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251105_zohran_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251105_zohran_act] Cori Bush is a pastor, nurse, survivor, and formerly unhoused single mother of two who knows what it means to struggle and what it means to rise .
As a Congresswoman, she has always shown up when it’s hardest: securing disaster relief, lowering insulin costs, and delivering millions for our communities.
Across St. Louis, families are being priced out and pushed out. Cori Bush is fighting back for affordable housing, fair wages, and utilities as a human right. Be part of this people-first movement. Join Team Cori today and sign up to volunteer. [https://www.coribush.org]
DONATE TODAY [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251105_zohran_act]

St. Louis, MO 63132

name: Chrissy Houlahan
subject: What a good night for Democrats!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chrissy Houlahan for Congress
body:
Last night, we all watched as voters across the country soundly rejected President Trump and the Republicans who enable him.
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118730825/580707533/-1072566757?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNjEzNDAzMC04NmJhLWYwMTEtOGU2MS0wMDIyNDgyNGUwOGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=86m-58s-rXbu2RCj2mRO8x1vUrD0l2klwlmKl937yQc=&emci=86c6f2e2-61ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&emdi=06134030-86ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&ceid=21278485)
---
Derek,

Last night, we all watched as voters across the country soundly rejected President Trump and the Republicans who enable him. We all worked hard for this, we deserve to celebrate, and to be a little relieved that so many voters, people who don’t live and breathe politics the way many of us do, are with us. I will be cheering, for a while, the Democratic victories of my friends Abigail Spanberger in **Virginia** and Mikie Sherrill in **New Jersey** , the Supreme Court races in **Pennsylvania** , the redistricting ballot measure in **California** , and two statewide seats in **Georgia**.

As I write this, I’m in Washington, D.C., working to end the shutdown, which is now the longest in our nation’s history. People are suffering needlessly. It’s long past time to end this.
I promise to continue leading and fighting for you. I promise to keep my foot on the gas so we do exactly the same thing one year from now. I hope you’ll hang in there with me. The stakes are simply too high not to.
There's work to be done,

Chrissy

name: Georgia Dems
subject: Big wins for Georgia and the nation!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic Party of Georgia
body:
Dr. Alicia Johnson and Peter Hubbard flipped two seats on the Public Service Commission!

If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/502xl98alkm3J5MS-c04xA2?unsubscribedata=hFHt3cMsKlnGPr3a%2FRFGqwzBPVgSXAZYCvLwxG%2BhN6B284dEM1LOtvqKwRSY2SvuhiIb7oBM4n9d0dCxMljaREAU9BAxbkOCiRCN2qtt8EoBupMV51g4vXmgbzz86okxD%2F9W%2BjvETLBBfYme7lwfLlSK0SrkLj5Tax5GQDmbrQZlemvf5iXAst7jqgH9RLTTHNV1IjxsRwBzay6Xasu2U8vIy3iP3jIqW5bVAoUzo7jX27XP%2FC2517lzvr44Ex6Lbvvz%2Bjmc28%2BJ9wrMBiiAn9jHgHTPWcvCngoSt2uSF2BebRGiUr8X%2BNAkUcM2%2FNgBq9mG2jaxqQ6jPhjVC7zxaDOf3aMPCetbY60PN6zH8KmeE%2Bon%2Fz3F%2FRCbuDAwXdxeoV111vRF9iJhGkCfZ%2BmenFsJ7mTs0ozssl%2F%2BzQj6sP6uRu03vG132P75YzK%2FmZuk)
---
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118730876/580707666/2021534627?source=SBD_DPG_EM_FR_110525_POSTELECTION_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjNlM2QzNi04NmJhLWYwMTEtOGU2MS0wMDIyNDgyNGUwOGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LvDoQxp-agYnhoXdN_PQfUbtd0FHYdwbFvlxsEH9aCs=)
---
Last night was incredible, Peter. We won! Dr. Alicia Johnson and Peter Hubbard Just Flipped Two Public Service Commission Seats BLUE! Peter, Dr. Alicia Johnson and Peter Hubbard just flipped two Public Service Commission seats BLUE! After three decades of Republican control and skyrocketing power bills, _Georgians said enough is enough and voted to put people ahead of corporate profits._ And it’s not just Georgia — there were big Democratic wins up and down the ballot nationwide. Americans showed up to the polls fed up with rising grocery prices, the widening wealth gap, and blatant corruption of the Republican party. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118730878/580707670/-2004472022?amount=10&source=SBD_DPG_EM_FR_110525_POSTELECTION_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjNlM2QzNi04NmJhLWYwMTEtOGU2MS0wMDIyNDgyNGUwOGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LvDoQxp-agYnhoXdN_PQfUbtd0FHYdwbFvlxsEH9aCs=) So Peter, I hope you take a moment to celebrate our hard-won victories, because tomorrow we’ve got to get back to work. We’re carrying this momentum forward to elect and re-elect strong Democrats at every level of the ticket, and we have our work cut out for us. **The 2026 Senate race in Georgia will be the most important race of the year, and our democracy is on the line.** It’s up to us to stop Donald Trump and Project 2025’s dangerous agenda. We do that by defending Georgia’s blue Senate seat and taking back the Democratic Senate majority. [It’s going to take a lot of strategic organizing, and that takes resources. That’s why we’ve set a goal of raising $10,000 TODAY to get started.
