name: Senator Mark Huizenga
subject: Big News: Mark Huizenga is
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Huizenga - 3841 Butterworth Street SW, Walker, MI 49534[View email in browser](https://mailchi
body:
| | | | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| |
Dear Friend,
I wanted you to hear it from me first: I’m officially running for re-election to the Michigan Senate.
Serving the people of Kent and Ottawa Counties has been one of the greatest honors of my life. From day one, I’ve focused on common-sense, bipartisan solutions that make life better for families, job creators, and communities across West Michigan.
Together, we’ve delivered real results, from tax relief for working families, to record investments in our schools, to stronger support for public safety and mental health services. We’ve also secured important local projects like the Fruit Ridge Bridge improvements and expanded our beautiful trail and greenspace systems.
But there’s still more work to do. Our communities deserve leaders who will focus on people, not politics, who will work across the aisle to solve problems, not create new ones. That’s always been my approach, because no party has a monopoly on good ideas, and cooperation delivers the best results for the people of Kent and Ottawa Counties.
I’m proud of what we’ve accomplished together, and I’m excited for the opportunity to continue serving you. With your support, I’ll keep fighting for safer streets, stronger schools, lower taxes, and a government that works for you.
Thank you for believing in this mission and for being such an important part of our West Michigan community. To get involved in the campaign or learn more about the important work we are doing, visit: VoteHuizenga.com. You can also read our press release below.
With gratitude,
**Mark Huizenga**
State Senator, 30th District
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| | **Thank You!** Your efforts are truly impactful. By contributing, you’re helping us advocate for the changes and reforms our community needs. Every donation, no matter the size, fuels our mission and ensures a brighter future for all. Join us in shaping a better tomorrow; donate today!

name: Raja Krishnamoorthi
subject: two brown guys with funny names from Illinois
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action
body:
I got my first major job in Illinois politics working for another guy from Illinois with a funny name on his first Senate campaign.
| | | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T9e6652fb-f600-4206-8e89-62592c790fb8/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
| Hey Peter, My name is Raja Krishnamoorthi. I’m a brown guy with a funny name from Peoria, Illinois, running for US Senate to fight for a better future for hardworking Americans. I got my first major job in Illinois politics working for another guy from Illinois with a funny name on his first Senate campaign. His name was Barack Obama. [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T618542c4-85e4-418f-903f-4bfa67c090f5/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) When I first started working for President Obama back in 1999, it was a far cry from where he’d eventually end up. He was about as different from the mainstream politicians as they come — he came from an immigrant background and had a “funny name”. I’d never thought that someone like me could run for office – let alone win. [](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta193f0b1-fea9-4076-83c7-45d8cabfdac7/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Now, I know it can seem like we’re a long way away from the era of “hope and change”. I certainly miss having a real President in office instead of a wannabe dictator like Donald Trump. Right now, it’s clear that we need real leaders with a backbone to stand up to Donald Trump’s insanity and fight for the same issues that President Obama championed. Because every family – regardless of who they are, where they live, or how funny their name might sound – deserves the chance to work hard and succeed. [That’s why I’m running for Senate, Peter. So please, will you take a chance on another brown guy with a funny name from Illinois and split $5, $25, or more between my campaign and Blue Amp Action right now?](https://default.salsalabs.org/Te34a5d9b-d6cf-4b98-b845-699aa604ba17/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade, it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in a democracy, not a dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tc81e5817-0205-4690-b9ce-163b3d907856/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: Jim MacEachern
subject: The Blue Wave Stopped in New Hampshire
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Hampshire Republican State Committee, Chairman Jim MacEachern
body:
The so-called “Blue Wave” fizzled out the moment it hit New Hampshire.

| | | | | [|
---](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=f920b1601a&e=f3aa6419cd)
| | | | | |
Friend,
The so-called “Blue Wave” fizzled out the moment it hit New Hampshire.
Across the country, Democrats may have found headlines — **but here in the Granite State, Republicans WON.**
✅ We won the special election for the State Legislature.✅ Mayor Jay Ruais won re-election in Manchester — in a _blowout_.✅ And we FLIPPED the mayor’s office in Laconia.
While Democrats peddled higher taxes and soft-on-crime policies, New Hampshire Republicans stayed focused on what matters: keeping our communities safe and our state affordable. That’s the message that won — and **it’s proof that when we stay united, disciplined, and well-resourced, we win.**
_But these victories didn’t happen by accident._
They happened because people like you stepped up. You believed in our message. You helped fund the effort to reach voters in every corner of the state. And it worked.
Now, **we need to build on that momentum** — because Democrats are already plotting their comeback. They’re pouring millions of dollars of national money into New Hampshire to try to undo what we’ve built.
Let’s make sure they fail.
[**Chip in HERE to help us keep New Hampshire red, responsible, and free.**](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=397f52aa84&e=f3aa6419cd)
---
| [CONTRIBUTE TODAY >>](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=cf014e048e&e=f3aa6419cd)
---
| |

For New Hampshire,Chairman Jim MacEachern
---

I really hope it’s because my messages haven’t been getting through to you.

clean_body: It’s been a while since I heard from you. | | | | From Trump: You’ve got me worried I’ve been trying to reach you for DAYS, and I’m starting to think that you’re ignoring me. I really hope it’s because my messages haven’t been getting through to you. Because the Fake News has been saying that MAGA support is at an ALL-TIME LOW... But I know you haven’t abandoned me. I’m reaching out because YOUR NAME is at the top of my list of special MAGA Patriots, and I can’t afford to lose you. I know that you have never abandoned me and the MAGA Movement, but it’s been a while since I’ve heard from you... Please, I know that you’re a very busy person, but I could really use your response to some important questions that I have for you. I have always taken into account YOUR thoughts, and I need them now more than ever. THANK YOU. \- President Donald J. body:
Winning is not just about one night. It’s about staying focused and organized, and being willing to do the work.

Wow, everyone. It feels so _good_ to have something to celebrate. Last night’s Democratic victories are a testament to the hard work of grassroots communities, and are a referendum on MAGA Republicans’ attempts to unravel the moral fibers of our county.

But yesterday’s victories don’t guarantee tomorrow’s success. _We have to build it. We have to fight for it. And that starts now._

**[_Can you please pitch in $5 or as much as you can today? Every dollar puts us that much closer to carrying this momentum to 2026 to flip the Senate._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118731715/580710257/1734447991?amount=5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.11.05_Senate-Momentum_B1_F1_S1_C1_30OP_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNTM1NWY2Ni04OWJhLWYwMTEtOGU2MS0wMDIyNDgyNGUwOGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IPSI-xCSMhZbfJgTWx1gRixXx-002wLQA8-2Bho7GgY=)**

name: Squad Victory HQ
subject: Trump said he “would love” to run for a third term.
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20251105-1608-foote&t=1001&akid=1608%2E342411%2EDXYy3f ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

Donald Trump has said it himself: he “would love” to run for a third term.

Even Steve Bannon, who served as Trump’s chief strategist, admits there’s
a plan to keep him in office beyond his constitutional two terms.

This isn’t politics as usual — it’s a direct assault on our democracy. The
22nd Amendment exists to make sure no president gets more than two terms.
Trump doesn’t care. He thinks the rules don’t apply to him. He has no

name: Conservative Liberty Wire
subject: What was Eisenhower's toughest decision?
body:
This powerful moment is the centerpiece of our new video lesson

| | [ ](https://email.alert.conservativelibertywire.com/c/eJyU0LFuwyAQgOGnwVssuMMQDwxVK09N14wWwRcbFccRXEzbp6_SN-j-6xv-82k8-Zjibf7wK7mQ6pjLWBfP1ReKhW7LVikX3h7zQmUECd1BqYPsnh00k-uOQL1pyClrAK3RqJvFaSNRo0bVadQEFvBytP0xTBbU1ZrQRPeklJIdKClRtt5cpTRqUlKrEEwQWvpEmduw3Qrl3XPcKcULZf6uMVMbtrVJbmG-F4EvAgYBw_0rtbGwgKGffy72k_vCAocHryNTXgW-vb6frdaITXbTvcaUYjFWaDmvPqY_k_9_YXfwGwAA___XrGl9)
---
Friend, At 4:15 a.m. on June 5, 1944, **General Dwight D. Eisenhower** faced the most difficult decision of his life: whether to send **156,000 Allied troops** into a **full-scale invasion** of Nazi-occupied France. The weather was treacherous and the risk enormous - and the responsibility rested solely on Eisenhower's shoulders. After giving the go-ahead, Eisenhower quietly wrote a note taking full responsibility in case the mission failed: **_" If any blame or fault attaches to the attempt, it is mine alone."_** He also wrote that, _in the event of success, he would give full credit to the soldiers on the ground_. That handwritten message, never released, never needed, shows what real leadership looks like. Eisenhower wasn't simply commanding an army. He was embodying the kind of integrity and humility we rarely see today. With the full knowledge that success or failure rested in his hands, Eisenhower trusted his judgement and made the decision to proceed. This powerful moment is the centerpiece of our new video lesson: **" General Dwight D. Eisenhower: D-Day and Responsibility."** We believe the best way to prepare young people for complex challenges is to show them how great Americans faced them before. **Will you contribute today to help us continue this work and gain special access to our "General Dwight D. Eisenhower: D-Day and Responsibility" class?** Your support ensures these powerful historical lessons reach students nationwide, shaping informed and principled citizens. The video is free to watch, but as a **special thank you for your support** , we're offering donors access to the free exclusive lesson. Your contribution helps us equip teachers, administrators, and homeschoolers across the nation with stories like this. Stories that use America's proud history to teach the next generation of Americans courage, humility, and leadership. **_Please take just :30s and donate whatever you can right now. It is absolutely critical that every student in America has access to material like this_. ** [**https://secure.anedot.com/bill-of-rights-institute/dday-aug2025**](https://email.alert.conservativelibertywire.com/c/eJyU0LFuwyAQgOGnwVssuMMQDwxVK09N14wWwRcbFccRXEzbp6_SN-j-6xv-82k8-Zjibf7wK7mQ6pjLWBfP1ReKhW7LVikX3h7zQmUECd1BqYPsnh00k-uOQL1pyClrAK3RqJvFaSNRo0bVadQEFvBytP0xTBbU1ZrQRPeklJIdKClRtt5cpTRqUlKrEEwQWvpEmduw3Qrl3XPcKcULZf6uMVMbtrVJbmG-F4EvAgYBw_0rtbGwgKGffy72k_vCAocHryNTXgW-vb6frdaITXbTvcaUYjFWaDmvPqY_k_9_YXfwGwAA___XrGl9) **And remember if you donate today, you immediately receive access to the video.** Eisenhower's story shows what it means to lead with integrity, even when the stakes are life or death. His decision changed history and his legacy can change how we think about responsibility in our own lives. **Your investment in civic education will help students understand the importance of personal responsibility.** Thank you, Bill of Rights Institute | [** Contribute Now >>**](https://email.alert.conservativelibertywire.com/c/eJyU0LFuwyAQgOGnwVssuMMQDwxVK09N14wWwRcbFccRXEzbp6_SN-j-6xv-82k8-Zjibf7wK7mQ6pjLWBfP1ReKhW7LVikX3h7zQmUECd1BqYPsnh00k-uOQL1pyClrAK3RqJvFaSNRo0bVadQEFvBytP0xTBbU1ZrQRPeklJIdKClRtt5cpTRqUlKrEEwQWvpEmduw3Qrl3XPcKcULZf6uMVMbtrVJbmG-F4EvAgYBw_0rtbGwgKGffy72k_vCAocHryNTXgW-vb6frdaITXbTvcaUYjFWaDmvPqY_k_9_YXfwGwAA___XrGl9)

Non-Contribution Account (For TTPAC, Inc.'s Expenditures Other Than Direct Contributions to Federal Committees)

As permitted by law, TTPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, address, occupation, and employer of individuals whose contribution exceeds $200 in an election cycle. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee's name and address. State and local law may require us to collect and report additional information.

Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law.

Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.'s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Contributions to TTPAC, Inc.'s non-contribution account are not tax-deductible for federal income tax purposes.

clean_body: Let me introduce you to Zohran Mamdani, the new SOCIALIST mayor of New York City. | | | Friend, We need to have an honest conversation about who we allow into our country. Let me introduce you to Zohran Mamdani, the new SOCIALIST mayor of New York City. Charlie had Mamdani pegged from Day 1. He knew that, despite his attempts to portray himself as a modern Western man, Mamdani was nothing more than a dangerous radical, hell-bent on devastating New York City with policies straight out of The Communist Manifesto. A self-declared socialist, Mamdani wants to "globalize the intifada" and import values into the West that will destroy New York City and destabilize our civilization. Everything about him runs counter to America First. The fact that he WON in arguably America's greatest city proves that, while we won Congress and the White House,the fight for the heart, soul, and future of America still rages on. Turning Point PAC is working harder than ever to . We're carrying on Charlie's fight to ensure that radical socialists like Mamdani do NOT gain power in other cities, counties, and states across the country. For America, Turning Point PAC Non-Contribution Account (For TTPAC, Inc.'s Expenditures Other Than Direct Contributions to Federal Committees) As permitted by law, TTPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, address, occupation, and employer of individuals whose contribution exceeds $200 in an election cycle. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee's name and address. State and local law may require us to collect and report additional information. Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law. Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.'s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Contributions to TTPAC, Inc.'s non-contribution account are not tax-deductible for federal income tax purposes. Paid for by Turning Point PAC, Inc. | | Peter – The first big election of the new Trump era swept in overwhelming Democratic victories from California to New Jersey last night – and sent a powerful message to a wannabe dictator. We're now one year out from one of the most important midterm elections in our lifetime. We need to keep our momentum strong to send a clear message that we will not put up with Republicans' blatant attacks on democracy. **Mike Johnson declared that "Trump is on the ballot in 2026," and I couldn't agree more. We have our opportunity to push Mike Johnson out of the Speakership and block Trump's destructive congressional agenda.** Building on the wave of last night's successes, taking to the streets to rally, and organizing our communities will turn the tide next year. name: Team Rayfield
subject: Fighting Trump's Tariffs in the US Supreme Court
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dan Rayfield
body:
Today, Oregon fights Trump's illegal tariffs and presidential overreach in the US Supreme Court. | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/danrayfield/oregon-fights-trump-tariffs-in-the-us-supreme-court?e=1768325bd4)
---
| | | [ ](https://danrayfield.us15.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f0c603f9d9801c198f6fc68fa&id=84a9cd3170&e=1768325bd4)
---
|
---
| | | Friend,

Our team just walked out of the US Supreme Court where we're fighting to stop President Trump’s illegal tariffs. Costs are going up, and Americans are facing the consequences of the President's bad trade power grab.

Stopping these illegal tariffs is not a partisan issue – it's common sense. Oregonians and all Americans can't afford the increased costs from these tariffs on food to energy to cars to everything in between. Americans need relief, and that's what we are fighting for now.

**That's why today the Oregon Department of Justice is holding President Trump accountable in our nation's highest court. **

We're fighting for an affordable life for every American. Can you join the fight by donating today?

---
| [$5](https://danrayfield.us15.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f0c603f9d9801c198f6fc68fa&id=a0867dbb57&e=1768325bd4 "$5")
---
| [$10](https://danrayfield.us15.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f0c603f9d9801c198f6fc68fa&id=73ef83acef&e=1768325bd4 "$10")
---
| [$25](https://danrayfield.us15.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f0c603f9d9801c198f6fc68fa&id=dde512cb09&e=1768325bd4 "$25")
---
| **People are waking up to the threat this President poses to their lives.** We saw it yesterday in the election results across the country, and we saw it today in front of our Supreme Court. My job as Attorney General is to use the law to protect everyone from criminals and bad actors. We use the law to keep you safe, we use it to protect your rights, and we use it to protect your pocketbook.

Today, we're proudly using the law to stop Trump from ignoring the law at our expense.

As we await the court's decision, our work continues to defend the rule of law and stop the President's overstep of authority. **This fight carries on, and **[**we're counting on your support**](https://danrayfield.us15.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f0c603f9d9801c198f6fc68fa&id=3745c39e65&e=1768325bd4)** to keep fighting for Oregonians. **

Together, justice will prevail.

Your attorney general,
Dan
---

|

PO Box 2284, Corvallis, OR 97339

name: Team Abdul
subject: Yesterday was huge
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Abdul for U
body:
[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1001&akid=1235%2E251025%2EssGuz2 ]Abdul for US Senate [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1002&akid=1235%2E251025%2EssGuz2 ]Abdul for US Senate

Yesterday was huge,
Peter.

The movement we’ve been talking about – standing behind real progressives
who put people over profits – came to life.

In Michigan and across the country, voters turned out to vote for some
seriously inspiring candidates: Mo Baydoun, Abdullah Hammoud, Mary
Sheffield, Gabriela Santiago-Romero, Denzel McCampbell, and big wins in
New York, Virginia, and New Jersey.

Yesterday showed the world what we already know: this movement has the
legs to win.

Now, it's time to get out, knock on doors, hold rallies, and share our
vision for Michigan. But we need the grassroots resources to make it
happen.

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20251105-2-ak.j5FGZg&amount=10&refcode=em20251105-1233&t=1004&akid=1235%2E251025%2EssGuz2 ]Please chip in anything you can today:

Thank you,

Team Abdul

Yesterday showed the world what we already know: this movement has the legs to win. Now, it's time to get out, knock on doors, hold rallies, and share our vision for Michigan. But we need the grassroots resources to make it happen. [ ]Please chip in anything you can today: [ ]Donate $10 ›› Thank you, Team Abdul

| | Friend,I won’t mince words: our democracy is facing a crisis on two fronts. In Washington, extremists have shut down our government, holding the paychecks of our federal workers hostage to their political games. And back home in Texas, their allies are using this chaos as cover to ram through an illegal, discriminatory gerrymander designed to silence our community's voice in Congress. It is a coordinated attack on our government and our district. I am fighting back with everything I have on both fronts, but I cannot do it alone. Yesterday was an incredible stand for our Democracy. clean_body: We need government of the people, by the people, for the people.| | | | | Friend,I won’t mince words: our democracy is facing a crisis on two fronts. In Washington, extremists have shut down our government, holding the paychecks of our federal workers hostage to their political games. And back home in Texas, their allies are using this chaos as cover to ram through an illegal, discriminatory gerrymander designed to silence our community's voice in Congress. It is a coordinated attack on our government and our district. I am fighting back with everything I have on both fronts, but I cannot do it alone. Yesterday was an incredible stand for our Democracy. But today, we have to start winning. Will you be one of our first November donors, to keep the "Fight Back" fund going and help me fight for you? = = = = = = = Thank you for standing with me when it matters most. This is a man who wants government-run grocery stores. He praised George Soros-style soft-on-crime policies. He thinks the problem after 9/11 was that his aunt got dirty looks—not that Americans were murdered in cold blood. He wants to rip down statues of Columbus. **How did someone like that get elected?****_Because too many of the sane people had already fled New York City._** Since I became Governor, we’ve gone from 300,000 fewer registered Republicans to 1.4 million more. Center-right voters from every state have been flowing into Florida. People are voting with their feet, friend.

But when bad leftist policies drive out these voters, it leaves the remaining electorate even more left. It’s a downward spiral that ends with radical leftists like Mamdani winning our cities. **I welcome all who come seeking freedom, but I’d rather export the Florida Blueprint than import every conservative to the Free State of Florida.** **[That’s why I started RON PAC.](http://links.email.ronpac.com/s/c/45fHnnYngMxeQDNDxeC-Y8SeUnuksFY9WED3srW8zbzAD0anVgBd76QWrgpgajIeqHbeNQ0NXahE2MHXAJK03uO_Sl7XkeOvgmRvC-oszDCFjsl-M1qEV9jowgEOTZi_H4ZJF9YS7ZTurxiGC-FmYOdirWtdOCrkR3B3oyVKEqkOwjJidgrqYWHM6qySBPwG1OIKvzB0CR4gXww-NGx4VVvX9oTXwaAUwEkYEvI_oYYzTk4HlIrG_yHVkP0xmtJG0Dl3JTA1tI2nGO3765j3CcwgJSfd1VurPGQGI9bXaUZ5HeKJ0n_MmhNyBtjBaL05Q8a4xDqs06kS1W7JdFiW1y4WvKFKhD1y_RJKFgNgnEo7oBDoYx8hMrUF/xaZ4cO0-DY40yiE6yULC8mPimrdd3S6I/14)** You shouldn’t have to uproot your life to get good governance. We should not be in retreat. **Let’s take the fight to them. Let’s bring Florida values—freedom, law and order, prosperity—to every corner of this country.** We’re electing bold, battle-tested conservatives across America, in every state, at every level. In 2024, we backed fighters like Tim Sheehy & Dave McCormick. Today, they are fighting for our values in Washington. And we’re just getting started. In 2026, we’re going to elect even MORE fighters. But I need your help to grow this movement: **[>>> CHIP IN TO RON PAC NOW – Help export the Florida Blueprint <<<](http://links.email.ronpac.com/s/c/vlft7MJbMavbyE9QaA_Nzlej-iexSDMIb1UprUXlXMuEocPtxGxMq0THDzqMbfT3Wjy5X12RD40T7I7lSX1-VFRnC3s18Zae4GbHgt87uOzAIh09MvbKSS9khgzQKRrSDlIMJPFqdE-YOE_xHxuo4QXMhebSUeQsV6UTwvoi6GwLta-ZpIx4Fvwl4e9HygoL91a-hd9jkVBK3EE2gES64yM4qUozWBPsAjw-1gRBCdY5tPOi0qY8tyOw2LYdq5oPAwlfycTT2qk-AK3KCpwNrLFW41OQY_p6Ppw0DOGbsmQSwVxlYN5u6snRCZQWSr6SXtgtzY6IZO95LLbCjsyUjSdRHLXkGsi2XkxNsIdWnxkikPef876iybY-/rAhVU5CRohcob3hszvojOdZNbnozXjmK/14)** New Yorkers are fleeing. More are coming. We’ll be ready for them. **But if we don’t act now, we’ll be stuck in a cycle of losing cities, losing states, and losing the country.** Help us fight back and win. Let’s take America back—one battleground at a time.

| **“ The government shutdown is now the longest in U.S. history.”**
\- _NPR, 11/5_ ❌ **42 million Americans** haven't received their SNAP food assistance benefits. ❌ **1.4 million federal workers** have missed their first full paycheck. ❌ **The economy has already lost at least $7 billion** and counting — money that will _never_ be recovered. _And House Republicans refuse to even call their members back into session to negotiate an end to this mess._ **Let’s be clear about why:** Republicans won't extend ACA subsidies that keep healthcare affordable for millions of Americans. They'd rather let families go hungry and workers go unpaid than _compromise._ **This is Republican cruelty at its worst.** **[Sign the petition: Tell Republicans to end the shutdown NOW and reopen the government.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118733119/580714625/-1315357384?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNS8xLzc4OTg3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ5MmVlMTM5LThiYmEtZjAxMS04ZTYxLTAwMjI0ODI0ZTA4ZCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=JdSiotMLvqIveLMUAAlb6caqqpVKNswJ0ClORGaSc0Q=&emci=ab22cf9d-a5b9-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=d92ee139-8bba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&ceid=16951753)** **[SIGN THE PETITION](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118733120/580714626/-1315357384?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNS8xLzc4OTg3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ5MmVlMTM5LThiYmEtZjAxMS04ZTYxLTAwMjI0ODI0ZTA4ZCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=JdSiotMLvqIveLMUAAlb6caqqpVKNswJ0ClORGaSc0Q=&emci=ab22cf9d-a5b9-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=d92ee139-8bba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&ceid=16951753)** \- Unite the Country

name: Ken Martin (via Democrats.org)
subject: Last night:
body:
---
Derek, From New Jersey and Virginia to Pennsylvania and California, to Georgia and Maine, American voters just delivered a reckoning to Donald Trump — and a resurgence for Democrats. New Jersey elected Mikie Sherrill. Virginia elected Abigail Spanberger. New York City elected Zohran Mamdani. Pennsylvania protected its Supreme Court from right-wing billionaires. Albany, Atlanta, Boston, Charlotte, Cincinnati, Cleveland, and Pittsburgh elected Democratic mayors. Georgia elected its first non-federal statewide Democratic officials in nearly 20 years. California passed the Election Rigging Response Act. **Our Democratic candidates, no matter where they are, no matter how they fit into our big-tent Party, are meeting voters at the kitchen table, not the gilded ballroom.** [**Now, it’s up to us to keep this momentum going into the midterms. If you can, pitch in $7 to the DNC now to help us elect more candidates like these all over the country.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2025?refcode=em_20251105_5178_fr&amount=7&refcode2=5178_22930333_UJ4v-z&akid=5178%2E22930333%2EUJ4v-z) We all remember what Donald Trump promised. He promised to lower costs on Day One. He promised to make the economy work for all of us. He promised to break the system. But Donald Trump _is_ the system. And the only thing he’s managed to break is the economy. Today, costs are higher than ever. Health insurance premiums are set to double for millions of Americans next year. Groceries are out of control. Utility bills have skyrocketed. The list goes on. **Last night, voters sent a message: We see Democrats meeting us where we are, and we see Democrats fighting for us.** One year from now, in the midterms, our ability to stop Trump in his tracks runs directly through the Democratic Party. Between now and next November, the job of this Party — my job — is to show voters exactly what we stand for. [**With your help, we’ll do exactly that. Anything you can chip in today will help us lay the groundwork we’ll need to win next year’s midterms.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2025?refcode=em_20251105_5178_fr&amount=7&refcode2=5178_22930333_UJ4v-z&akid=5178%2E22930333%2EUJ4v-z) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2025?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20251105_5178_fr&refcode2=5178_22930333_UJ4v-z&akid=5178%2E22930333%2EUJ4v-z)**

New Jersey Counties like Passaic and Hudson that Trump won swung back to Democrats because of Mikie Sherrill’s authenticity and tenacity.

Abigail Spanberger made history and became the first-ever woman elected Governor of Virginia **by staying laser-focused on lowering costs for working families** , not _silly_ culture war issues stirred up by Fox News.

The bottom line: Authentic women, focused on economic opportunity, can win in Trump territory. **That’s _exactly_ what Reilly Neill is working to accomplish in 2026**.

**We have the momentum, but we can’t let up now**. Reilly Neill is working to be only the second woman ever to represent Montana, and she is reaching across the state to build a movement by Montanans, for Montanans.

name: ElectJon.com
subject: An opportunity
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
#

Last night, voters sent a message loud and clear.
Democrats won critical elections up and down the ballot across the nation — including historic gubernatorial races in Virginia and New Jersey. Here in Georgia, Democrats won not one, _but two_ , Republican-held Public Service Commissioner seats. It was the first time Democrats have won a non-federal statewide election in _almost 20 years._
And now, all eyes are on Jon Ossoff’s race in Georgia.
With the extra attention on our race, we have an opportunity to send a message to national Republicans, the media, and everyone looking to see if we have what it takes to win again.
_That means that these next few weeks are going to be some of the most important of the entire campaign._
Jon’s opponents and GOP-backed Super PACs have spent MILLIONS of dollars attacking Jon, and we have to respond and set the record straight.
We need to show that we’re ready — not just to rebuild the grassroots movement that won Georgia in 2021 — but to go _even bigger_ and fight back against anything the GOP throws at us.
And honestly, the only way that’s going to be possible is if you and lots of people like you decide to make it happen.
**So we are humbly asking:**
[**Are you in, Peter? Use this link to make your first contribution to Jon’s reelection campaign today.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-nov25.fw0_9v&refcode=em251105-1-subs-a&t=1&akid=1039%2E5691347%2E60pFWe)

clean_body: Last night, voters sent a message loud and clear. Democrats won critical elections up and down the ballot across the nation — including historic gubernatorial races in Virginia and New Jersey. Here in Georgia, Democrats won not one, but two , Republican-held Public Service Commissioner seats. It was the first time Democrats have won a non-federal statewide election in almost 20 years. And now, all eyes are on Jon Ossoff’s race in Georgia. With the extra attention on our race, we have an opportunity to send a message to national Republicans, the media, and everyone looking to see if we have what it takes to win again. That means that these next few weeks are going to be some of the most important of the entire campaign. Jon’s opponents and GOP-backed Super PACs have spent MILLIONS of dollars attacking Jon, and we have to respond and set the record straight. We need to show that we’re ready — not just to rebuild the grassroots movement that won Georgia in 2021 — but to go even bigger and fight back against anything the GOP throws at us. And honestly, the only way that’s going to be possible is if you and lots of people like you decide to make it happen. So we are humbly asking: If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | ** | | | | | | | Your donation powers the campaign that organizes in every corner of Georgia. Peter,

What a night!

From the governor’s mansion, to state legislatures, to city halls, Emerge alums won up and down the ballot across the country.

Our biggest wins came in Virginia. Emerge alum **Abigail Spanberger became the first woman Governor in the Commonwealth** —and she’s coming into office with a Democratic trifecta, thanks in large part to Emerge alums.

Emerge had 33 women on the ballot for the Virginia House of Delegates. **25 alums won their races, giving Democrats a supermajority in the House!** This means that fully one quarter of the Virginia House of Delegates will now be represented by Emerge alums.

**All 8 of our alums running in red-to-blue House of Delegates races won** , replacing Republicans with women of the New American Majority, Black and Brown, women of color, LGBTQ+ women, young and unmarried women.

_These victories didn’t happen by chance._ They’re the result of years of trainings, mentorship, and community that make Emerge the most effective pipeline for Democratic women leaders in the nation.

**That’s why first-time Emerge candidates are six times more likely to win their races.**

_And while the numbers are still coming in, one thing is clear: this movement is winning where it matters most._

Every single one of these wins is powered by people like you who believe that training, supporting, and electing Democratic women changes the future.

Now, we build on this momentum. The next class of Emerge women is already in training, ready to take on what comes next.

Peter, There’s no feeling quite as heavy as waking up the morning after a tough election when the results didn’t go our way. The disbelief. The disappointment. The sinking feeling that our country was headed in the wrong direction, and that the progress we’d fought so hard for was suddenly at risk. **One year from now, we’ll wake up after the 2026 midterm election with a chance to turn the page and chart a new course for our nation.** A chance to reject Donald Trump’s extreme agenda and the Republican majority fasttracking it in Congress. There’s a lot at stake regarding the future of our country. And winning won’t be easy. **We have one year to do the work. One year to organize. One year to remind the country what we stand for, and who we’re fighting for.** The deck may be stacked against us, but we can’t afford to wake up the day after next year’s election wishing we had done more. That’s why the work to take back the House — and restore a Congress that works for the American people — starts _right now._ Despite Republicans’ nationwide redistricting scheme, despite their efforts to silence voters, despite it all, I know we have what it takes to defend our democracy and take back the House. **[I have never backed down from a tough fight, and never will. If you’re not done fighting either, will you chip in $10 or any amount now to help us take back the House and take back our country, one year from now?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118733453/580715802/1751535416?amount=5&source=SBD_VF_EM_FR_110525_OneYear_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZDYzMzFjNy04Y2JhLWYwMTEtOGU2MS0wMDIyNDgyNGUwOGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4pY-D0gT3aKk8-A0nv0axoUSvD3eUT7qlWLAdBSixsU=)** | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now.

clean_body: We have one year to do the work. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | = Peter, There’s no feeling quite as heavy as waking up the morning after a tough election when the results didn’t go our way. The disbelief. The disappointment. The sinking feeling that our country was headed in the wrong direction, and that the progress we’d fought so hard for was suddenly at risk. One year from now, we’ll wake up after the 2026 midterm election with a chance to turn the page and chart a new course for our nation. A chance to reject Donald Trump’s extreme agenda and the Republican majority fasttracking it in Congress. There’s a lot at stake regarding the future of our country. And winning won’t be easy. We have one year to do the work. One year to organize. One year to remind the country what we stand for, and who we’re fighting for. The deck may be stacked against us, but we can’t afford to wake up the day after next year’s election wishing we had done more. That’s why the work to take back the House — and restore a Congress that works for the American people — starts right now. Despite Republicans’ nationwide redistricting scheme, despite their efforts to silence voters, despite it all, I know we have what it takes to defend our democracy and take back the House. = | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now. | = | = | = | = | = | = A lot will change between now and next year, but with your support, we’ll be ready. Valerie Foushee * | | | ---|---|--- = Valerie is fighting to protect access to health care, working families, and to reform our criminal justice system. But she needs your help to continue to do this great work in Congress. Your donation right now will help protect Democrat Valerie Foushee by giving us a jumpstart on the next cycle. | = = Paid for by Foushee for Congress Valerie Foushee for Congress PO Box 16446 Chapel Hill, NC 27516 United States If you need to you can unsubscribe, You can also =.

name: Ted Cruz
subject: Heartbreaking
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ted Cruz for Senate
body:

name: Chris Ahuja
subject: A referendum on oligarchy — and we’re winning.
body:
They think they can buy power. We just keep taking it.

| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=b88d4c7e03&e=5a6ee82cdb)
| | | | | |
Dear Peter,

**What we’re seeing across the country isn’t random — it’s a reckoning.** It’s a referendum on who gets to lead: the people, or the oligarchs.**In California, Prop 50 was a clear stand against power hoarding.** Voters said no to the insiders rewriting the rules for themselves — and yes to democracy that actually listens and represents them.**In New York City, Zohran Mamdani’s mayoral win wasn’t just a campaign victory.** It was an earthquake. Ordinary people organized, stood up to moneyed interests, and won.These fights share one truth: the era of oligarchy only continues if we let it. The people who buy influence are terrified of what happens when we all show up — because we keep proving money can’t outmatch momentum.[**Here’s what that means for us:**](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=bd389c500f&e=5a6ee82cdb)
* Power moves when people move.
* The insiders don’t decide — **we** do.
* Small donations, volunteer energy, and collective will are stronger than any check from the wealthy few.

[**Let’s keep the wave going. Chip in today to prove that people power still runs this country — not the billionaires trying to buy it.**](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=536a751d5f&e=5a6ee82cdb)
---
| [Donate](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=7381d9d58d&e=5a6ee82cdb)
---
| | **In solidarity,
**[****](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=b7f9e028bc&e=5a6ee82cdb)**Chris Ahuja****Democrat for Congress, CA-32**

[**P.S. Every grassroots win chips away at their illusion of control. Let’s make the next one ours.**](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=98218f12ab&e=5a6ee82cdb)

body:
Republicans Can Ignore the Warning Signs at Their Own Peril

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..yumycHaZ2OkoIKMh5HGuKY6l0AX4yr1Q23sEXxyyqAg?) for more
---
# [Democrats Dominate 2025 Elections](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=365201&post_id=178124266&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MTI0MjY2LCJpYXQiOjE3NjIzNzc3NDgsImV4cCI6MTc2NDk2OTc0OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM2NTIwMSIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.5Hh8IcjgcDFmWBb1bM2wspHhmpmIentf5enKxx-2EUM)
### Republicans Can Ignore the Warning Signs at Their Own Peril
| | [Joseph Bentivegna](https://substack.com/@jfbentivegna)
---
| Nov 5
---
| [](https://substack.com/@jfbentivegna)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=365201&post_id=178124266&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MTI0MjY2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2MjM3Nzc0OCwiZXhwIjoxNzY0OTY5NzQ4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzY1MjAxIiwic3ViIjoicmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.NVMxp147hD3eVo4oOvjvZIGT9tuouKmLwE01UeySPxk&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=365201&post_id=178124266&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MTI0MjY2LCJpYXQiOjE3NjIzNzc3NDgsImV4cCI6MTc2NDk2OTc0OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM2NTIwMSIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.5Hh8IcjgcDFmWBb1bM2wspHhmpmIentf5enKxx-2EUM&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/jfbentivegna/p/democrats-dominate-2025-elections?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

November 5, 2025
Odd-year elections don't involve congressional, senatorial or presidential candidates so they attract little attention. A few states and cities have elections, among them Virginia, New Jersey and New York City. In my home state of Connecticut, some towns have municipal elections.
Nonetheless, the tea leaves can be interpreted. The main results were that New Jersey and Virginia elected Democratic governors, New York City elected a self-described socialist, a California referendum championed by Governor Newsom allowed further gerrymandering, and Pennsylvania [retained three Democratic justices](https://substack.com/redirect/2d17b2fb-efcd-4282-97de-f4fb96435f20?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for their Supreme Court.
Republican spinmeisters are writing off these results as they occurred in Democratic states. But what these spinmeisters are failing to recognize is that none of these elections were even close. Both Democratic gubernatorial candidates won by comfortable double-digit margins. The new socialist mayor of New York City eclipsed 50% in a three-way race after the highest turn out in decades. California approved gerrymandering by a huge 63%. Pennsylvania retained Democratic judges by over 60%. In Connecticut, Republicans got slaughtered in a massacre that would embarrass Attila the Hun.

What does this mean? Here goes:

**President Trump is not popular.** While his presidency has accomplished a great deal - closing our borders, neutering Iran, lowering gas prices, making progress in the Mid East, bringing industry and investment to the United States etc. - he has not conquered the Big Enchilada: inflation. Housing prices and rents are out of control. The premiums for health insurance, car insurance and homeowner's insurance continue to skyrocket.

This is the main reason for Mamdani's mayoral victory in New York City. Young people who have never experienced the disaster of socialism are outraged because they are paying $5,000 a month to live in an ant colony while few make wages high enough to meet other daily escalating expenses comfortably.

And this is a national trend. As one young man stated in his viral video (and I**** paraphrase): "My grandfather and grandmother bought a house when they were my age and started having kids. I can't even afford an apartment let alone a girlfriend."
**The Democrats are going to have a huge fight between moderates and liberals.** In [2016](https://substack.com/redirect/67adc6db-728b-49b6-bfdd-425ddadec026?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [2020](https://substack.com/redirect/bde258a3-7c87-4964-b677-a2f4839baabd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), moderate Democrats shafted self-proclaimed socialist Bernie Sanders by rigging the delegate rules to deny him the nomination. The result: Hilliary Clinton lost in 2016, and the ineffective President Biden poisoned the well enough to allow a Trump victory in 2024.

Liberal Democrats are furious and are now screaming, "It's our turn." And they have a point. It was liberal Democrats who organized and marched in the No Kings protest. It was liberal Democrats who had gave moderate Democrats the spine to stand up to President Trump and shut down the government. And the result? Liberal Democrats showed up in droves to create the huge Democratic victories that just occurred. But moderate Democrats still believe a liberal presidential nominee in 2028 is unelectable. Furthermore, most billionaires are Democrats, not Republicans, and dislike liberal socialist schemes.

**California Governor Gavin Newsom is on the rise**. Democrats hope to overcome the slim Republican majority and retake the House in 2026. The problem is that Democrats have gerrymandered almost all the Blue States so that they cannot steal any more seats. For example, in Deep Blue New England, President Trump won 40% of the vote but there is not a single Republican Congressman. Similar situations exist in New York, Maryland and Illinois. Presently, California is well-gerrymandered with 43 Democratic and only 9 Republican seats. But with creative software, Republicans could lose 4 or 5 more seats. And Governor Newsom is successfully choreographing this.
**President Obama is not going away**. Most former presidents keep a low profile. Carter, Reagan, George H. W. Bush, Clinton, George W. Bush and even Joe Biden rarely make political statements. Not President Obama, mainly because his legacy can be described in two words: Donald Trump. Obama is determined to destroy President Trump's base, and he worked like a dog to help Kamala Harris and the Democratic candidates this cycle. He will do the same in 2026 and if he doesn't like any of the Presidential candidates in 2028, he may entice his wife Michelle to run.

**The Pennsylvania Supreme Court will allow the liberal interpretation of ballot harvesting rules.** Philadelphia Democrats harvest ballots with such efficacy that even a Bridgeport alderman would blush. But it was not enough to stop the Trump tsunami in 2024. But with some more creative voting rules, this crucial swing state could go Democratic in 2028 if the Pennsylvania Supreme Court allows it.
The problem with this analysis is that it merely points out that the Democratic states are becoming more Democratic. The same phenomenon is happening in Republican states, but this election cycle did not generate enough meaningful data in this area. But the 2026 election will do so. Stay tuned.

Liberal Democrats are furious and are now screaming, "It's our turn." And they have a point. It was liberal Democrats who organized and marched in the No Kings protest. It was liberal Democrats who had gave moderate Democrats the spine to stand up to President Trump and shut down the government. And the result? Liberal Democrats showed up in droves to create the huge Democratic victories that just occurred. But moderate Democrats still believe a liberal presidential nominee in 2028 is unelectable. Furthermore, most billionaires are Democrats, not Republicans, and dislike liberal socialist schemes. California Governor Gavin Newsom is on the rise. Democrats hope to overcome the slim Republican majority and retake the House in 2026. The problem is that Democrats have gerrymandered almost all the Blue States so that they cannot steal any more seats. For example, in Deep Blue New England, President Trump won 40% of the vote but there is not a single Republican Congressman. Similar situations exist in New York, Maryland and Illinois. Presently, California is well-gerrymandered with 43 Democratic and only 9 Republican seats. But with creative software, Republicans could lose 4 or 5 more seats. And Governor Newsom is successfully choreographing this. President Obama is not going away. Most former presidents keep a low profile. Carter, Reagan, George H. W. Bush, Clinton, George W. Bush and even Joe Biden rarely make political statements. Not President Obama, mainly because his legacy can be described in two words: Donald Trump. Obama is determined to destroy President Trump's base, and he worked like a dog to help Kamala Harris and the Democratic candidates this cycle. He will do the same in 2026 and if he doesn't like any of the Presidential candidates in 2028, he may entice his wife Michelle to run. The Pennsylvania Supreme Court will allow the liberal interpretation of ballot harvesting rules.** Philadelphia Democrats harvest ballots with such efficacy that even a Bridgeport alderman would blush. But it was not enough to stop the Trump tsunami in 2024. But with some more creative voting rules, this crucial swing state could go Democratic in 2028 if the Pennsylvania Supreme Court allows it. The problem with this analysis is that it merely points out that the Democratic states are becoming more Democratic. The same phenomenon is happening in Republican states, but this election cycle did not generate enough meaningful data in this area. But the 2026 election will do so. Stay tuned. name: Marsha Blackburn
subject: I owe you big time, Peter!
body:
I know my team and I have been reaching out a lot lately.

| | | | Peter, it’s Marsha Blackburn!
I know my team and I have been reaching out a lot lately, but your support truly means more than you know. To show just how much it means to me, I am coming just to you with an exciting opportunity today.
My team has been hard at work creating a brand-new merch item to celebrate our great nation, show our patriotism off with pride, stand strong for FAITH and FREEDOM, and show the Left that we are “ONE NATION UNDER GOD.”
[As a small token of my appreciation, I have put the very first one on hold for you in our Merch Store and attached your confirmation below:](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn._k09jk6-ocyybFgZnw)MERCH STORE ITEM HOLD CONFIRMATION
Hold Status: ConfirmedID Code: [dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.)-25-908[CLAIM YOUR EXCLUSIVE ITEM NOW >>

Your hold reservation expires at 11:59 PM tonight.
But, Peter, I wouldn’t wait too long to claim your exclusive item. We are going to open these up to the rest of our file very soon, and I have a feeling they will go quickly!
Thank you again for all of your support and for EVERYTHING you do!
\- Marsha Blackburn| | |

body:
Women leading, building, and fixing what’s broken
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=944%2E181023%2EdUuJk_)
What a nightfolks!
**2026 is the year women governors lead, build, and fix what’s broken. And it’s starting NOW.**
Abigail Spanberger in Virginia and Mikie Sherrill in New Jersey notched big victories last night. And right here in Detroit, Mary Sheffield was elected as the first woman mayor of the city.
These women didn’t just win, they _shattered barriers_ with a message of lowering costs and making life easier for the people they’re going to serve.
**That’s the message we’re running on — and that’s why we’re going to win big next year. Let’s do this:**
**[Will you rush a contribution to my campaign so we can carry this momentum and elect another Democratic woman to lead Michigan in 2026?](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q42025.hprbVa&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251105_PM2_V1_S1&t=2&akid=944%2E181023%2EdUuJk_)**

name: Heidi Hall for Congress
subject: Prop 50 just passed
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY HEIDI HALL FOR CONGRESS
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20251105_em_fr_b1_HH_BIG [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20251105_em_fr_b1_HH_BIG]
Friend,
Prop 50 passed, which means that California's 3rd Congressional District will now include parts of Sacramento and no longer represent some areas along the CA-NV border.
I want to take a second to make it very clear that:
While the lines of our district have changed, our goal has stayed exactly the same.
I joined this race months ago when I saw where our Republican opponent's inaction and toxic politics were leading us.
Friend, we deserve a leader who understands that 1️⃣ we need affordable healthcare, 2️⃣ we need to support working-class families, and that 3️⃣ we need to safeguard our public lands and protect our environment— not one who's only interested in enacting Trump's agenda and furthering his political career.
It's time to fight for change and bring our communities' voice to Congress, but to do that I need your help.
I'm not taking a single cent from corporate PACs or billionaires because I'm in this race to create real change, not to be a puppet for the status quo. Will you join me in unseating our opponent and flipping one of EMILYs List's key 2026 flip targets by donating what you can to our people-powered campaign today? Let's hit the ground running! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20251105_em_fr_b1_HH_BIG]
If you have saved your information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process instantly.DONATE $15 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/Hall-email?express_lane=true&amount=15&refcode=20251105_em_fr_b1_HH_BIG_]
DONATE $35 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/Hall-email?express_lane=true&amount=35&refcode=20251105_em_fr_b1_HH_BIG_]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/Hall-email?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=20251105_em_fr_b1_HH_BIG_]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/Hall-email?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=20251105_em_fr_b1_HH_BIG_]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/Hall-email?express_lane=true&amount=250&refcode=20251105_em_fr_b1_HH_BIG_]
Another amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20251105_em_fr_b1_HH_BIG_]
Thank you so much,
Heidi Hall
Heidi Hall has spent her three-decade public service career working with others to solve problems, protect our land and water, improve health, and build opportunities for others, all while raising two boys primarily as a single mom.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20251105_em_fr_b1_HH_BIG [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20251105_em_fr_b1_HH_BIG]
Raised by parents who had persisted to make a middle-class life for their kids in Contra Costa County while lifting up others, Heidi was taught to push through and overcome challenges rather than complain about them. She learned to stick up for the vulnerable, to respect working people, and to value equal opportunity and justice.
After graduating from Pomona College and earning graduate degrees from Columbia and Rutgers, Heidi went to work for the Rockefeller Foundation promoting reproductive care and then began her career at the Environmental Protection Agency, stopping the dumping of toxic waste and holding corporate polluters accountable.
Today, Heidi chairs the Nevada County Board of Supervisors, where she’s taken on homelessness, expanded broadband access, and worked to reduce crime and illegal cannabis growth. Heidi flipped her seat from red to blue – and now she’s ready to do it again in Congress to take on toxic politics in Washington and put people first.
Prefer to donate via mail? Address a check to:
Heidi Hall for Congress
PO Box 729
Grass Valley, CA 95945
SUPPORT HEIDI [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20251105_em_fr_b1_HH_BIG]
PAID FOR BY HEIDI HALL FOR CONGRESS

Heidi Hall for Congress
PO Box 729
Grass Valley, CA 95945
United States

If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/Qu-gopm63E63DwJRAnQQlw2?unsubscribedata=6FaLcinZ8PCWC9DSn8S835GtbAeb%2Fgn1kHi1bDgNRS1fczzW1RgqYYHbvU%2BMTCWgzPRvfitx5G%2BMrOWieeR99N%2FMqxkWria26aLi4IN1kVPHuyIx%2BLLIeTCpcY8kNj5rYnESDpOcOlyCghETXHOK7e%2FtahbR1gODYJEhts8MGfhOEfTrK%2FoGDtWys8cQ7uHOZ5m47ZalAH6MT2CB7LRJxQrFJCh3%2FYHp%2FNBRgjfkpIDWyeBGJ9vL7Fw9h6fDi1CZN7cNjqdPOEirjp1L5%2BmATNO7m7L7oINgOAtkI2wGP88WCfx%2Fpg9aeBFNPVl5YgF2OhlGhvki53gPYISauz7F5AskrDECunR1VVIO%2FIpwDeShB5kiZYb9GSI8Z3OorrqNZzVC4EuLxxA%2BUvdi4a%2BUpGA9tjnzK2z7r1Oy7yg%2B90nELlwAu37RvkSVvhBnp8rE .

name: Working Families Party
subject: 2026 starts now — join us tonight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St
body:
From New York to Virginia, working people just made history. Join us at 8 PM ET to chart the path ahead. | | | Peter,
Election Day is behind us — but the story isn’t over. Across the country, working people just made history. While some races are still too close to call, the Working Families Party elected working-class champions up and down the ballot — from city halls to statehouses to major mayoral races. **[Join us tonight at 8 PM ET for the Working Families Party’s Post-Election Mass Call — “2026 Starts Now.”](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdJxZAP5xLg1YZaKIinIFmIxwneR9YUH85oX6XyKoEwATL_hbR3LKU0fNB3u-IwAuIzSp9PhgL92ULM2HYdn4SEo2ax62eue0YtXPr8LI_V0N4So650g4nTtYWNLBr_O88jJPcV-XplZJ2xO8yJdEwRboYjdoiMCd7o0dByv6512mpEYqIjvqaNy098F9xVWUs3VOLNSDometLxEO7YDMV_aTZgJu6I_8vsha0AL49pTz3OSS0sI2SeCgFDLBEnLOBg/4lc/MRZJl5aLT3-JqxTjq-1t0A/h0/h001.SK5pFOATiIhJofklM_hir2tFZAkwbsfBI4tYwM1tBQ0)**
We’ll break down what we won, what we learned, and how we fight back heading into the 2026 midterms.
**[RSVP »](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdJxZAP5xLg1YZaKIinIFmIxwneR9YUH85oX6XyKoEwATL_hbR3LKU0fNB3u-IwAuIzSp9PhgL92ULM2HYdn4SEojdBOYkF0f-czDdK-yycPm0rFh_rxTANs-5eQeOmsfTocTYDgGj2NeuSnURaeuzr4iMDqu9YIggL2YxT9IakvRjtu-vBSBZvm_fYQ0qy3nu0m_NIKA_UM69s5OXHFxJMY9AQkG9CQdAfQYPqbVO9-Q0qUen4lcS6m21CRMzY2kmQ/4lc/MRZJl5aLT3-JqxTjq-1t0A/h1/h001.hcNOi1T2aHFEVOaGPbQ-whJS-MkesifhZnx26VXLX3Q)** Here’s what we’ve won so far:
* **In New York, Zohran Mamdani withstood $40 million in Super PAC spending to defeat former Governor Andrew Cuomo****— running on a message of affordability, economic fairness, and working-class power.** His victory drove record turnout and proved that people want a different kind of politics: one that prioritizes working people, not billionaires.
* **In Virginia, WFP-backed candidates helped expand the Democrats’ governing majority in the General Assembly — with Virginia becoming a Democratic trifecta.** That means we can restore voting rights, protect reproductive freedom, and deliver real economic relief for families across the Commonwealth.
* **In California, voters approved Proposition 50, a direct response to Trump’s MAGA redistricting power grab.** The measure restores fairness to the congressional maps by empowering California voters — not politicians — to approve emergency district lines through 2030. Prop 50 reaffirms California’s commitment to democracy, fairness, and resistance to election manipulation by Trump and his Republican allies.

Across the country:
* In Detroit, **Denzel McCampbell** and **Gabriela Santiago-Romero** both won their City Council races, proving that when we organize, we win.

* In Dayton, OH, **Shenise Turner-Sloss** upset an entrenched incumbent to become mayor.

* Despite billionaire Jeffrey Yass spending more than $2 million to upset statewide Pennsylvania Supreme Court judicial retention elections, the sitting justices were retained by historic margins.
* WFP-backed candidates flipped Republican-held offices like the Mayor of New Britain, CT (Bobby Sanchez after 15 years of Republican rule) and Erie County (won by Trump in 2024, defeating a Republican incumbent).

* WFP candidates also won in Buffalo, Syracuse, Albany, Hartford, Bridgeport, Georgia, and across Colorado — and more victories are still being called.

In city after city, WFP candidates beat big money and billionaire-backed Super PACs — and we did it by centering the basic needs of working people: lowering costs, defending reproductive freedom, protecting public schools, and ensuring that everyone has healthcare when they need it. **[Tonight at 8 PM ET, we’re coming together to celebrate these wins, unpack what we’ve learned, and chart the path forward.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdJxZAP5xLg1YZaKIinIFmIxwneR9YUH85oX6XyKoEwATL_hbR3LKU0fNB3u-IwAuIzSp9PhgL92ULM2HYdn4SEoNEuV6hyqUGPcxfXTwfet52weVwRRW6eJbU87pF0XsDBjm8IgNaZeSsujwOvIlsMiAAVyQqccsyaGdkm371oVMA8biLHtwP87bb3-tQGt_V2iAR-wlGwQ96-plNS4Hy3kVIrz5ZeFpzJXnlZo7sIYQAC2F9xdQAu6bl4LrDaCWGM/4lc/MRZJl5aLT3-JqxTjq-1t0A/h2/h001.CGkXk2JBnUj5xt6onOBGG-8evhUKLvSp3-5Gujai7zQ)** **[RSVP »](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdJxZAP5xLg1YZaKIinIFmIxwneR9YUH85oX6XyKoEwATL_hbR3LKU0fNB3u-IwAuIzSp9PhgL92ULM2HYdn4SEopKQPNkckR0ZnCkLnRJNV_e_n-PQjSWyik9fleg9fxpZQSzbqEudXetYxZ3eEs9gvWCVmvPLHV6SWqcF6DHahnMtufA5eUDSxbRFNT-4LfcpVzzfuFpmIhD9VQwFwsuqYmRkIcbM5SbPO6du5yGckJMCOiy9S8ct48UYsLW8uwOc/4lc/MRZJl5aLT3-JqxTjq-1t0A/h3/h001.Zket4ipIFnDlaDrf-pSVPIFsju7PnHkPeNGQslyabfM)** This moment demands bold leadership, clear-eyed strategy, and solidarity that doesn’t flinch in the face of power. That’s what the Working Families Party is built for — and that’s why we need you on this call. We’ll break down how we beat back Trump and the GOP’s agenda — from attacks on SNAP and healthcare to attempts to silence working people — and how we’re laying the groundwork for a working-class wave in the 2026 midterms. The truth is, our work didn’t end last night. It’s just beginning. Hope doesn’t come from waiting. clean_body: From New York to Virginia, working people just made history. Join us at 8 PM ET to chart the path ahead. | | | Peter, Election Day is behind us — but the story isn’t over. Across the country, working people just made history. While some races are still too close to call, the Working Families Party elected working-class champions up and down the ballot — from city halls to statehouses to major mayoral races. We’ll break down what we won, what we learned, and how we fight back heading into the 2026 midterms. Here’s what we’ve won so far: In New York, Zohran Mamdani withstood $40 million in Super PAC spending to defeat former Governor Andrew Cuomo**— running on a message of affordability, economic fairness, and working-class power. His victory drove record turnout and proved that people want a different kind of politics: one that prioritizes working people, not billionaires. In Virginia, WFP-backed candidates helped expand the Democrats’ governing majority in the General Assembly — with Virginia becoming a Democratic trifecta. That means we can restore voting rights, protect reproductive freedom, and deliver real economic relief for families across the Commonwealth. In California, voters approved Proposition 50, a direct response to Trump’s MAGA redistricting power grab. The measure restores fairness to the congressional maps by empowering California voters — not politicians — to approve emergency district lines through 2030. Prop 50 reaffirms California’s commitment to democracy, fairness, and resistance to election manipulation by Trump and his Republican allies. Across the country: In Detroit, Denzel McCampbell and Gabriela Santiago-Romero both won their City Council races, proving that when we organize, we win. In Dayton, OH, Shenise Turner-Sloss upset an entrenched incumbent to become mayor. Despite billionaire Jeffrey Yass spending more than $2 million to upset statewide Pennsylvania Supreme Court judicial retention elections, the sitting justices were retained by historic margins. WFP-backed candidates flipped Republican-held offices like the Mayor of New Britain, CT (Bobby Sanchez after 15 years of Republican rule) and Erie County (won by Trump in 2024, defeating a Republican incumbent). WFP candidates also won in Buffalo, Syracuse, Albany, Hartford, Bridgeport, Georgia, and across Colorado — and more victories are still being called. In city after city, WFP candidates beat big money and billionaire-backed Super PACs — and we did it by centering the basic needs of working people: lowering costs, defending reproductive freedom, protecting public schools, and ensuring that everyone has healthcare when they need it. This moment demands bold leadership, clear-eyed strategy, and solidarity that doesn’t flinch in the face of power. That’s what the Working Families Party is built for — and that’s why we need you on this call. We’ll break down how we beat back Trump and the GOP’s agenda — from attacks on SNAP and healthcare to attempts to silence working people — and how we’re laying the groundwork for a working-class wave in the 2026 midterms. The truth is, our work didn’t end last night. It’s just beginning. Hope doesn’t come from waiting. It comes from what we do next — together. In solidarity, Working Families Party Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. name: Save America Now PAC
subject: Recap of our October 24 Candidate Forum!
body:
Plus, happy 1 year anniversary to President Trump | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/saveamernow/introducing-save-america-now-pac-11041295?e=c50c08d277)
---
| | [ ](https://saveamernow.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bfee6d8faa8bd945842640631&id=507f94da81&e=c50c08d277)
---
|
---
| |
---
| |
# **HAPPY 1 YEAR ANNIVERSARY TO OUR PRESIDENT!**
---
| **Our October 24th Candidate Forum** was one of our**most successful PAC Luncheons yet! **Supporters packed the room for a powerful program that opened with a special **Charlie Kirk tribute** and continued with **presentations from five dynamic candidates**. Attendees also received practical insights on effective **ballot harvesting** and how these efforts can make a real impact in the upcoming election cycle.
---
|
---
---
| We also heard from CAGOP leadership, who delivered a clear and compelling message on the importance of voting **No on Prop 50**. Together, we mobilized supporters through coordinated phone banks and shared strategies to help ensure every vote counts. The momentum from this event has strengthened our mission and reinforced the urgency of the work ahead.
---
|
---
| Looking forward, mark your calendars for our next major event: the **Save America Now PAC Gala on June 26, 2026** , at the beautiful Westin Carlsbad. We’re thrilled to welcome **Emily Compagno** as one of our featured guests, with more exciting speakers and details to come. This will be an inspiring evening you won’t want to miss. Help keep these efforts moving forward. Your support is critical to protecting our values and shaping the future. Make a contribution today at **[www.saveamernow.com](https://saveamernow.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bfee6d8faa8bd945842640631&id=9d326a3fcd&e=c50c08d277)**

name: Sarah McBride
subject: Voters rejected the politics of division in favor of a politics of progress
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
We have to build now.
#
Last night, voters resoundingly rejected the politics of division in favor of a politics of progress.
From Virginia to New Jersey to New York City, those elected met voters where they are while staying true to their own values — all while maintaining a laser focus on making life more affordable.
**This is the way.**
It’s how we win back the House. It’s how we protect our freedoms, lower costs, and restore faith in our government. But we won’t get there by waiting until 2026.
We have to build now, fueled by a movement that believes in something better. [**If you’re ready to help keep my voice in Congress and power our work to reclaim the House next year, pitch in now. **](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20251105-1.xTCFeZ&refcode=em20251105-2473&t=1&akid=2473%2E21716%2Eide6pR)
**[Donate »](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20251105-1.xTCFeZ&refcode=em20251105-2473&t=20&akid=2473%2E21716%2Eide6pR)**
Thank you,
— Sarah
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-footer?refcode=em20251105-1&t=21&refcode2=2473_21716_ide6pR&akid=2473%2E21716%2Eide6pR)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/receive_fewer_emails/?t=22&akid=2473%2E21716%2Eide6pR)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/update_your_information/?t=23&akid=2473%2E21716%2Eide6pR)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=24&akid=2473%2E21716%2Eide6pR)

name: Team Crow
subject: A real fighting chance
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jason Crow for Congress
body:
Jason Crow For Colorado [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/88523/images/Screenshot%202025-08-21%20at%2010.50.41%E2%80%AFAM.png]
Folks,

This week, voters in New Jersey, Virginia, Georgia, and beyond turned out to the polls to cast their ballots in statewide and local elections.

Americans across the country showed overwhelmingly that Democrats who put service first win.

Servant leaders, like Jason’s friends Mikie Sherrill and Abigail Spanberger, helped build momentum. Now, it’s time to grow it as our work for the 2026 midterm elections ramps up.

Republicans hold a historically narrow majority in the House of Representatives, and we know they'll do and spend whatever it takes to hold on.

Tuesday’s wins prove that we have a real fighting chance at flipping the House blue, and Jason knows what it will take to deliver wins:

In 2018, he flipped Colorado's 6th congressional district blue for the first time since its creation, and he did it with the support of this grassroots team.

Now, Jason is working to bring the next generation of Democratic leaders to Congress. Will you make a donation of $10 or more to help us organize and build winning coalitions across the country? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-nov25]

name: Tobias Read
subject: Add your name: Call on Christy McCormick to resign
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251105em-mccormick-pet [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251105em-mccormick-pet]
Hi, it’s Oregon Secretary of State, Tobias Read.
I don’t say this lightly — what we’re witnessing is a betrayal of the public trust.
This week, Christy McCormick, a sitting federal election official, went on stage at a Trump-aligned event and claimed that Democrats “need the votes of illegal citizens” to win elections:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251101em-data-pet-rs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251101em-data-pet-rs]
That is a dangerous lie — one that undermines faith in our democracy and fans the flames of paranoia.
These are not the words of a nonpartisan public servant. They’re the words of a conspiracy theorist with a federal platform. And every day she remains in office, she damages public confidence in the elections that hold this country together.
That’s why I’m calling on Americans everywhere — Democrats, Republicans, and independents alike — to join me in demanding accountability.
Add your name to the petition calling for Commissioner McCormick’s resignation immediately: [https://secure.ngpvan.com/CL-OwXrTU0m-UjKeLBwZXg2]
ADD YOUR NAME [https://secure.ngpvan.com/CL-OwXrTU0m-UjKeLBwZXg2]
Simply put: It’s un-American to spread lies that sow fear and paranoia in the heart of our democracy.
Our founders built this nation on free and fair elections. Every generation since has been asked to defend democracy in its own way. For us, that means standing up to the lies that corrode trust in free and fair elections.
If we stay silent now, this kind of rot spreads — from the edges of the fever swamp straight into the heart of our government.
Commissioner McCormick’s false claims dishonor our democracy. She betrayed the trust of the people she was meant to serve. She must resign or be removed immediately. Let’s make our voices heard. Let’s prove that Americans still stand for truth, decency, and democracy.
Right now, add your name to demand Christy McCormick’s resignation: [https://secure.ngpvan.com/CL-OwXrTU0m-UjKeLBwZXg2]
Thank you for standing up for democracy.
Tobias Read
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251105em-mccormick-pet]
https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR [https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR] https://twitter.com/TobiasRead [https://twitter.com/TobiasRead] https://instagram.com/tobiasreador [https://instagram.com/tobiasreador]

Friends of Tobias Read (5208)
PO Box 42307
Portland, OR 97242
United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/IMahE5FQZEyEjGesjn_XSA2?unsubscribedata=160uaoXwD8kftQ7%2F9zsi4z0JtTNn%2BNjM5pClPP%2B8IltSTB0%2Fd4qatDnyWt7rfd6Z2mo1m7G%2FI%2BS0Wx5FF6mKqB6KK08Lwn493R48F7vmYQt8S8xMWeRD6Hi6ksCnVSUnVUzgQCF%2F5VkjlU82Ma6dmETjGhs7wADve%2BM8a4rYKiVQ%2BKyONMQnXoEKJPKxGDIj94mktnUVsTPAwLT8%2Fv30tG44Sdcarf%2FjU01o%2BbRLMVYABzB%2F8RkSKRG8SwP89XMgVTD8QhKQisTKxT9K1V65dwwnzYdAuRFEf2rIFWT0%2FsYKIZ6oxUgop1yfMsrVa%2FdO%2FKmIUkmyjXh1aw2fZ8zYPdM3hoW8ZkHg6WD%2FAaX6ECceId8DeEzpucJexKaad2tyswRTJFu8NjX%2BFqbtrlfWDzdoBoBUmc1xE2e9QV%2FvdXq1W1NYOAWvLyp36Z5yYj2r .
